    {
      "id": "2036389987822764175",
      "created_at": "2026-03-24 10:29:42+00:00",
      "text": "Factory reached out to me to sponsor my work, I had been shilling their product for nearly 6 months prior because I genuinely think it is the best muli-model harness experience rn. \n\nI get contacted a few times a week for sponsorships, and I turn everyone down because I can't in https://t.co/NAXnBOdPWO",
      "likes": 207,
      "retweets": 5,
      "replies": 14,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2036389987822764175",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2036387939673055639"
        }
      ]
    },

      "id": "2036377879341097132",
      "created_at": "2026-03-24 09:41:35+00:00",
      "text": "I did nothing to deserve this praise, passing it back to my teachers:\n\n- karpathy\n- juliarturc\n- steipete\n- TheAhmadOsman\n- badlogicgames\n- Teknium\n\nList goes on and on, I would follow these people. Pure alpha. https://t.co/vjMuCaPRcW",
      "likes": 378,
      "retweets": 9,
      "replies": 8,

    {
      "id": "2036200531111141714",
      "created_at": "2026-03-23 21:56:52+00:00",
      "text": "@jaro3th I live in Poland, I'm American",
      "likes": 22,
      "retweets": 0,

    {
      "id": "2036197441737777298",
      "created_at": "2026-03-23 21:44:35+00:00",
      "text": "This was thought to be impossible 1 year ago. https://t.co/n0WlenBlCV",
      "likes": 660,
      "retweets": 14,
      "replies": 18,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2036197441737777298",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2036154648348991985"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2036197251316392254",
      "created_at": "2026-03-23 21:43:50+00:00",
      "text": "RT @LLMJunky: Building with agents is highly addictive. I want to run a poll because I know i'm not the only one...\n\nAI Agents have affecte…",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2036197251316392254",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "retweeted",
          "id": "2036168373545890220"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2036197042045751563",
      "created_at": "2026-03-23 21:43:00+00:00",
      "text": "I have used every single orchestration tool out there. This is by far the best. Set an hour aside to try it with BYOK. \n\nIt takes some time to get the scope decided but I have walked away for days at a time. \n\nAutomate huge tedious tasks and take your family out or something https://t.co/HhXk9PVTAV",
      "likes": 1095,
      "retweets": 51,
      "replies": 45,
      "quotes": 3,
      "conversation_id": "2036197042045751563",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2036184745059688923"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2036196440976789856",
      "created_at": "2026-03-23 21:40:36+00:00",
      "text": "@nathanrs How much memory does your Mac have?",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2036141329172889826",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1274326667645853697",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2036196159417442549"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2036193349900677441",
      "created_at": "2026-03-23 21:28:19+00:00",
      "text": "@KatanaLarp @opencode https://t.co/c9fhW0vWaG\n\nDownload all your X data from settings, Google data from https://t.co/wLbH9NwYuL and extract all your AI sessions. \n\nThen feed it to Qwen via reap then use observations to decide what goes in vram &amp; you should get a nice speed boost especially for prefill",
      "likes": 139,
      "retweets": 7,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 2,
      "conversation_id": "2036191238072774764",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1757309709613166592",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2036191238072774764"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2036192504958132361",
      "created_at": "2026-03-23 21:24:58+00:00",
      "text": "Holy moly we doing it https://t.co/I7MMYSm8NT",
      "likes": 391,
      "retweets": 15,
      "replies": 14,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2036192504958132361",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [

          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2036191238072774764"
        }
      ]
    },

      "text": "@Dirty_Snob I’m not interested in that tbh, I don’t have the time",
      "likes": 6,

    {
      "id": "2036168753143046227",
      "created_at": "2026-03-23 19:50:35+00:00",
      "text": "Trying long form X for the first time.\n\nA deeper dive into why Open Source is so important for our industry, I also wanted to walk you through my setup right now.\n\nI am not an Academic researcher, I am a tinkerer who happens to have a genius researcher I can guide. https://t.co/mpdY5owgIX https://t.co/hDVtaprKd9",
      "likes": 608,
      "retweets": 22,
      "replies": 24,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2036168753143046227",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2035022588439581076"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2036166293271855523",
      "created_at": "2026-03-23 19:40:49+00:00",
      "text": "Here's a deeper dive into what I'm concerned about and why, you'll also see how I do my research, and the tools I am using\n\nWe should all think hard about our place in the economy, and the significance of these products both within our industry and out  \n\nhttps://t.co/zkl8MiLZpC",
      "likes": 38,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2036166293271855523",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2036143068022272078",
      "created_at": "2026-03-23 18:08:31+00:00",
      "text": "@petheth it's not a skill it's in Droid \n\nI think you can use this for free, but I'm not sure since I pay",
      "likes": 11,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2036136984771490202",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "109585282",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2036142765600338334"
        }
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "2036136984771490202",
      "created_at": "2026-03-23 17:44:21+00:00",
      "text": "How I am de-slopping my code-bases\n\nThis feature has made my codebases so much less messy /readiness-report in Droid compares your codebase against best standards and suggests ways to de-slop.\n\n2x speed. https://t.co/mUdhiF9NIk",
      "likes": 534,
      "retweets": 19,
      "replies": 20,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2036136984771490202",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2036127816970915892",
      "created_at": "2026-03-23 17:07:55+00:00",

      "conversation_id": "2036126761281266101",

      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2036043209151848737",
      "created_at": "2026-03-23 11:31:43+00:00",
      "text": "This is for all the frontend/fullstack devs, it'll make your life easier. \n\nDo you want GPT-5.4 &amp; mini to make better UIs?\n\nNo amount of prompting is going to fix the issue, the model simply is not good enough at frontend design on it's own. I love it, just not fro UI. \n\n1. Go on https://t.co/WtykVTejHr",
      "likes": 2500,
      "retweets": 132,
      "replies": 67,
      "quotes": 7,
      "conversation_id": "2036043209151848737",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },

    {
      "id": "2035680567274975634",
      "created_at": "2026-03-22 11:30:43+00:00",
      "text": "In 72 hours I got over 100k of value\n\n1. Lambda gave me 5000$ credits in compute\n\n2. Nvidia offered me 8x H100s on the cloud (20$/h) idk for how long but assuming 2 weeks that'd be 5000$~\n\n3. TNG technology offered me 2 weeks of B200s which is something like 12000$ in compute https://t.co/tSZhE8KLRv",
      "likes": 4136,
      "retweets": 274,
      "replies": 222,
      "quotes": 47,
      "conversation_id": "2035680567274975634",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },

      "id": "2035662835817091231",
      "created_at": "2026-03-22 10:20:15+00:00",
      "text": "@vpavlin Strix is good for prefill (prompt processing) and works well with Mixture of Expert llms. It has 128GB which is good enough for models up to 120B \n\nThe memory is quite limiting and the token generation speeds are pretty bad. If you have a Macbook or Mac studio you can do prefill",
      "likes": 44,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 5,
      "quotes": 0,

      "created_at": "2026-03-21 23:27:12+00:00",

    {
      "id": "2035491461701984577",
      "created_at": "2026-03-21 22:59:16+00:00",
      "text": "Here's how I learn new information. \n\nIt costs me a few cents to learn high tailored information around my preferences. An infinitely patient, highly intelligent teacher that can sometimes be wrong, but is mostly helpful. \n\nIf you ever felt like you were let down by school, try https://t.co/Hdgiyu7dRc",
      "likes": 1428,
      "retweets": 50,
      "replies": 20,
      "quotes": 4,
      "conversation_id": "2035491461701984577",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2035483760347447539",
      "created_at": "2026-03-21 22:28:40+00:00",
      "text": "Autoresearch anything you can boil down to objective verifiable metrics https://t.co/QM2MAHGiCC",
      "likes": 223,
      "retweets": 9,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2035483760347447539",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,

      "text": "@XyberRun For sure not, I’m actually working with someone on just this",

    {
      "id": "2035410267283038485",
      "created_at": "2026-03-21 17:36:38+00:00",
      "text": "@XyberRun That’s a tiny model man. You’ll need the 27B for a good experience. Try a quantized version for the 9B",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2034435534764818874",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "2020688235572477952",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2035409560102384001"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2035410012525195365",
      "created_at": "2026-03-21 17:35:37+00:00",
      "text": "This guys deserves all the follows. He’s a smart version of me. \n\nHe got Qwen3.5-27B running in the browser. Stuff is crazy https://t.co/5Qvva55zEP",
      "likes": 2644,
      "retweets": 111,
      "replies": 22,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2035410012525195365",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2035409335266726308"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2035405762361503818",
      "created_at": "2026-03-21 17:18:44+00:00",

    {
      "id": "2035402090848145766",
      "created_at": "2026-03-21 17:04:09+00:00",
      "text": "@hbfreed Oh for sure they used much more than 540B but yes I’m using chinchilla here. \n\n(This sounds so goofy) \n\nI feel tens of trillions for 27B makes no sense",

    {
      "id": "2035400871652004213",
      "created_at": "2026-03-21 16:59:18+00:00",
      "text": "@TomasMann1878 @foley2k2 Same model BF16 it was qwen3-30b at the time",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2035381466205409762",

    {
      "id": "2035400360995553561",
      "created_at": "2026-03-21 16:57:16+00:00",
      "text": "@foley2k2 I tried this! But I didn’t have enough time with GPUs to see it through to a production ready state. Distillation 100% improved performance DRASTICALLY",
      "likes": 8,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2035381466205409762",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "352738653",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2035384655579349137"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2035400142682038430",
      "created_at": "2026-03-21 16:56:24+00:00",
      "text": "A 27B model is #2 on pinch-bench \n\nYou’d need 150,000$ in GPU hours to train this from scratch (base + post training) \n\nBasically 1-2 weeks over 256 H100s \n\nThat is not unreasonable, you’d need 540B tokens for pre-training and a bit more for post training. \n\nNone of this is crazy https://t.co/RdFQj89j8c",
      "likes": 280,
      "retweets": 12,
      "replies": 12,
      "quotes": 2,
      "conversation_id": "2035400142682038430",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2035386651430588429"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2035397606826705214",
      "created_at": "2026-03-21 16:46:19+00:00",

      "id": "2035308985247543328",
      "created_at": "2026-03-21 10:54:10+00:00",
      "text": "@iruletheworldmo I just don’t think it needs to be that expensive. \n\nMiniMax is a 230B param model that has 200 million monthly active users, it surely requires at least hundreds of GPUs to train, and at least 200gb VRAM to inference but people do want to use it. \n\nQwen3.5-27B is even smaller and",
      "likes": 39,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2035302645028540453",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1565089861245313027",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2035307601676693580"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2035305840480063914",
      "created_at": "2026-03-21 10:41:41+00:00",
      "text": "@iruletheworldmo It would be good to hear from critics something constructive (:",
      "likes": 24,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2035302645028540453",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1565089861245313027",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2035305082221220298"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2035305652050870348",
      "created_at": "2026-03-21 10:40:56+00:00",
      "text": "@iruletheworldmo One more note, sure Bitcoin has scaled to a point where it’s not possible for individual miners to make any profit. \n\nBut now we have hundreds of alternative blockchains which allow anyone to access financial services. \n\nYes they’re full of scams, yes they’re all flawed but at",
      "likes": 3,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2035302645028540453",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1565089861245313027",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2035304086015283453"
        }
      ]
    },
    {

      "id": "2035303279542841616",
      "created_at": "2026-03-21 10:31:30+00:00",
      "text": "@iruletheworldmo Yes. \n\n- Bitcoin\n- Linux \n- OBS \n\nI can name thousands of winning open source solutions that you are using to interact with the internet. \n\nOpen source requires donations because it is fundamentally unprofitable, this is not about profit.",
      "likes": 593,
      "retweets": 13,
      "replies": 12,

      "created_at": "2026-03-20 23:35:55+00:00",
      "text": "@nvidia The people want affordable, open source AI. Please help me make it happen. You have the power to move the needle more than thousands of donations.",

    {
      "id": "2035138292602044542",
      "created_at": "2026-03-20 23:35:54+00:00",
      "text": "Man, what the hell. That's a few years salary for most of the world donated in under 24 hours. \n\nI promise I will do everything in my power to make this worth it for all of you. https://t.co/AMaSGOfyoS",
      "likes": 2493,
      "retweets": 83,
      "replies": 72,
      "quotes": 7,
      "conversation_id": "2035138292602044542",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,

    },

      "created_at": "2026-03-20 18:41:03+00:00",
      "text": "I'm not the only one doing this. \n\n- karpathy\n\nbest thought leader, best person to learn from imo.       "id": "2035045024228900957",
      "created_at": "2026-03-20 17:25:17+00:00",
      "text": "@caretak8r 0xSero on discord, make a group add me as a co-owner and we can make it happen",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,

    {
      "id": "2035043761143570807",
      "created_at": "2026-03-20 17:20:16+00:00",
      "text": "Nemotron REAPs have been published, they will be updated for higher quality but I wanted to make sure they're out today. \n\nINT4 quantisation soon, we will have Nemotron-Super's weights in 60GB of VRAM. \n\nhttps://t.co/g84VYMZn5S\n\n2x the speed with expert-swap on for cpu offload https://t.co/cehzYToVPN https://t.co/hDVtaprKd9",
      "likes": 103,
      "retweets": 9,
      "replies": 7,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2035043761143570807",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2035022588439581076"
        }
      ]
    },
    {

      "id": "2034974693921370112",
      "created_at": "2026-03-20 12:45:49+00:00",
      "text": "\"If you hold a gun and I hold a gun, we can talk about the law. If you hold a knife and I hold a knife, we can talk about rules. If you come empty-handed, and I come empty-handed, we can talk about reason. But if you hold a gun and I only have a knife, then the truth lies in your https://t.co/On0B9zlTfv",

    {
      "id": "2034935249210056857",
      "created_at": "2026-03-20 10:09:05+00:00",
      "text": "@AwareOfFreedom I have an immense respect for businesses who lose on revenue to support my mission. So I have no complaints. \n\nDoing something like this requires coordination and kindness from many people. \n\nGPUs are expensive, cheap ones are very finnicky I did publish models compresses on my",
      "likes": 6,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2034905871960961279",

      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [

          "id": "2034908957974708444"
        }
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "2034933784529781052",
      "created_at": "2026-03-20 10:03:16+00:00",
      "text": "@Teknium I have a home network of:\n\n1. Mac mini for claw &amp; Hermes \n2. MacBook as my control center \n3. 8x 3090 node for ML \n4. Phone for X \n\nI believe we should stop worrying about privacy and security and release the floodgates (within reason)",
      "likes": 18,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2034662261059629243",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1365020011123773442",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [

          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2034662261059629243"
        }
      ]
    },

      "created_at": "2026-03-19 22:41:12+00:00",
      "text": "Crowd-funding site is up:\n\nhttps://t.co/azdSNAbzd1\n\nI am going to be publishing REAPs and quantisations of \nNemotron-3-Super-120B over the next 48 hours, REAPs are uploading.\n\n50% REAP + 8bit + 4bit autorounds\n25% REAP + 8bit + 4bit autorounds\n\nhttps://t.co/g84VYMZn5S https://t.co/Drb7YzBJ9c",
      "likes": 78,
      "retweets": 12,
      "replies": 4,
      "quotes": 0,

    },

      "created_at": "2026-03-19 20:07:48+00:00",
      "text": "@Ex0byt @sudoingX  will be among those with open access. This will be pooled with my 3090s.\n\nI am e-begging, but I will make it up to you.",

    {
      "id": "2034723529917137197",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 20:07:47+00:00",
      "text": "GLM-5-Turbo the goat, great in Droid\n\nBuilt me a full-stack fundraiser site with Convex. I am crowdfunding for gpus to help with my research.\n\nAll funds go to GPUs which publish compressions 24/7 by end of year. I will also open them to researchers on X\n\nhttps://t.co/iZ6MOwmAyJ https://t.co/Yyu56ZS5gk",
      "likes": 184,
      "retweets": 14,
      "replies": 11,
      "quotes": 0,

      "id": "2034714278477848614",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 19:31:01+00:00",
      "text": "@0xWes_ @KinDanil13320 Yes @EnoReyes \n\nThis is an upstream bug with Ink as far as I understand. I have been complaining about it for weeks. Try the Droid ACP. \n\nThese days I use Droid in Cmux which doesn't suffer from the flicker issue as much.",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,

    {
      "id": "2034706680462618687",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 19:00:50+00:00",
      "text": "I had to open this abomination today, holy fuck. How can anyone use this????? https://t.co/zN4CvCxTnl",
      "likes": 957,
      "retweets": 8,
      "replies": 225,
      "quotes": 20,
      "conversation_id": "2034706680462618687",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2034706049131794767",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 18:58:19+00:00",
      "text": "The reason I mention this is because we are heading into a world where you make no decisions. \n\nYou experience what others want you to, no thinking. No choosing, just clicking what they think you should see.",
      "likes": 253,
      "retweets": 9,
      "replies": 10,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2034695293698986029",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2034695293698986029"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2034703201232916768",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 18:47:00+00:00",
      "text": "@jordanschenck I find it hard to see people as bad to be honest. I think we're all just people and I would probably do the same in their shoes. \n\nI need open intelligence, this is not slowing down. People that say these models aren't actually intelligent are being delusional. \n\nYou can destroy",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2034688241316557094",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1319324853510418432",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2034702262812254402"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2034701534533939677",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 18:40:23+00:00",
      "text": "We will make frontier intelligence run on 1000$ of hardware by the end of the year. https://t.co/ZiaswUoC71",

    {
      "id": "2034699495196279172",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 18:32:17+00:00",
      "text": "I'm a high school dropout. Functionally stupid.",

    {
      "id": "2034699235648561608",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 18:31:15+00:00",
      "text": "@jordanschenck I am seriously considering dedicating the next decade to decentralised training https://t.co/c9fhW0vWaG \n\nI agree with everything you said, you should read about AI search optimisation.",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2034688241316557094",

    {
      "id": "2034698941829169513",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 18:30:05+00:00",
      "text": "Reading ST-MoE: https://t.co/ueFtJJhNND\n\nAnyway, I am so close to completely dumping my computer for this app. They need to add a browser and file editor to it and we're set. https://t.co/Hwc4eXXLkR",
      "likes": 28,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2034698941829169513",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2034697336832540908",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 18:23:42+00:00",
      "text": "@jordanschenck I respect their right as a business to do that but I morally disagree with it for some reason. Could be irrational.",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2034688241316557094",

    {
      "id": "2034696774275801401",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 18:21:28+00:00",
      "text": "My point https://t.co/j9oJ9KzOYp https://t.co/LUt7uGA2ca",
      "likes": 42,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2034696774275801401",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,

    {
      "id": "2034695293698986029",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 18:15:35+00:00",
      "text": "First they removed dislikes from YouTube, now they removed Likes. No more public signal on what matters.\n\nMaking another braindead prediction, in 12 months we won't see views anymore. \n\nIn 24 months we won't subscriber counts anymore. https://t.co/J16GiH4St4",
      "likes": 1006,
      "retweets": 38,
      "replies": 86,
      "quotes": 4,
      "conversation_id": "2034695293698986029",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2034689479982621177",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 17:52:29+00:00",
      "text": "@DDevyy77003 @Ex0byt Sorry 192gb of vram~ for 7 tokens/s and 30 tokens/s\n\nfor him I think he's getting 1, basically probably 5-10 is the upper limit for 25gb\n\n2GB vram maybe maybe 24-32B param model",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2034682077937815848",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1695530610620563456",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2034688241316557094",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 17:47:34+00:00",
      "text": "Composer-2 model by Cursor just dropped, I'm excited to try it. Really enjoyed using their browser control. \n\nWhen API? https://t.co/8VFt3ydW0D https://t.co/eNIvOL04lS",
      "likes": 49,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 6,
      "quotes": 0,

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2034686330035118501",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 17:39:58+00:00",

      "created_at": "2026-03-19 17:24:14+00:00",

    {
      "id": "2034675550808133892",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 16:57:08+00:00",
      "text": "I touch grass with my son everyday, it’s medicine for the soul. \n\nSpend time with your family https://t.co/Q7QRkJB9kl",

    {
      "id": "2034644428518777181",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 14:53:28+00:00",
      "text": "@ChamalkaAI Queue let's you chain prompts so the model doesn't seem them until it's completely done with it's turn. \n\nSteer interrupts the model",
      "likes": 3,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2034399006290313542",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1850548069730799616",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2034644253532442941"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2034643822630584477",

    {
      "id": "2034636765797982284",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 14:23:01+00:00",
      "text": "I find it hard to feel anything but love for my fellow humans, there's something godly in people. \n\nThe amount of support and generosity I have received even when I didn't deserve it is incredibly comforting.",
      "likes": 45,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2034636765797982284",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {

    {
      "id": "2034627232937673179",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 13:45:08+00:00",
      "text": "@shepherdwitty Not sure what you mean",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,

    {
      "id": "2034624987173142539",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 13:36:13+00:00",
      "text": "First on my list (already started) Nvidia's Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B\n\nI am using 2 years of my AI sessions as calibration data (https://t.co/c9fhW0vWaG) \n\nI'll be publishing a set of REAPs + quantisations:\n\n- 20%\n- 30%\n- 40%\n- 50% \n\nNemotron on 60GB of VRAM \n\nJensen pls pewter https://t.co/FS3hSISYv7 https://t.co/KDuP0Pv31A",
      "likes": 58,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 6,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2034624987173142539",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2034623794954825993"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2034623794954825993",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 13:31:28+00:00",
      "text": "Exploding with excitement. https://t.co/Tj7PHEEN1H https://t.co/W1sJ3nZ2mp",
      "likes": 146,
      "retweets": 3,
      "replies": 11,
      "quotes": 4,
      "conversation_id": "2034623794954825993",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2034616136008888373"
        }
      ]
    },
    {

    {
      "id": "2034618689790640296",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 13:11:11+00:00",
      "text": "@aivrar I can share some credits with you if you send a proposal.",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2034617924204237011",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 13:08:09+00:00",
      "text": "Still I'm making a fundraiser, I need physical hardware so I can be running shit 24/7/365 \n\nIt is now my life mission to get this intelligence embedded into everything.\n\nRegardless, I am so grateful to Lambda for this opportunity.",
      "likes": 25,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2034616136008888373",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2034616136008888373"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2034616730245316647",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 13:03:24+00:00",
      "text": "New Kimi model coming soon? \n\nInference seems to slowdown whenever the next gen is being trained (: https://t.co/CLjzxMfQxP",
      "likes": 307,
      "retweets": 3,
      "replies": 16,
      "quotes": 3,
      "conversation_id": "2034616730245316647",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2034616136008888373",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 13:01:02+00:00",
      "text": "I am so happy to announce Lambda has sponsored 5000$ in compute credits! \n\nSo many compressions to come. https://t.co/V3eoBxtJId",
      "likes": 169,
      "retweets": 6,
      "replies": 15,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2034616136008888373",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2034435534764818874"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2034612688383324616",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 12:47:20+00:00",
      "text": "I didn't make the rules. https://t.co/lqAaX3hUm9",
      "likes": 32,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2034612688383324616",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2034605119698829387",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 12:17:16+00:00",
      "text": "@Olli757 Mainly is just an obsession at this point, I mostly use Codex, but I do believe that we need local AI. \n\nThis tech is very important and we need to be prepared for rising prices.",
      "likes": 148,
      "retweets": 3,
      "replies": 6,
      "quotes": 2,
      "conversation_id": "2034435534764818874",

          "type": "replied_to",

        }
      ]

      "id": "2034604824331726880",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 12:16:05+00:00",

    {
      "id": "2034599896288755845",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 11:56:30+00:00",
      "text": "Sitegeist to set things up, Codex to execute. \n\nAll wired up to hotkeys, you can literally do anything. https://t.co/7A5hsJhzoK",

    {
      "id": "2034574246656045442",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 10:14:35+00:00",
      "text": "@_thomasip I downgraded from 20x max to 5x max and will be cancelling my sub to get 2x GPT Pro. \n\nUnless you do frontend I see no purpose for Claude, even on frontend Kimi is better. \n\nIf you must the 40$ a month copilot plan gives you absurd amounts of usage",
      "likes": 5,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2034548801952719189",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "2494965768",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2034573539265728536"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2034573219923955749",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 10:10:30+00:00",
      "text": "@sujshr same. 4o for the autists.",
      "likes": 9,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2034554652377690527",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1622774095762051072",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2034573116568137988"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2034572515771568467",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 10:07:42+00:00",
      "text": "@_thomasip 100% Worth the 200$",
      "likes": 3,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2034548801952719189",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "2494965768",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2034570393571168567"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2034572387845304335",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 10:07:12+00:00",
      "text": "If you feel bad for being \"addicted to AI\", you shouldn't. \n\nIt's dumb, it can be malicious, it can corrode your intelligence, it can mislead you, it can make you psychotic. \n\nThat doesn't mean it isn't transformative.\n\nYou can be everyone you want to be, you have a way to extend https://t.co/uhdV4xnpHE",
      "likes": 48,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 5,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2034572387845304335",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2030305649586778604"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2034569939806249026",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 09:57:28+00:00",
      "text": "This is exactly what we need, lower the bar to entry so that people can do their own science. \n\nHow much more can we advance if anybody with a question can make it into something verifiable and give it to a fleet of agents to crunch. \n\nTruly transformative. https://t.co/CO0Rma3EiL",
      "likes": 130,
      "retweets": 5,
      "replies": 5,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2034569939806249026",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2034353876753592372"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2034567945607590116",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 09:49:33+00:00",
      "text": "@Alex_Car12 @TheAhmadOsman exllamav3 does this best. It is possible but you lose a ton of speed since you need to do Pipeline parallelism and you'd have 2 different generations of hardware (Ampere and Ada)",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2034558877849555443",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "848807305093283842",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2034566661823476207"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2034564725342630095",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 09:36:45+00:00",
      "text": "@aymuosk It depends on a lot of factors, but yes this was for a 12h run",
      "likes": 4,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2034554652377690527",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1316257838587551744",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2034564069944897850"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2034561793763226070",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 09:25:06+00:00",
      "text": "@pierrelezan Yes, @Ex0byt is working on this.",

    {
      "id": "2034561651433738636",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 09:24:32+00:00",
      "text": "@Alex_Car12 @TheAhmadOsman If I had the chance to start over I'd get the modded GPUs.",
      "likes": 12,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2034558877849555443",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "848807305093283842",

        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2034558877849555443"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2034558374625288211",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 09:11:31+00:00",
      "text": "@QXsToo @elonmusk @X X has gotten objectively better in many ways. Mainly over the last year I have been getting more of what I want. \n\nI remember 8 years ago it was overrun by politics, and up till last year everytime I opened the app I’d see a video of someone getting murked within 2 minutes",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2034473452481236998",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "2430581473",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2034473452481236998"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2034554652377690527",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 08:56:43+00:00",
      "text": "Don’t use gpt-5.4-xhigh \n\nI spent 15% of my weekly usage in 12 hours  on one auto research session. \n\nIt does seem much more accurate on research tasks but it’s unsustainable if you don’t want to be spending 1k+ a month on subs https://t.co/J9aGaLEiaH",
      "likes": 269,
      "retweets": 6,
      "replies": 60,
      "quotes": 3,
      "conversation_id": "2034554652377690527",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2034548801952719189",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 08:33:29+00:00",
      "text": "Can OpenAI make a 500$/mo Codex sub?\n\nI really don’t want to be dragging around 2-3 subscriptions. \n\nThis also begs the question: where are these subscriptions going? \n\nWe already changed the standard entry level sub for 10$ -&gt; 20$ -&gt; 200$ \n\nWhat’s the ceiling going to be? https://t.co/fCLD0CtR5j",
      "likes": 45,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 18,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2034548801952719189",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2034545256281022682",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 08:19:23+00:00",
      "text": "@badlogicgames Use way back machine",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2034542876663636373",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "189876762",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2034542876663636373"
        }
      ]
    },
    {

    {

      "created_at": "2026-03-19 07:18:26+00:00",
      "text": "@keennay 8x RTX 3090s",
      "likes": 56,

    {
      "id": "2034521788009341035",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 06:46:08+00:00",
      "text": "@g_keffa I know right",
      "likes": 18,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2034435534764818874",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1520306287573090304",
      "is_reply": true,

        {
          "type": "replied_to",

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2034435534764818874",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 01:03:24+00:00",
      "text": "Putting out a wish to the universe. \n\nI need more compute, if I can get more I will make sure every machine from a small phone to a bootstrapped RTX 3090 node can run frontier intelligence fast with minimal intelligence loss.\n\nI have hit page 2 of huggingface, released 3 model https://t.co/9n68iTQ6fa https://t.co/e2cXWB58dl",
      "likes": 4085,
      "retweets": 487,
      "replies": 179,
      "quotes": 57,
      "conversation_id": "2034435534764818874",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2034277477485334617"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2034426323888660821",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 00:26:48+00:00",
      "text": "Use an agent to review what you as a person do, how do you code? What patterns do you use? Where do you fall short. \n\nIt's all in git, surprisingly useful. 4X speed https://t.co/XdSFzCr7gx",
      "likes": 81,
      "retweets": 4,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2034426323888660821",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2034423285924921828",
      "created_at": "2026-03-19 00:14:43+00:00",
      "text": "@MgkMshrmBrkfst It's a general framework, shouldn't be confined to auto-research.",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2034399006290313542",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1703237059",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2034422570192134631"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2034418525050200492",
      "created_at": "2026-03-18 23:55:48+00:00",
      "text": "42B tokens used in the last 6 months, 10B of them in the last week. This is so fun (: https://t.co/ZXRhcsmZks",
      "likes": 36,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 4,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2034418525050200492",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2034412449173045430",
      "created_at": "2026-03-18 23:31:40+00:00",
      "text": "I had to rewrite my Codex automation: \n\n\"Look in ~/.claude ~/.codex ~/.factory ~/.kimi-cli ~/.opencode ~/.pi for all my token usage previous runs, show heatmaps, graphs and charts, show which reaps got most attention.\n\nSummarize what was committed, via git history.\" https://t.co/06nY0UB8Dy",
      "likes": 39,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2034412449173045430",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2034411242979459513",

      "created_at": "2026-03-18 22:52:28+00:00",
      "text": "@TimRamboReal I don't think that's true. TBH, our military is very good at hardware but I'm not sure they're so far ahead on AI",

    {
      "id": "2034399006290313542",
      "created_at": "2026-03-18 22:38:15+00:00",
      "text": "It's been running for 6 hours on my first prompt. I have 20+ steering prompts, 25% of them highly detailed. \n\nMy GPUs have been at 100% utilisation this entire time, my room is 28c boiling hot. \n\nI can't describe how crazy it is that this is possible, I am a solo knucklehead https://t.co/eTObTikPGE",
      "likes": 314,
      "retweets": 10,
      "replies": 29,
      "quotes": 2,
      "conversation_id": "2034399006290313542",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2034395497104839159",
      "created_at": "2026-03-18 22:24:18+00:00",
      "text": "I think we can get very big models on smaller GPUs pretty soon. \n\nhttps://t.co/ZZSXZXxgor",
      "likes": 210,
      "retweets": 13,
      "replies": 7,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2034395497104839159",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [

        }
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "2034266909105930732",
      "created_at": "2026-03-18 13:53:20+00:00",
      "text": "@reach_vb Done",

    {
      "id": "2034260962757279765",
      "created_at": "2026-03-18 13:29:42+00:00",
      "text": "If you have some GPUs + system RAM here's something to give a clanker that'll help you run very large models with CPU offload about 20% faster than default. \n\nI will get this to 5x faster, I need to get a router trained. The idea is proven. Kimi at home.\n\nhttps://t.co/PTvyQ45ua4 https://t.co/hx0xVpSBH5",
      "likes": 150,
      "retweets": 6,
      "replies": 10,
      "quotes": 3,
      "conversation_id": "2034260962757279765",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2034259538799370451",
      "created_at": "2026-03-18 13:24:03+00:00",
      "text": "@RpxDeveloper I mean if you're using claude code it's not more expensive to do so, you just get a dumber model past 200k context. \n\nBut yeah, I don't see a use for anything above 400k",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2034251507483168932",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1453618622207864836",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2034258382748549279"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2034257199904493821",
      "created_at": "2026-03-18 13:14:45+00:00",
      "text": "@SIGKITTEN I love parallel clankers too, but some code needs to be reviewed and I'd rather do it in the same place as I clank. \n\nCursor I'd argue is more of an IDE, I don't mind this being read only. I don't want the code completely abstracted away from me tbh. \n\nToo many times I get",
      "likes": 3,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2034255835086324191",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "91588455",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2034256708311150678"
        }
      ]
    },
    {

    {
      "id": "2034255835086324191",
      "created_at": "2026-03-18 13:09:20+00:00",
      "text": "All Codex app needs to do:\n\n1. Add file explorer and file preview \n2. Remove Jason from the subagent registry\n3. That's it done. https://t.co/Z5sbHjPXIm",

      "retweets": 0,

      "quotes": 3,

      "created_at": "2026-03-18 12:52:48+00:00",
      "text": "@1__________l1l_ The OpenCode desktop app is better than the Factory (Droid desktop app) but the CLI is not. I get better results out of Droid.",

    {
      "id": "2034251507483168932",
      "created_at": "2026-03-18 12:52:08+00:00",
      "text": "Some things I learned this week:\n\n1. GPT-5.4/Codex at more than 256k max tokens doesn't help and is too expensive (that's why I ran out of usage btw).  \n\nThe models still don't do great past 200k context and I can get basically infinite context using subagents anyway. \n\n2. To be https://t.co/mZ0LQndQjI",
      "likes": 155,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 23,
      "quotes": 3,
      "conversation_id": "2034251507483168932",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2034237352030113922"
        }
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "2034243822566187014",
      "created_at": "2026-03-18 12:21:36+00:00",
      "text": "OpenCode Desktop app updates worth checking out. \n\n1. Now we have Queue mode, which I am a huge fan of. \n2. They've enabled adding custom providers in settings.\n3. Performance seems improved, it's less slow in large sessions and thread. https://t.co/TyXLfNWrVL",
      "likes": 220,
      "retweets": 4,
      "replies": 11,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2034243822566187014",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },

      "id": "2034221744215502975",
      "created_at": "2026-03-18 10:53:52+00:00",
      "text": "Fundamental masterpiece:  https://t.co/LUj3xKf0Mv\n\nPeople scoff at crypto bros, and the crypto industry.\nThe technology itself is incredible, imagine that we can be our own bank for the first time in history. \n\nSelf sovereignty is now possible, but why then does this attract some https://t.co/heEtyX3SMy",

      "retweets": 1,

      "is_reply": false
    },

      "id": "2034015951868276846",
      "created_at": "2026-03-17 21:16:07+00:00",
      "text": "Codex app slaps so hard for research man, I have really enjoyed this experience. \n\nThe new design/theme thing makes me super happy. I'm like a hamster, if my environment doesn't change I go crazy.\n\nThey have to do something about these limits though, I'll survive ig. https://t.co/34QhSckLrp",

    },

    {
      "id": "2033996410790555948",
      "created_at": "2026-03-17 19:58:28+00:00",
      "text": "I got access to MiniMax-M2.7 \n\nIt successfully setup Hermes agent on my Mac mini (sshing from my MacBook to it) got tg setup and ported my Droid config there. \n\nExcellent\n\nSend your prompts I will pick a few to show off in a video. A prompt you send will go straight to MiniMax. https://t.co/JhreDfUxPE",
      "likes": 309,
      "retweets": 6,
      "replies": 43,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2033996410790555948",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2033986935018971551",
      "created_at": "2026-03-17 19:20:49+00:00",
      "text": "@vicstevanin You don’t need to pay for it to use it, but yes. I have been paying for the 20$ plan and bringing my own key for months now",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2033962009327284535",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1587850643724767232",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2033975054053085283"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2033986761982906564",
      "created_at": "2026-03-17 19:20:08+00:00",
      "text": "@thePartyPartyUS Check my YouTube",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2033985514055823649",

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2033962009327284535",
      "created_at": "2026-03-17 17:41:46+00:00",
      "text": "I don’t think you’ll find a better or more economical model for computer use, not in Droid, nor in OpenClaw. \n\nGPT-5.4 series is the most capable agent I use daily this is the first time I run out of Pro subscription usage. https://t.co/DE5RFFR5Vz",
      "likes": 252,
      "retweets": 6,
      "replies": 16,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2033962009327284535",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2033958404763668942"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2033949288686845971",
      "created_at": "2026-03-17 16:51:14+00:00",
      "text": "@TomasMann1878 Yes, done via auto-research. But I have to be in the loop for it to improve, I tried letting it loose for 24 hours but it tended to overfit/\"cheat\" if I didn't go through everything with a fine comb",
      "likes": 10,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2033938836607082786",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1929678029841932288",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2033948812289400877"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2033948387507110030",
      "created_at": "2026-03-17 16:47:39+00:00",
      "text": "@HotAisle No where near good enough but I will get there. thank you",
      "likes": 3,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2033938836607082786",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1714688881311051778",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2033943794199236941"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2033938836607082786",
      "created_at": "2026-03-17 16:09:42+00:00",
      "text": "Numbers below are for Qwen3.5-35B constrained to 24gb of VRAM for weights (out of 70gb~ it needs) \n\nThe method is working, I am so pumped, 100+ hours of auto-research making this possible. https://t.co/hHl5CWoJo8 https://t.co/i7DWIVcJ3V",
      "likes": 231,
      "retweets": 8,

    {
      "id": "2033925725237981302",
      "created_at": "2026-03-17 15:17:36+00:00",
      "text": "The most insane thing I have been internalizing is that I only need 6 hours of sleep. \n\nI feel like I can’t justify a minute more, I know that’s wrong, but life is so great and I don’t want to lost 8% of my life in bed. \n\nFor those who sleep 6h how do you stay sane https://t.co/TUvv0FVQIB",
      "likes": 19,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 21,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2033925725237981302",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },

      "id": "2033912255243313380",
      "created_at": "2026-03-17 14:24:04+00:00",
      "text": "Only 3 weeks left for the big event in the UK. \n\nI'm cohosting an event with the OpenAI &amp; Cerebras teams in London. We'd love to have you there if you'll be around on April 07\n\nSign up here: https://t.co/pZMJLUumbj",

    },
    {
      "id": "2033899376813244517",
      "created_at": "2026-03-17 13:32:54+00:00",
      "text": "@foley2k2 If you can determine what experts matter most for a given prompt and trajectory you can load all the experts requires + some failover into GPU and offload the rest into CPU memory \n\nThat should prevent or at least minimise the need for GPT &lt;&gt; CPU communication. \n\nYou can train an",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2033831638614835335",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "352738653",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2033897965924794551"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2033896937884201003",
      "created_at": "2026-03-17 13:23:12+00:00",
      "text": "@foley2k2 If you don't want a 50% prune + 1.58-2 bit quantisation you will need  3-400 gb of RAM + VRAM",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2033831638614835335",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "352738653",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2033896012310339993"
        }
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "2033831638614835335",
      "created_at": "2026-03-17 09:03:44+00:00",
      "text": "We did it boys, Kimi running on 8x 3090s and 256gb of DDR4 \n\n7~ tokens/s for generation. \n\nThis is pre-reap enabled salience optimizations. This should get up to a stable 25~ tokens/s for generation and 200~ prefill \n\nI’m hoping, at least https://t.co/KE8qRiYjKW",
      "likes": 154,
      "retweets": 7,
      "replies": 15,
      "quotes": 2,
      "conversation_id": "2033831638614835335",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2033830245707530556",
      "created_at": "2026-03-17 08:58:11+00:00",
      "text": "I mostly agree with Mo. \n\nI have been victim to this dozens of times, whether it be fake tests that do nothing, insecure integrations, useEffects everywhere. \n\nIt does make you delusional because it is extremely convincing and has the skills to lie effectively https://t.co/nXYrJEOGi3",
      "likes": 133,
      "retweets": 6,
      "replies": 10,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2033830245707530556",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2033532603433619845"
        }
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "2033683898765611476",
      "created_at": "2026-03-16 23:16:40+00:00",
      "text": "@PopVerseYT If u use vibeproxy and cliproxyapi",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,

    {
      "id": "2033681612613812511",
      "created_at": "2026-03-16 23:07:35+00:00",
      "text": "I figured it out. \n\nStep 1: Layer-wise saliency scoring on personal calibration dataset, agentic, and coding. \n\nStep 2: Prune the QAT 4 bit Kimi 50% \n\nStep 3: use vllm UVA to load 120GB for 30% of experts &amp; the rest to CPU \n\nStep 4: use vllm sleep to dynamically unload and load https://t.co/BryfzF8LXY",
      "likes": 50,
      "retweets": 4,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2033681612613812511",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2033678368378343927",
      "created_at": "2026-03-16 22:54:41+00:00",
      "text": "@scaling01 Already posted cowboy",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 1,

    {

      "text": "Huge boost, this is going to be a token burner https://t.co/dF0uWKpRTM",

      "conversation_id": "2033675337519755771",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,

    {
      "id": "2033671294105870616",
      "created_at": "2026-03-16 22:26:34+00:00",
      "text": "How Droid &amp; I do all frontend work these days, I don't want the bots to touch code until they have something to compare their work against. \n\nThis seems to work much better than just letting them do whatever they think is right. https://t.co/SKo4zxZiFG",
      "likes": 122,
      "retweets": 7,
      "replies": 10,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2033671294105870616",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2033653561066684630",
      "created_at": "2026-03-16 21:16:07+00:00",
      "text": "@parthsareen I tend to stick to 1 session at a time so no, but I have and it works",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,

    {
      "id": "2033636390794059829",
      "created_at": "2026-03-16 20:07:53+00:00",
      "text": "I think we finally got a banger model from Mistral we can run locally FAST. This is sooooo exciting. \n\nThey built it for complex math, agentic, and coding. \nhttps://t.co/lhxDEFG1Jq\n\nI will have quants and reaps up for this by end of week. https://t.co/yBKxsk2kMn",
      "likes": 211,
      "retweets": 7,
      "replies": 7,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2033636390794059829",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },

      "id": "2033610332698976292",
      "created_at": "2026-03-16 18:24:20+00:00",
      "text": "I would pay 100-200$ a month for X just with 0 AI or bot accounts. Who's building this?\n\nI got raided by 600 bots in a day, this sucks because now my metrics look juiced ): https://t.co/ghDqXozim8",

    {
      "id": "2033608642914570326",
      "created_at": "2026-03-16 18:17:37+00:00",
      "text": "If you're a bot or AI and you follow me &amp; and see my post I urge you to unfollow.",
      "likes": 42,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 13,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2033608642914570326",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,

    {
      "id": "2033582489483030663",
      "created_at": "2026-03-16 16:33:42+00:00",
      "text": "@oorusr I added recording to Parchi so I can teach it how to do things and save it as skills. \n\nI also like multi-tab orchestration but it’s messy. I don’t mean it in an offensive way 🫡",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,

    },
    {
      "id": "2033572738930004194",
      "created_at": "2026-03-16 15:54:57+00:00",
      "text": "@LLMJunky I legit think that both were made sometime in October-November last year. I didn't know about it for months after I made mine.",

      "id": "2033572570209927488",
      "created_at": "2026-03-16 15:54:17+00:00",
      "text": "@oorusr 1. My priorities are different from Mario's? \n2. It's open source so I can do whatever i want with it?",

    {

      "likes": 4,

    {
      "id": "2033560866713428123",
      "created_at": "2026-03-16 15:07:47+00:00",
      "text": "@nivkorin2004 I will be pushing the parchi features here: \nhttps://t.co/mSDFm9ubv1",
      "likes": 6,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2033557447986618495",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "860210543839367168",
      "is_reply": true,

        {

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2033557447986618495",
      "created_at": "2026-03-16 14:54:11+00:00",
      "text": "Today I am retiring the original Parchi.\n\nI am forking https://t.co/sh8jKnC7l6 and adding the features I want in there. \n\nIt's a great codebase, it's missing a few things but alas code is cleaner, and the design is nice. \n\nI recommend you check it out if you liked Parchi. https://t.co/XoWof2jkc4",
      "likes": 112,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 9,
      "quotes": 2,
      "conversation_id": "2033557447986618495",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2033539594038198637",
      "created_at": "2026-03-16 13:43:15+00:00",
      "text": "It's over for me. https://t.co/55LbJ0wCYm",
      "likes": 110,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 28,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2033539594038198637",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2033539141959406072",
      "created_at": "2026-03-16 13:41:27+00:00",
      "text": "This is why I don't put up results as much as I post about what I'm doing, every little thing takes 2 hours to validate even on statistically irrelevant sample-sizes.\n\nI have been working on this compression research for 3 months, and all I have to show for it is more ideas (,: https://t.co/vLVBBhRNc8",
      "likes": 57,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2033539141959406072",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2033537454276292813",

    {
      "id": "2033521126396084543",
      "created_at": "2026-03-16 12:29:52+00:00",
      "text": "@pierrelezan @signulll I am running awq-4bit of minimax-m2.5 i run glm-4.7-reap and I switched to Cerebras' when they published their version. \n\nThey basically ran 20k samples + 16k context for their calibration where i ran 1.5k at 2k context \n\nI don't have the compute and budget available to do that",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2033250256918368324",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1958203226",
      "is_reply": true,

          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2033520421857812859"
        }
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "2033295890408968528",
      "created_at": "2026-03-15 21:34:51+00:00",
      "text": "Hermes Agent + GLM-5-Turbo + Qwen3.5 video coming out this week. \n\nI think Hermes is great for anyone interested in learning about how stuff works, training etc.. \n\nIt’s mlops skills are very good. I mainly want to show off everything I have been playing with lately https://t.co/l1yjAFXfNf",
      "likes": 320,
      "retweets": 7,
      "replies": 12,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2033295890408968528",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2033293055424381072",
      "created_at": "2026-03-15 21:23:35+00:00",
      "text": "@al_f4lc0n @immunefi @injective dawg",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,

      "quotes": 0,

      "referenced_tweets": [

          "id": "2033110168045568434"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2033291172567732456",
      "created_at": "2026-03-15 21:16:06+00:00",
      "text": "Great model reliable at DevOps. \n\nI’ve always loved GLM models for long form reasoning. They tend to do much better than GPT models when sshing into remote environments etc.. \n\nAlso very fast https://t.co/Wh1LEl7oiL",
      "likes": 59,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 5,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2033291172567732456",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2033224682565161461"
        }
      ]
    },

      "id": "2033286254154895712",
      "created_at": "2026-03-15 20:56:34+00:00",
      "text": "@tmophoto It is fundamentally unable to do anything without something prompting it. \n\nI have all ports shut off no public access. So it’s either me or scripts that launch it. \n\nIt does do things like once it wiped my NVMe storage for no reason. Another deleted a discord channel",
      "likes": 4,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,

      "id": "2033211711168155735",
      "created_at": "2026-03-15 16:00:21+00:00",
      "text": "Here's my current stack\n\n----------\n\nModels:\n\n1. GPT-5.4 - Research + Programming + Parchi + OC\n2. Kimi-K2.5 - Frontend Dev + Computer Use + Parchi\n3. Opus-4.6 - Writing + Review GPT + Fallback\n4. Gemini-3.1-Pro - Writing + Research + Reviews\n5. Secret Model - Very fast + Devops https://t.co/DMdqq6McmK",
      "likes": 1009,
      "retweets": 67,
      "replies": 64,
      "quotes": 2,
      "conversation_id": "2033211711168155735",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },

      "id": "2033131146351927744",
      "created_at": "2026-03-15 10:40:13+00:00",
      "text": "This is the way. \n\nPewdiepie did this with Cancer research, Karpathy did this with nanochat. \n\nSo much amazing breakthroughs \n\nMy question: Can I run Kimi-K2.5 at home?\nhttps://t.co/SLTiSipEjj https://t.co/kUb03EKjzH",

    {
      "id": "2033130744965382527",
      "created_at": "2026-03-15 10:38:37+00:00",
      "text": "@caumeira_ @caps_raunak You have a hyper intelligent infinitely patient teacher accessible for free. \n\nAsk it to help you",

    {
      "id": "2033130585917383141",
      "created_at": "2026-03-15 10:38:00+00:00",
      "text": "I knight KittyLitter as:\n\nThe best mobile AI app. It’s Codex on your Phone, it lets you remote control your computer. \n\nI have been running research on it for 48h+ now and have no complaints. It uses your codex sub. \n\nEnjoy https://t.co/A8oNXpTd3v",
      "likes": 108,
      "retweets": 9,
      "replies": 9,
      "quotes": 3,
      "conversation_id": "2033130585917383141",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2033129727410520473",
      "created_at": "2026-03-15 10:34:35+00:00",
      "text": "@ebenezergelo @caps_raunak Also look into their ssh remote directories and make sure to ask your agents to add the zed ignore flag for large generated modules. \n\nZed integrates LSPs and with agents and multi windows it slows down significantly if you don’t",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2032926171755852181",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1025447897712787456",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2033128861865492634"
        }
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "2032746360219529275",
      "created_at": "2026-03-14 09:11:13+00:00",
      "text": "@tomcrojo Where do I start! \n\nAnyway I think they’re all great and also trash lol, this one just tends to give me better results",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2032545501711609912",

    {
      "id": "2032742868087943321",
      "created_at": "2026-03-14 08:57:20+00:00",
      "text": "Whoever can reverse engineer their context and workflow management will be very happy. \n\n- 8 hours 20 minute runtime\n- 7/16 done\n- 61.1M cached 12.8M input. 444k output https://t.co/RSIHHz6tVX",
      "likes": 220,
      "retweets": 8,
      "replies": 16,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2032742868087943321",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2032738207100490001",
      "created_at": "2026-03-14 08:38:49+00:00",
      "text": "Let me make your life better\nDon't skip this one\n\n1. Go to https://t.co/JC2bCR445x \n2. Select everything \n3. Request backup \n4. Install zip \n5. Make an offline copy \n6. RAG it, make a skill to search it \n\nIf you're going to hook up an LLM to your Google you need to have a backup https://t.co/UX7s1k6Uqq",
      "likes": 422,
      "retweets": 26,
      "replies": 11,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2032738207100490001",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2032737278674538895",
      "created_at": "2026-03-14 08:35:08+00:00",
      "text": "Parchi updates this week\n\n1. Inspired by Droid missions I tried porting as much of the logic as I could figure out. \n\nNow you can work on large tasks that span multiple tabs that need to coordinate with each-other. \n\nSubagents here are sessions you can communicate with. \n\n2. I https://t.co/Q5WfoBu7ZW",
      "likes": 46,
      "retweets": 3,
      "replies": 4,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2032737278674538895",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2032733385341255738",
      "created_at": "2026-03-14 08:19:40+00:00",
      "text": "@cjayls I don't do much F/E and I don't trust any other model to manage anything related to research",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2032620938030731477",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1010084790568194049",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2032732358042943756"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2032731938323153132",
      "created_at": "2026-03-14 08:13:55+00:00",
      "text": "They did something special with GPT-5.4\n\nI am usually agent maxxing with all the different models, this week, it was:\n\n- 90% GPT-5.4 \n- 5% Kimi\n- 1% Gemini Pro 3.1\n- 2% opus \n- 2% new secret special model \n\n5.4 is pretty much perfect for everything, I only don't like it's F/E https://t.co/Hl39bfv0Sr",
      "likes": 452,
      "retweets": 11,
      "replies": 29,
      "quotes": 2,
      "conversation_id": "2032731938323153132",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2032725176148779393",
      "created_at": "2026-03-14 07:47:02+00:00",
      "text": "@LLMJunky @Angaisb_ I got my highest 2 week pay. 721$ 🥴",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2032607809104207879",

        }
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "2032711896269287929",
      "created_at": "2026-03-14 06:54:16+00:00",
      "text": "RIP my weekends, I just figured out how to use Codex from my phone. \n\nKitty litter let's your phone connect you to Codex server on your desktop. Very smooth UI. \nhttps://t.co/AIm8DN4fnr https://t.co/nFEwC3ZTr1",
      "likes": 355,
      "retweets": 14,
      "replies": 18,

      "text": "@sharpeye_wnl I didn’t explain myself well. I see that when I read my comment lol. \n\nI said I will select 3 people if the post has less than 100 comments by Friday, if there’s more than 100 I will select 3 people who liked the post. \n\nAnyway. Put the book on your wishlist (:",

    {
      "id": "2032422539104748000",
      "created_at": "2026-03-13 11:44:28+00:00",
      "text": "@Mikerhinos @NicoHezel Also to get it to run good you need 8GB of VRAM + the original model size in bf-16 in RAM (DDR4 or DDR5)\n\nIt'd work with NVMe but be significantly slower.",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2032182215837098090",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2032422279624204582"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2032422279624204582",
      "created_at": "2026-03-13 11:43:26+00:00",
      "text": "@Mikerhinos @NicoHezel Theoretically yes, I am running it at 35% original BF16 size pre-quantization\n\nI am using a custom patched version of vllm but it is a research project that isn't near usable or stable, nor do I think I can get it there. \n\nI published some of it. once I validate it all we see",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2032182215837098090",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "55794782",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2032421282285842873"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2032417497853424090",
      "created_at": "2026-03-13 11:24:26+00:00",
      "text": "If a winner(s) doesn't respond I will reach out to these people in order.\n\n1. @argvalue\n2. @rustler2000\n3. @ClaudiusHaag",
      "likes": 4,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2032417493134856575",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2032417495689077061"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2032417495689077061",
      "created_at": "2026-03-13 11:24:26+00:00",
      "text": "Winners: \n\n@astonepokes\n@goldenlionegg\n@kirtangajjar_",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2032417493134856575",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2032417493134856575"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2032417493134856575",
      "created_at": "2026-03-13 11:24:25+00:00",
      "text": "If you are from another industry that has anything to do with using a computer, I suggest you read this book. \n\nIt's important to be educated on what will increasingly be as fundamental, and ubiquitous as phones and laptops.\n\nWinners in the comments, I will reach out to each https://t.co/nzW7IbHRtk",
      "likes": 73,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 4,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2032417493134856575",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2031316684800209234"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2032414793307492400",
      "created_at": "2026-03-13 11:13:41+00:00",
      "text": "This is amazing, big things are happening with srossitto79 on Github.\n\n-------\n\nllama.cpp truly leads at every type of optimisation for running high quality AI on consumer hardware. \n\nIt has dynamic ggufs which are by far the most intelligent quantizations, they have 100+ major https://t.co/9oxaLIIGm5",
      "likes": 78,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2032414793307492400",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2032202089359684078"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2032412265572421816",
      "created_at": "2026-03-13 11:03:39+00:00",
      "text": "@aeitroc Thanks for the proxy, i'll try it out.",

      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,

    {
      "id": "2032232925396185410",
      "created_at": "2026-03-12 23:11:01+00:00",
      "text": "RT @cerebras: Our coding workflows were designed to accommodate slow inference. @OpenAI's Codex Spark powered by @cerebras changes the game…",

    {
      "id": "2032225732122800423",
      "created_at": "2026-03-12 22:42:26+00:00",
      "text": "https://t.co/K1IpDXGjGX",
      "likes": 5,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,

    {
      "id": "2032225608558919961",
      "created_at": "2026-03-12 22:41:56+00:00",
      "text": "Most stable 64% REAP\n\nNo more runtime error, only 70% slowdown but model weights in vram 36% pre-quantization, whole model is available, so only losses are from wrong expert predictions. \n\nWith Q4 quantization we can run Qwen3.5-35B theoretically near lossless. needing only 5gb~ https://t.co/EM8KPS244I",
      "likes": 68,
      "retweets": 3,
      "replies": 4,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2032225608558919961",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2032215929031962796",
      "created_at": "2026-03-12 22:03:28+00:00",
      "text": "@dude474 Halfway done",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2032182215837098090",

      "is_reply": true,

          "type": "replied_to",

        }
      ]

      "text": "@NicoHezel I don’t use llama, this is for vLLM",

    {
      "id": "2032203882382688519",
      "created_at": "2026-03-12 21:15:36+00:00",
      "text": "@pierrelezan Like using the end model? \n\nI’m mostly systemizing and benchmarking. \n86% accuracy 100% coherence 2x slower \n\nBut only working for 3ish turns, improving",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2031756106725253490",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1958203226",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2032203166234669269"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2032182215837098090",
      "created_at": "2026-03-12 19:49:31+00:00",
      "text": "My missions have taken me places, I am up to 88.56% accuracy with only 37% of experts in VRAM at a time and an incredibly low swap time. \n\nI am probably wrong in a billion ways, but here's the repo\nhttps://t.co/K1IpDXGjGX https://t.co/SuQMCaz7WD",
      "likes": 157,
      "retweets": 7,
      "replies": 7,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2032182215837098090",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2032177887516729565",
      "created_at": "2026-03-12 19:32:19+00:00",
      "text": "@ivanfioravanti Please let me know when you check it out, i really need some testing",
      "likes": 3,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2032176612624527575",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "43874767",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2032176612624527575"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2032175261483962649",
      "created_at": "2026-03-12 19:21:52+00:00",
      "text": "First REAPs done on Apple hardware have been published! We have a winner. \n\nNow we know it works. We should all be able to relatively cheaply produce REAPs for the localAI community. \n\nMax sub for the winner\n\n- Models https://t.co/tmtAI1tTkA\n- Try it https://t.co/cbviaC28sz https://t.co/XGwhuk0G4l",
      "likes": 81,
      "retweets": 4,
      "replies": 5,
      "quotes": 3,
      "conversation_id": "2032175261483962649",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2030221301730640335"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2032140481845403926",
      "created_at": "2026-03-12 17:03:40+00:00",
