    {
      "id": "2032041084046475327",
      "created_at": "2026-03-12 10:28:42+00:00",
      "text": "Foundational LLM knowledge, how models think. I will use LLMs interchangeably with Claude.\n\n1. LLMs use the same weights to answer the prompt in different languages.\n\n2. LLMs can compute with the future in mind, since they're able to produce logical poetry which requires planning",
      "likes": 122,
      "retweets": 10,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2032041084046475327",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2032037818428514308",
      "created_at": "2026-03-12 10:15:44+00:00",
      "text": "The pursuit of freedom is my favourite narrative in any media, I wrote so much about the idea but never published any of it. \n\nI was born and raised in NYC, freedom is practically our city symbol. I also traveled around the world and heard so many people say that freedom is all https://t.co/BNdsXRv2kG",
      "likes": 14,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2032037818428514308",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2032034523643269168",
      "created_at": "2026-03-12 10:02:38+00:00",
      "text": "@Kaiyes_ let's test it together, I am happy to help. \n\nif the llm is an MoE we can make it work, I can look into tts compression",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2031990190038782092",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1526972136",

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2032026316992549032",
      "created_at": "2026-03-12 09:30:01+00:00",
      "text": "@Kaiyes_ You could protect the languages you want and prune around them, which does shrink the model but doesn't translate cleanly to you trim off languages you don't want.",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2031990190038782092",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2032025736479924285"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2032025736479924285",
      "created_at": "2026-03-12 09:27:43+00:00",
      "text": "@Kaiyes_ It's not that simple, unfortunately. Weights tend to be pretty language agnostic https://t.co/gDCnH3oI9Z based on my limited understanding on Anthropic's paper.",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2031990190038782092",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1526972136",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [

          "type": "replied_to",

        }
      ]
    },

    {

      "text": "It’s 9c outside the guys are cooking https://t.co/VaWYBe52oB",

    {
      "id": "2031780467196727334",
      "created_at": "2026-03-11 17:13:06+00:00",
      "text": "Computers weren't built to handle the throughput of agents, my Macbook can't handle it. I have to move onto my Linux machine for my real work permenantly. \n\nWe need new computers, and simpler software. Everything is broken from the OS, to Browsers, to IDEs and terminals. \n\n(,:",
      "likes": 190,
      "retweets": 8,
      "replies": 17,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2031780467196727334",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2031778603067748694",
      "created_at": "2026-03-11 17:05:42+00:00",

      "retweets": 0,

      "quotes": 0,

          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2031778339573416356"
        }
      ]
    },

      "created_at": "2026-03-11 16:12:15+00:00",

    {
      "id": "2031761568338239790",
      "created_at": "2026-03-11 15:58:00+00:00",
      "text": "@iamsaranhere I use to have a software agency, I worked on a lot of core open source infra in blockchain and AI\n\nNow I do a lot on socials, some education, and R&amp;D + Devrel\n\nBefore all this I did sales online, home appliances, electric contracts, before that in fast food, and b4 that w my dad",
      "likes": 9,

      "replies": 1,

    {
      "id": "2031757792160120909",
      "created_at": "2026-03-11 15:43:00+00:00",
      "text": "@markfrancisio You have to tell it what different means. It works really well for me, like here it mostly gave me different themes. I reprompted to get different DevEX",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2031730581831114962",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1518573248023339008",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2031757183348510833"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2031756106725253490",
      "created_at": "2026-03-11 15:36:18+00:00",
      "text": "Hot swapping experts in an out of VRAM during runtime with REAP activation saliency scoring. \n\n63% VRAM reducing pre quantization. \n\nthis is a record for me. \n\nThis brings down the cost of running Kimi from 2TB in FP16 down to 175GB in 4 bits. https://t.co/jANDbPx27B",
      "likes": 51,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2031756106725253490",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2031749038312190001",
      "created_at": "2026-03-11 15:08:13+00:00",
      "text": "@saffabyte Have you thought about why it would be a problem? What can the CCP do to you? \n\nAre you American? I am much more paranoid about the US",

    {
      "id": "2031730581831114962",
      "created_at": "2026-03-11 13:54:53+00:00",
      "text": "This is how i build everything now. Before the agents touch a line of code \n\n\"before you do any building make 10 mock ups in html of how we can make it look\" \n\nI then pick a path and ask for more mockups until I feel happy with the feel. The only way to work with these things. https://t.co/ueSBLTmwHk",
      "likes": 509,
      "retweets": 22,
      "replies": 36,
      "quotes": 4,
      "conversation_id": "2031730581831114962",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {

    {
      "id": "2031723008918753321",
      "created_at": "2026-03-11 13:24:47+00:00",
      "text": "Stop the Slop - Day 02\n\n- Ends in a question mark\n- Perfect grammar \n- Account posts on 1 hour schedule \n- Needlessly accusatory \n\nA lot of people will run bots like this to experiment, they tend to target larger accounts to reply to.\n\nImpression farms.\n\nhttps://t.co/Y8m7deOCit",
      "likes": 15,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 5,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2031723008918753321",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2031721439141777736"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2031721718692065619",
      "created_at": "2026-03-11 13:19:39+00:00",
      "text": "12 hour run, refactoring Parchi to standardise types, break down large files, deduplicate code, improve test coverage and code style. \n\nReally loving missions I have to jump between tabs way less and it's finally stable with custom providers. https://t.co/Y9B7wIxEdE",
      "likes": 39,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2031721718692065619",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {

    {
      "id": "2031698534232113373",
      "created_at": "2026-03-11 11:47:32+00:00",
      "text": "@sourcetms Mainly cause I'm using it everyday and I wanted to try it out. I'll be honest the amount of tokens they provide is too low for me, I am also on 500$ of gpt pro kimi allegreto, and claude max",

    {
      "id": "2031697265056690599",
      "created_at": "2026-03-11 11:42:29+00:00",
      "text": "@sourcetms I am on Max now but you can BYOK for free.",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,

    {
      "id": "2031693576451604687",
      "created_at": "2026-03-11 11:27:50+00:00",
      "text": "When you get into building AI harnesses, an amazing thing you can do is ask the model what tools or additions can make it easier. \n\nKimi has been dethroned as the best browser use model, GPT-5.4 is leaps beyond. https://t.co/gt7FWZVrB1",
      "likes": 58,
      "retweets": 5,
      "replies": 6,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2031693576451604687",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2031690724618510528",
      "created_at": "2026-03-11 11:16:30+00:00",
      "text": "@boxy37 @johntheyoung @agentmail GPT-5.4 holds up to 1 million token, and it seems to be more efficient than stacking a bunch of tools to mimic what this can do.",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2031680964619858105",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "21859366",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2031689792895729865"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2031689180170821656",
      "created_at": "2026-03-11 11:10:22+00:00",
      "text": "@RayFernando1337 It's really nice for refactoring, and frontend. The mission contract stuff makes it perform really well",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2031524265992847668",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1691669297637277696",

      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2031688979603456378",
      "created_at": "2026-03-11 11:09:34+00:00",
      "text": "@Olli757 I don't either. DW",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2031527285778489682",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "245567249",
      "is_reply": true,

    {
      "id": "2031687609999556766",
      "created_at": "2026-03-11 11:04:07+00:00",
      "text": "It is important to give LLMs tools that focus on computer use. \n\n1. Reduce security risk surface + reducing dependencies\n2. Data will be fed back to Claude, GPT and labs downstream via distillation.\n3. LLMs that learn to navigate our existing tools will be more predictable. https://t.co/mQ9YyoBD1Q",
      "likes": 21,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2031687609999556766",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2031685703487139871"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2031685703487139871",
      "created_at": "2026-03-11 10:56:33+00:00",
      "text": "@johntheyoung @agentmail I prefer to minimize using other people's stuff. JS/TS have a massive supply chain risk. \n\nI can't avoid it fully, but peekaboo let's it navigate and figure things out organically. This model is very good at that. \n\nThis is why I bought a mac mini, btw. I work on MacOS and need",
      "likes": 4,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2031680964619858105",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1235457454642573312",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2031684609105363364"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2031684505111519325",
      "created_at": "2026-03-11 10:51:47+00:00",

    {
      "id": "2031681575331549484",
      "created_at": "2026-03-11 10:40:09+00:00",
      "text": "@__jason________ @alexhooketh Yes, it's worse at high complexity tasks. It doesn't have the same get it fixed at all costs as Opus. \n\nI find it the most productive for UI, styles, writing and copy, etc..",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,

    {
      "id": "2031680964619858105",
      "created_at": "2026-03-11 10:37:43+00:00",
      "text": "GPT-5.4 is the first model in OpenClaw to e2e purchase stuff for me and deliver it to my house in 5 mins. \n\nIt picked up the credit card I gave Kimi over a month ago, logged in, setup the delivery details, and purchased. I am bamboozled.\n\nOpus and Kimi took hours, and got it all https://t.co/ReXkoDuWdw",
      "likes": 50,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 10,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2031680964619858105",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },

    },
    {
      "id": "2031662196506210459",
      "created_at": "2026-03-11 09:23:08+00:00",
      "text": "@moinulmoin Kimi + GLM + GPT-5.4 in various roles \n\nI did one which was just GLM and another just Kimi they’re all running fine. \n\nI am using vibeproxy and some scripts to prevent some issues with Droid and BYOK",
      "likes": 7,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2031649050085245220",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1248119942194421760",

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2031651320709464527",
      "created_at": "2026-03-11 08:39:55+00:00",
      "text": "@karpathy @maxbittker The fact they don’t want to run forever is very interesting. I’ve found great success in prompting “continue” in queue mode 20+ times. \n\nEventually it says it has nothing left to do still, but it gets through 10 hours",
      "likes": 20,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2031473248320311326",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "33836629",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [

          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2031621392609980754"
        }
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "2031649050085245220",
      "created_at": "2026-03-11 08:30:54+00:00",
      "text": "I ran 5x 8 hour missions overnight on my Kimi 40$ subscription. \n\nI woke up to all of them running smoothly still, each has 20+ sessions. \n\nThis model is often indistinguishable from Claude, about 5x cheaper, open weight, and their Kimi code has to be incentivized I never run out https://t.co/UeeeRPIsQK",
      "likes": 717,
      "retweets": 25,
      "replies": 32,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2031649050085245220",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2031630810344825242"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2031529880471450017",
      "created_at": "2026-03-11 00:37:22+00:00",
      "text": "@WolfeFolks @thdxr Ok lol",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2031490937197645918",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1300552606163431425",

    {
      "id": "2031527285778489682",
      "created_at": "2026-03-11 00:27:03+00:00",
      "text": "Running my first Mission paired with Autoresearch: https://t.co/OaNJNAuVUh\n\nToday I was able to maintain 90% coherency + 37% correctness compared to the BF16 with only 20% of the VRAM. \n\nI was able to swap in experts are runtime with really low latency adding only 25% delay. The https://t.co/3cXa3ggTgm",
      "likes": 160,
      "retweets": 11,
      "replies": 17,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2031527285778489682",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2031526281959899199",
      "created_at": "2026-03-11 00:23:04+00:00",
      "text": "This looks so damn good. I want to go back to. Inspiring me to go back to Azul. \n\nI want to see how far the current gen models can get browsers inside GPU accelerated terminals. \n\n These repos grow with the models when the models get better I progress.\nhttps://t.co/aU4pkMADZK https://t.co/lMlc7I3XGK",
      "likes": 45,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2031526281959899199",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,

        }
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "2031492773321339267",
      "created_at": "2026-03-10 22:09:55+00:00",
      "text": "GPT-5.4 is so nice, so fast. A great researcher. It sucks at traditional UI, but man it's perfect in Openclaw, it's great at browser use, and I've been driving it mad researching GPU stuff 24/7",
      "likes": 223,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 15,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2031492773321339267",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2031478542572167458",
      "created_at": "2026-03-10 21:13:22+00:00",
      "text": "Very interesting look into AI privacy &amp; FHE, basically we've been able to do encrypted e2e AI for ~50 years, but the reason we can't at scale is speed. https://t.co/DuVpgNMrBE",
      "likes": 21,
      "retweets": 0,

    {
      "id": "2031457710198145374",
      "created_at": "2026-03-10 19:50:35+00:00",
      "text": "I think I’m going to start a series\n\nHow to spot slop\n\n1. Curious \n2. Question mark\n3.  Balance\n4. Affirming language \n\nLLMs mostly try and get to the next turn, so they will overwhelmingly ask questions like this\n\nWhen prompted to craft a response to x amount of visible accounts https://t.co/KSuyBO1X14",
      "likes": 30,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 8,

    {
      "id": "2031418995836911704",
      "created_at": "2026-03-10 17:16:45+00:00",
      "text": "I had to switch off of Firefox, after 20+ years\n\nI love FireFox but they have so many technical issues that it's become hard to justify using it. \n\nPerformance wise it's not good, extensions are difficult to work with, and they recently pulled out of some privacy promises. Brave",
      "likes": 74,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 27,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2031418995836911704",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2031410378215682332",
      "created_at": "2026-03-10 16:42:30+00:00",
      "text": "@scholzmx @theo why would I do that??",

      "retweets": 0,

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2031401452996813117",
      "created_at": "2026-03-10 16:07:02+00:00",
      "text": "The flickering with Ink is a blight on humanity I can't take it anymore. I think I will try fixing it myself.\n\n&lt;Inb4 I make it 10x worse&gt;\n\nhttps://t.co/o8VFZEWNhW",
      "likes": 4,
      "retweets": 0,

    {
      "id": "2031385560644833586",
      "created_at": "2026-03-10 15:03:53+00:00",
      "text": "Life hack. \n\nMake your agents watch recording of what it's built or done, I tend to use Gemini for this the most. \n\nDon't let it tell you it's done, force it to see before and after comparisons, all this can be built into hooks. https://t.co/cweDDICDwI",
      "likes": 96,
      "retweets": 3,
      "replies": 5,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2031385560644833586",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2031371379639984274",
      "created_at": "2026-03-10 14:07:32+00:00",
      "text": "@ZaiforStartups I’ve been playing around with this to reduce vram requirements for running bf16 weights and it’s amazing. It actually works",
      "likes": 6,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2031363842371097031",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "2027267679112007680",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2031367443302560164",
      "created_at": "2026-03-10 13:51:54+00:00",
      "text": "@astonepokes end to end, i am reading it for the 3rd time lol.",
      "likes": 13,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,

    {
      "id": "2031343200737116189",
      "created_at": "2026-03-10 12:15:34+00:00",
      "text": "@SebastienCaunes @theo 8x 3090s. \n\nI am mainly running:\n\n-----------\n\nGLM-4.7-reap &lt;-- Very good at chat for me since i ask a lot of technical questions and like markdown artifacts\n\nMiniMax-M2.5-awq-4bit &lt;-- great agent can do basic dev\n\nQwen-3.5-212 &amp; 262B &lt;-- good at browser use, I use them for",
      "likes": 3,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2031169047119933460",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "106776317",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2031341855359545473"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2031341747310084467",
      "created_at": "2026-03-10 12:09:47+00:00",
      "text": "@stephenandstuff Did you know that MiniMax has more than 200M monthly active users?",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2031283490633712022",

    {
      "id": "2031341399233249467",
      "created_at": "2026-03-10 12:08:24+00:00",
      "text": "@RNVR77 I learned the history and math behind LLMs at a much higher degree than I have ever cared to learn math. \n\nThis stuff is pretty basic to people who have studied it but everything from logic gates, to neurons, to statistics, to neural networks, CNNs, back-propogation, gradient",
      "likes": 51,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2031316684800209234",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1945031683100860416",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2031339288181485918"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2031316684800209234",
      "created_at": "2026-03-10 10:30:12+00:00",
      "text": "This book really changed my career, I don’t think I’d be where I am without having read it. \n\nI learned so much from it, and have not found any other book that has been able to teach me as this one has. \n\nI will buy this book for 3 people, winners selected on Friday https://t.co/YwGDOpuFou",
      "likes": 1584,
      "retweets": 86,
      "replies": 102,
      "quotes": 7,
      "conversation_id": "2031316684800209234",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {

    {
      "id": "2031022517868003407",
      "created_at": "2026-03-09 15:01:17+00:00",
      "text": "I made a dataset from every AI chat and session I ever made, and passed it to Qwen3.5-35B\n\n- 7.6% of the model handled for 50% of my requests\n- 27.5%  over 80% of my requests\n\nThat means I can technically get any model to 7.6%/3.8% in fp8 someone give me 1 million pls https://t.co/V8kfeb55pO",
      "likes": 69,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 5,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2031022517868003407",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },

      "id": "2030992429311000677",

      "text": "@alexhooketh {\n      \"model_display_name\": \"Kimi - For Coding\",\n      \"model\": \"kimi-for-coding\",\n      \"base_url\": \"https://t.co/ziZIPlMkH7\",\n      \"api_key\": \"sk-kimi-\",\n      \"provider\": \"anthropic\",\n      \"supports_images\": true,\n      \"extra_args\": {\n        \"thinking\": {",

      "replies": 2,

    {
      "id": "2030987366492500022",
      "created_at": "2026-03-09 12:41:36+00:00",
      "text": "@Wald52Wald you can use it for free but no missions, and no custom sub-agents, but you don't need that.",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2030974578663489865",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1769037882532970496",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2030986478637965646"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2030987201052299514",
      "created_at": "2026-03-09 12:40:57+00:00",
      "text": "Let me make your life easier. \n\n1. Before building think about dependencies\n2. Get the model to research libraries that fill your need\n3. Make it put the info in a table \n4. MOST IMPORTANTLY do not use carrots ^ in package/config \n\nThis will solve 50% of your messes. https://t.co/hygkaApFZ7",
      "likes": 89,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2030987201052299514",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2030986389324513609",
      "created_at": "2026-03-09 12:37:43+00:00",
      "text": "@Dhruv14588676 You can by setting up https://t.co/6PsPM6RmSL it's a bit hacky, but it works.",
      "likes": 3,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2030985034748788921",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1504038715047366657",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2030986153294447065"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2030985034748788921",
      "created_at": "2026-03-09 12:32:20+00:00",
      "text": "Why do I recommend Droid? \n\nLook at the way it breaks down it's work, this is why Droid does better IMO. \n\nI have never seen it NOT use a plan, NOT check off the tasks, not run validation criteria. \n\nEven lower quality models do well in it because it forces them to just do what https://t.co/pXFQi6jXDN",
      "likes": 217,
      "retweets": 10,
      "replies": 29,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2030985034748788921",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2030982509966590143",
      "created_at": "2026-03-09 12:22:18+00:00",
      "text": "I got shivers man, I let this run overnight.\nhttps://t.co/OaNJNAuVUh\n\nI am exploring using:\n\n1. vllm sleep mode \n2. REAP salience scoring \n3. https://t.co/c9fhW0vWaG \n\nTo load the model backbone, then have a router trained on expert important to create cartridges of experts https://t.co/NwFaovuyie https://t.co/XGwhuk0G4l",
      "likes": 166,
      "retweets": 13,
      "replies": 9,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2030982509966590143",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2030221301730640335"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2030979007475278199",
      "created_at": "2026-03-09 12:08:23+00:00",

    {
      "id": "2030975894148333795",
      "created_at": "2026-03-09 11:56:01+00:00",
      "text": "It always amazes me how culturally dominant the Japanese are. Their design philosophy and \"taste\" is practically all en-compassing. \n\nFrom Software, Sony, Anime, Manga, Software etc.. only second to USA in reach\n\nPeople who grew up with Anime are embedding that design everything https://t.co/Y20V9NNt5C",
      "likes": 38,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2030975894148333795",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2030757016516616611"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2030974578663489865",
      "created_at": "2026-03-09 11:50:47+00:00",
      "text": "Using my own subs for Droid is such a blessing, I still have to pay 20$ for Factory but it's well worth it. \n\nI'm going to mission doing my taxes this week, stay tuned. https://t.co/CzgXmgNYcP",
      "likes": 90,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 21,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2030974578663489865",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2030963651549471019",
      "created_at": "2026-03-09 11:07:22+00:00",
      "text": "Who's going to be at GTC? You should drop by if you're there. \n\nhttps://t.co/3A84G5oXQq",

    {
      "id": "2030916252554666280",
      "created_at": "2026-03-09 07:59:01+00:00",
      "text": "This is still true. \n\nI love trying new things but Zed’s project tabs is where it’s at. Their terminal has a big issue with rerendering which makes Ink go haywire https://t.co/3xPzVtTzg0",
      "likes": 12,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2030916252554666280",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2030915691784663309",
      "created_at": "2026-03-09 07:56:48+00:00",
      "text": "@Spandyy0 I’m like a hamster. I need novelty or i will KMS. \n\n#1 codex \n#2 droid \n#3 OpenCode and pi",

    {
      "id": "2030757169256083870",
      "created_at": "2026-03-08 21:26:53+00:00",
      "text": "@justBill I am having trouble mentally tracking everything and keeping it all in mind. The apps feel closer to the right thing. \n\nThey just all suck performance wise.",
      "likes": 3,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,

    },
    {
      "id": "2030756847263556034",
      "created_at": "2026-03-08 21:25:36+00:00",
      "text": "@ant_dracula I don’t sell anything. Everything I do is open source",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,

    {
      "id": "2030707529500532924",
      "created_at": "2026-03-08 18:09:38+00:00",
      "text": "@moinulmoin Definitely I discuss this in the video that’s the only thing keeping me from adopting desktop apps",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2030701105936556276",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1248119942194421760",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2030707163899777312"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2030701105936556276",
      "created_at": "2026-03-08 17:44:06+00:00",
      "text": "I spent all day using Opencode desktop app today, I think it's amazing and you should try it out. \n\nTheir plan is very cheap and gives you Kimi, GLM-5 and MiniMax-M2.5 plus you can bring your own subs and now pay a dime.\n\nhttps://t.co/xUY7glrFXa https://t.co/dOnaC5MC3I",
      "likes": 205,
      "retweets": 3,
      "replies": 16,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2030701105936556276",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2030690667370786892",
      "created_at": "2026-03-08 17:02:38+00:00",
      "text": "@johnway___ @sourcetms Definitely, I think it can be better after we figure out what works. I think Opencode leads here, followed by Codex, then Droid.",
      "likes": 3,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2030682585320325388",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1792521023708864512",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2030690132974592034"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2030686947773997205",
      "created_at": "2026-03-08 16:47:51+00:00",
      "text": "@AdhyaayK https://t.co/5oFRdBmz8H",
      "likes": 17,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2030653670375751942",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1913372686195335169",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2030684709378822271",
      "created_at": "2026-03-08 16:38:57+00:00",
      "text": "@sourcetms Opencode, I love Droid's aesthetic but the desktop app is a bit behind I need folders and tasks as threads. \n\nI also need a code viewer, I need to see the code.",
      "likes": 15,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2030682585320325388",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "944936115420659712",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2030684081696973240"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2030684388728390019",
      "created_at": "2026-03-08 16:37:41+00:00",
      "text": "@aaaxny Yes",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2030682585320325388",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "2424299200",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2030683864310391129"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2030682585320325388",
      "created_at": "2026-03-08 16:30:31+00:00",
      "text": "This is the way. \n\nI need to see my files and fs, I need to see what is going on, and I need to have a great diff view, I also need to organize my work and projects. \n\nI am so in love with this https://t.co/hkRcFbrKuW",
      "likes": 239,
      "retweets": 5,
      "replies": 12,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2030682585320325388",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2030678881254932790",
      "created_at": "2026-03-08 16:15:48+00:00",
      "text": "@abundand Of course, but the latest gen models are very good at keeping that to a minimal",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2030661100086141233",

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2030678780394561874",
      "created_at": "2026-03-08 16:15:24+00:00",
      "text": "@MrV_777 Very cool",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,

    {
      "id": "2030675258080960907",
      "created_at": "2026-03-08 16:01:24+00:00",
      "text": "Let me make your life easier. \n\nSetup visual-explainer, and have pre-commit hooks to analyze your state and sequences. \n\nYou can save these in git to see visually how your project is growing. You should never trust what the LLMs say, validate everything. \nhttps://t.co/tLZRUDlPOO https://t.co/ftML3nZq65",
      "likes": 625,
      "retweets": 32,
      "replies": 10,
      "quotes": 2,
      "conversation_id": "2030675258080960907",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2030665291156357307",

    {
      "id": "2030657490308813264",
      "created_at": "2026-03-08 14:50:48+00:00",
      "text": "If you’re using Openclaw, Discord imo is where it’s at\n\nHere’s a new repo that will let you make it more useful https://t.co/a7mzvPhhx2 it’s by the founder of the project. \n\nI asked it to review our activity in discord to figure out what’s wrong, and what isn’t\n\nBetter memory too https://t.co/e5NZXOgDe5",
      "likes": 113,
      "retweets": 7,
      "replies": 5,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2030657490308813264",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2030655882250297747",
      "created_at": "2026-03-08 14:44:24+00:00",
      "text": "@junelovestmh Use whatever you love. I love them all, genuinely :0",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2030423046335373769",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1992929814526013440",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2030654895888404641"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2030653670375751942",
      "created_at": "2026-03-08 14:35:37+00:00",
      "text": "\"Take screenshots and videos of everything you've built, then review it.\"\n\nThe amount of time this saves is wild, add onto it automated QA and you have yourself a semi-autonomous system. \n\nI was given this advice by Leo from Factory on my podcast. https://t.co/mqp4qFZJhY https://t.co/EhaundOOLf",
      "likes": 261,
      "retweets": 9,
      "replies": 12,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2030653670375751942",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2030643765795230113",
      "created_at": "2026-03-08 13:56:15+00:00",

    {
      "id": "2030609323416531007",
      "created_at": "2026-03-08 11:39:24+00:00",
      "text": "@petheth You’re on the right side of the bell curve I’m a midwit",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2030423046335373769",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "109585282",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2030608985766732165"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2030603185266512209",
      "created_at": "2026-03-08 11:15:00+00:00",
      "text": "@satanworker I'm trying the GUIs out, Opencode has the best one. I love toolmaxxing",
      "likes": 3,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2030597926498562120",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "2843423475",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2030602374322979223"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2030602592305160261",
      "created_at": "2026-03-08 11:12:39+00:00",
      "text": "Sub-agents in Parchi available for bigger problems like doing your taxes, for example. \n\nThe design sucks still but each tab is an agent, this let's your main model breakdown and work through a task across multiple websites. \n\nUX sucks rn but it will get better soon https://t.co/rlkuHddbph",
      "likes": 19,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2030602592305160261",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2030597926498562120",
      "created_at": "2026-03-08 10:54:07+00:00",
      "text": "I think a lot about how I can run Kimi in a recognizable form at home, at over 20 tokens/s \n\nI don't think models smaller than 1T will be able to provide high quality consistent responses across a wide range of topics, the math just doesn't work. \n\nI would mark MiniMax as the https://t.co/mwgTIBuGVv",
      "likes": 76,
      "retweets": 6,
      "replies": 9,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2030597926498562120",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2030580238241968136",
      "created_at": "2026-03-08 09:43:49+00:00",
      "text": "A lot of these research papers show the effect in task depth, and have very little consideration for parallelism. \n\nThe independent outputs of LLMs are typically inferior and not so well thought through. \n\nMeasuring people vs AI on single tasks will show a decrease in https://t.co/HOulHaupOn",
      "likes": 22,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 6,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2030580238241968136",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2030538096517796030"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2030579204736041299",
      "created_at": "2026-03-08 09:39:43+00:00",
      "text": "I have been saying this guy is the GOAT, he’s what Peterson could have been\n\nHis predictions are very well thought through, putting every penny I own down on the professors predictions\n\nHe’s made plenty of wrong calls before (Trump’s choice of VP) but he’s more right than wrong https://t.co/e7VE0w3HOv",
      "likes": 66,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 6,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2030579204736041299",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2030537672536862869"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2030554909423112629",
      "created_at": "2026-03-08 08:03:10+00:00",
      "text": "@nummanali OpenAI models are lazy. I had 5.3-codex copy a single task 20 times to tell me it scoped 20 tasks",
      "likes": 12,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2030457080478318647",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1629934761497047041",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2030457080478318647"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2030553937133113375",
      "created_at": "2026-03-08 07:59:19+00:00",
      "text": "Imagine a million Mac Studio ultras chugging together to support an entire country https://t.co/ZkPBNylCj1",
      "likes": 35,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2030553937133113375",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2030522524644065741"
        }
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "2030423046335373769",
      "created_at": "2026-03-07 23:19:12+00:00",
      "text": "Okay, so it goes like this\n\nbest model provider: OpenAI \nbest desktop app: Opencode \nbest cli: Droid\n\nI am trying so hard to move out of Zed + Terminal but I struggle with it, tbh. This just looks amazing to me. I don't know how anyone will catch up to this. https://t.co/Rcve1aKua7",
      "likes": 212,
      "retweets": 3,
      "replies": 24,
      "quotes": 2,
      "conversation_id": "2030423046335373769",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },

      "id": "2030374983881343343",

      "text": "@reach_vb @mweinbach 1. Add the ability to open the file-trees or better yet a dev mode that is closer to Opencode desktop. \n\n2. Performance can be quite bad when you have lots going on. \n\n3. The font and icons are a bit off but maybe that’s something I can already change \n\n4. Add a mobile mode",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2030325317735166210",
      "created_at": "2026-03-07 16:50:52+00:00",
      "text": "6 months of Codex locked in 6 months of GPT Pro thanks to the OpenAI team.\n\nThat’s 1700$ after tax that I would have paid. Very grateful. https://t.co/o2iSiuk63j https://t.co/MYS1NcoBGy",
      "likes": 712,
      "retweets": 4,
      "replies": 22,
      "quotes": 3,
      "conversation_id": "2030325317735166210",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2030252090937815396"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2030321530119307639",
      "created_at": "2026-03-07 16:35:48+00:00",
      "text": "@LLMJunky I can interrupt it literally whenever, it’s a weird thing to describe",

    {
      "id": "2030305649586778604",
      "created_at": "2026-03-07 15:32:42+00:00",
      "text": "https://t.co/oaNpgtyQiB",
      "likes": 84,
      "retweets": 6,
      "replies": 5,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2030305649586778604",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2030289987560677621",
      "created_at": "2026-03-07 14:30:28+00:00",
      "text": "This spoke to my soul on a level I haven't experienced since watching it.\n\nMillennials on-wards are a different species, not in a good or bad way. Just different. \n\nI couldn't imagine letting my son sit on the computer unsupervised at 4 years old. \n\nhttps://t.co/au7F6DAnAS",
      "likes": 5,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2030289987560677621",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2030286187928101026",
      "created_at": "2026-03-07 14:15:22+00:00",

      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,

      "conversation_id": "2030221301730640335",

      "referenced_tweets": [

        }
      ]
    },
    {

      "created_at": "2026-03-07 14:11:25+00:00",

    {
      "id": "2030252090937815396",
      "created_at": "2026-03-07 11:59:53+00:00",
      "text": "Putting this out there in case someone from OpenAI sees it. \n\nI have been contributing to open source for 5 years now. I would be very happy with a few months of Pro. \n\n😳 pls sir free compute? https://t.co/DGOKZ1N6Al",
      "likes": 120,
      "retweets": 2,

    },

    {
      "id": "2030249427714081077",
      "created_at": "2026-03-07 11:49:18+00:00",
      "text": "@alexhooketh It went for 7 hours and crashed I haven’t been able to get it back up busy with kids",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,

      "id": "2030225204631212492",
      "created_at": "2026-03-07 10:13:03+00:00",
      "text": "@RhysSullivan - Peekaboo\n- Airdrop \n- Faster cpu 1 Claude instance can hit 1gb+ \n- small form factor \n- aesthetically pleasing \n- full compatibility with latest software",

      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2030187142690070819",

    {
      "id": "2030224415766532515",
      "created_at": "2026-03-07 10:09:55+00:00",
      "text": "@coderankush 192gb+ preferably 512gb",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,

    {
      "id": "2030221301730640335",
      "created_at": "2026-03-07 09:57:32+00:00",
      "text": "I have a v0 alpha REAP MLX framework. Now anyone with a Mac or MacBook can compress models. \n\nIt supports layer by layer pruning so you can prune larger models at the cost of speed.\n\nI will gift a month of Claude Max to anyone who has a beefy Mac Studio or Ultra and is willing to https://t.co/IjEA1fTm8v",
      "likes": 105,
      "retweets": 7,
      "replies": 18,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2030221301730640335",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },

    {
      "id": "2029994509023474099",
      "created_at": "2026-03-06 18:56:21+00:00",
      "text": "Cheap high quality missions:\n\n- Kimi-K2.5 as orchestrator, this model is already trained on swarms and delegation \n- GLM-5 as a task executor, especially for the type of task I am running right now \n- GPT-5.2 for reviews, mainly cause that's all I have configured right now lol. https://t.co/dHVnAnGsZD",
      "likes": 411,
      "retweets": 17,
      "replies": 23,
      "quotes": 3,
      "conversation_id": "2029994509023474099",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2029986814040170673",
      "created_at": "2026-03-06 18:25:46+00:00",

      "likes": 4,

    {
      "id": "2029972003336155336",
      "created_at": "2026-03-06 17:26:55+00:00",
      "text": "What I mean by subscription is your own pro sub",
      "likes": 3,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2029966596370792498",

    {
      "id": "2029966596370792498",
      "created_at": "2026-03-06 17:05:26+00:00",
      "text": "Latest updates to Parchi:\n\n1. GPT-5.4 available via subscription, new best Parchi model although Kimi-K2.5 is not far behind.. just slower.\n\n2. More themes, and visual levers. \n3. Significantly simplified a lot of things\n4. Made compaction visible, and you are able to compact at https://t.co/d0YaeJNtOt",
      "likes": 48,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 11,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2029966596370792498",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },

    {
      "id": "2029955641649865063",
      "created_at": "2026-03-06 16:21:54+00:00",
      "text": "The mission is 8% done, and it's already been 5 hours. I think this is the coolest one shot experience I've seen.  \n\nOkay, this is wildly better than anything else I've tried. the tests are so logical, it's using the browser to validate the UX, it's doing real quantizations. https://t.co/lNsp2wxGP9 https://t.co/bSk5pKXZqv",
      "likes": 144,
      "retweets": 9,
      "replies": 12,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2029955641649865063",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2029903994651369724"
        }
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "2029925402034983168",
      "created_at": "2026-03-06 14:21:44+00:00",
      "text": "@tobitege it's sequential and sync \n\ndelegate -&gt; do -&gt; review\n\nso no 2 clash since only 1 runs at a time",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2029903994651369724",

        }

    {
      "id": "2029903994651369724",
      "created_at": "2026-03-06 12:56:40+00:00",
      "text": "Okay, Droid Missions are tied for #1 Orchestrator with Roocode's boomerang mode. \n\nThis has been incredibly stable. \n\n1. The \"orchestrator\" model simply delegates tasks and asks the users questions. \n2. A plan is created that is much deeper than what I would expect with spec https://t.co/UAzSolv77C",
      "likes": 168,
      "retweets": 12,
      "replies": 11,
      "quotes": 2,
      "conversation_id": "2029903994651369724",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2029899538438238457",
      "created_at": "2026-03-06 12:38:58+00:00",
      "text": "@artificia1grass I go over them once a week thanks for signing up (:",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2029895607121215930",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "455632885",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [

          "id": "2029899348943749519"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2029899089639264420",
      "created_at": "2026-03-06 12:37:11+00:00",
      "text": "@0xonimasa @birdabo I am a high-school dropout, I learned how to code on:\nhttps://t.co/YsumUjslOv starting 2018 and got my gig in 2020 and started making real money in 2022. \n\nI have since adopted agents for work and have not written more than a few lines of code in 2 years. \n\ni do read the code",
      "likes": 15,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2029806980647547149",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1254040742504173568",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2029896112287666525"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2029895607121215930",
      "created_at": "2026-03-06 12:23:21+00:00",
      "text": "Are you or people you know in London the first week of April? \n\nl'm co-hosting this event with OpenAI &amp; Cerebras\n\nIt's going to be a lot of fun, sign up if you haven't yet, if you know someone in London who might enjoy this pass it on to them\n\nSo excited\n\nhttps://t.co/pZMJLUumbj",
      "likes": 77,
      "retweets": 3,
      "replies": 15,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2029895607121215930",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2029883177578467625",
      "created_at": "2026-03-06 11:33:57+00:00",
      "text": "@carlo_taleon They say that spoofing oauth is against TOS, as for using a proxy I honestly don't know. They don't make it clear. \n\nI am still ripping out the claude oauth, option and it doesn't work rn anyway.",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026187230176268656",

    {
      "id": "2029873156513546281",
      "created_at": "2026-03-06 10:54:08+00:00",
      "text": "@mrmagan_ This is such an amazing resource. This is the same level of complexity as chat apps but with a ton of nonsense sprinkled in. \n\nI wish I had this when I started",

    {
      "id": "2029865881589764120",
      "created_at": "2026-03-06 10:25:13+00:00",
      "text": "@birdabo Yes, I went from unbelievably cooked to very successful by my own standards. My income has 2-3x since I started using AI. \n\nMy GitHub profile is now associated with dozens of high value open source projects (contributor) and this is outside my main business. \n\nI started YouTube",
      "likes": 100,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 6,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2029806980647547149",

      "referenced_tweets": [

          "id": "2029806980647547149"
        }
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "2029859552045187508",
      "created_at": "2026-03-06 10:00:04+00:00",
      "text": "Top AI accounts to follow \n\n- karpathy\n\nbest thought leader, best person to learn from imo. Nanochat is the best way to get into training LLMs its the simplest and most digestible source for building your first AI model\n\n- steipete \n\nThis guys GitHub is a national treasure, his",
      "likes": 719,
      "retweets": 90,
      "replies": 32,
      "quotes": 3,
      "conversation_id": "2029859552045187508",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2029849520255750512",
      "created_at": "2026-03-06 09:20:13+00:00",
      "text": "All my homies designated supply chain risks, Don Dario gets goat status. \n\nSo ironic how the CEO that hated the hardest on Chinese corporations is now in the same bucket as them. \n\nTruly makes me believe in Karma. https://t.co/bK8wkmHs1t",
      "likes": 32,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2029849520255750512",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {

    {
      "id": "2029822273822007601",
      "created_at": "2026-03-06 07:31:57+00:00",
      "text": "RT @yacineMTB: I have been in a debilitating state of panic about AI ever since OpenAI published their research on CLIP and DALLE\n\nIt is on…",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 12,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2029822273822007601",

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2029680810094162219",
      "created_at": "2026-03-05 22:09:49+00:00",
      "text": "One of the most informative videos on how memory works and we tend to stay below 200 tokens/s on Nvidia GPUs. \n\nReally good channel for learning about compression, inference, and AI. \n\nReally learned a lot from this channel I hope you do too. https://t.co/1pzaeyXzuJ",
      "likes": 35,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2029680810094162219",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2028874411756208365"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2029679713032007826",
      "created_at": "2026-03-05 22:05:27+00:00",
      "text": "@Yampeleg I was getting a failure mode where it would think through 32k tokens for 10 minutes the just die lol. \n\nI don’t know what they’re doing with cc anymore",

      "replies": 0,

    {
      "id": "2029654373861626364",
      "created_at": "2026-03-05 20:24:46+00:00",
      "text": "Updates to vllm-studio:\n\n1. 40~ themes added to frontend \n2. Electron desktop app of the frontend\n3. Smaller files &amp; moved a bunch of things around\n\n4. Experimental features:\n\n- Browser \n- Computer use agent\n- Files / Compaction \n- ROCm support \n- Non-local providers \n\n5. Stubs: https://t.co/r2RCeNxPCw",
      "likes": 34,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2029654373861626364",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },

      "text": "@vicnaum @banteg @claudeai Mainly that it will usually just reread all the files you stripped out again anyway. \n\nI’m not arguing with you it’s something I was interested in exploring too but I never quite got it right",

      "created_at": "2026-03-05 16:23:56+00:00",
      "text": "@levelsio @X I’m getting about 1000$ a month, 2M impressions a week. 7.5% engagement rate \n\n30%+ of my followers have premium. \nI wonder if they published the exact formulas",

    {
      "id": "2029592776820617620",
      "created_at": "2026-03-05 16:20:00+00:00",
      "text": "@vicnaum @banteg @claudeai everything with /compact - I can now surgically strip tool calls/results and thinking blocks while keeping all actual messages intact. \n\nYou’d be destroying the cache which will cost you tons of tokens and make the model less reliable",
      "likes": 21,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2029579972688379928",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "112655719",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2029579972688379928"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2029586236180959406",
      "created_at": "2026-03-05 15:54:01+00:00",
      "text": "I have been using Gemini-3.1-Pro for the last few days in Droid and my conclusion on it and Flash is very different than it use to be. \n\nI really disliked the 2.5-Pro variant because it was incapable in Cursor, the Gemini cli sucks as you all know and the models are unreliable https://t.co/XZvht6bSnE",
      "likes": 168,
      "retweets": 6,
      "replies": 18,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2029586236180959406",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2029567599973945366",
      "created_at": "2026-03-05 14:39:58+00:00",
      "text": "@BasedTeterPhiel 10-30 token/s is measured live. Most of the time the model only needs to output 10-50 tokens for a tool call. \n\nWhat you’re looking for is average TPs, for this model it’s around 70~ for generation",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2029335527292342522",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1935693548239949825",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2029567172863836654"
        }
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "2029553408877752597",
      "created_at": "2026-03-05 13:43:34+00:00",
      "text": "Tests are the most important thing to learn moving forward,  they will make creation automated.\n\n\"Synthetic software is software no one understands because they don't need to. It's designed to fit in a very specific shape, and nothing else. \"\n\nThis may be the last code you see. https://t.co/aTRVTBU4h7",
      "likes": 24,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2029553408877752597",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {

      "created_at": "2026-03-05 13:40:01+00:00",
      "text": "One of the most important videos on youtube, especially the last 5 minutes. \n\nThis guy gets it\nhttps://t.co/dJK3EOo1MF",

      "quotes": 1,

    {
      "id": "2029343058098016472",
      "created_at": "2026-03-04 23:47:43+00:00",
      "text": "@adEvvn It depends, I think you get a lot less tokens with them to be fair. It's not their fault :p but it seems like some providers are okay with their subs being used outside",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 4,

    {
      "id": "2029340603952120098",
      "created_at": "2026-03-04 23:37:57+00:00",
      "text": "@0xKwiss I regenerated after I filmed, thankfully x makes that easy.",
      "likes": 4,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2029335527292342522",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "22988299",
      "is_reply": true,

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2029335527292342522",
      "created_at": "2026-03-04 23:17:47+00:00",
      "text": "Here's what 10k USD gets you:\n\nMiniMax-M2.5 running locally in Droid creates an X bot with the newer X API. It is managing oauth tokens, opening links in my browser, managing it's work etc.. \n\nAmazing technology. https://t.co/72IAlgJN1T",
      "likes": 335,
      "retweets": 6,
      "replies": 20,
      "quotes": 3,
      "conversation_id": "2029335527292342522",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2029333418106871851",
      "created_at": "2026-03-04 23:09:24+00:00",
      "text": "Local MiniMax just did a 30 minute run and built a perfectly functional twitter/x automation system from a scope. What a life. https://t.co/SROa8GI6NZ",
      "likes": 188,
      "retweets": 7,
      "replies": 14,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2029333418106871851",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2029305131859161536",
      "created_at": "2026-03-04 21:17:00+00:00",
      "text": "Thanks @LLMJunky https://t.co/FtXYMobB9O",
      "likes": 12,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2029305128084218265",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2027032974202421336"
        },
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2029305128084218265"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2029305128084218265",
      "created_at": "2026-03-04 21:16:59+00:00",
      "text": "I think I‘ve used Spark more than anyone at this point. I see real-time as being a huge shift in how we work. \n\nFor any fast model, and especially models served by Cerebras you need to change your strategy. \n\nRead this and Will’s article for everything you need to know. https://t.co/Qxfql8SxiR",
      "likes": 226,
      "retweets": 6,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 2,
      "conversation_id": "2029305128084218265",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2029293824221299089"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2029279250260349126",
      "created_at": "2026-03-04 19:34:10+00:00",
      "text": "@jomisjm I love Pi, I use it in all my Ai codebases",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2028981764924788783",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "148299125",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2029279129447620690",
      "created_at": "2026-03-04 19:33:41+00:00",
      "text": "@LuciferMornens You have to pay 20$ a month to access the app but I think it’s more than worth it. I use it with vibeproxy so paying the 20$ for access and I get 20m tokens",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2029210267272249518",

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2029262857305518268",
      "created_at": "2026-03-04 18:29:01+00:00",
      "text": "@justBill You can make one, it’s built on cliproxyapi which is open source and agnostic",
      "likes": 1,

    {
      "id": "2029247718330122638",
      "created_at": "2026-03-04 17:28:52+00:00",
      "text": "Here's I deep dive into my personal AI infrastructure, if you're wondering what self hosting looks like this video is for you. \n\nI also go into why I chose Droid as my daily driver.\nhttps://t.co/96ygKVHcQu",
      "likes": 98,
      "retweets": 9,
      "replies": 5,
      "quotes": 3,
      "conversation_id": "2029247718330122638",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2029224809872138520",
      "created_at": "2026-03-04 15:57:50+00:00",
      "text": "If factory adds some form of folders feature so these can be better grouped and organized as well as a file viewer man it'd be viable to do all my work in here. \n\nAnyway REAP on MLX in the cooking, it's going to be so easy and cheap to produce custom compressions on Mac so hyped https://t.co/ShzsUbz29Q",
      "likes": 45,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 12,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2029224809872138520",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2029210267272249518",
      "created_at": "2026-03-04 15:00:03+00:00",
      "text": "Oh my god I love sub-agents as visible threads. https://t.co/ds7YH3fEgd",
      "likes": 121,
      "retweets": 3,
      "replies": 10,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2029210267272249518",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2029193014250295716",
      "created_at": "2026-03-04 13:51:29+00:00",
      "text": "I interviewed the creator of Oh My Opencode, we've been seeing an increased amount of reports of semi-automated companies or departments. \n\n- Models helping in their own creation \n- Companies dropping entire departments \n\nI think it's an important convo\n\nhttps://t.co/S2Ebq9tFxd",
      "likes": 47,
      "retweets": 4,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2029193014250295716",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },

    {
      "id": "2029093769111375929",
      "created_at": "2026-03-04 07:17:07+00:00",
      "text": "@sayodotfun @Yampeleg It works really good for me, there are some issues in vibeproxy and cliproxy under that cause these errors I fixed them locally",

    {
      "id": "2028991466723323958",
      "created_at": "2026-03-04 00:30:37+00:00",
      "text": "@kanisverma I have it’s sooo fast and really good. I use the authors agent loop and core in a lot of my codebases. \n\nNothing bad to say about it, just from an end result standpoint droid takes care of more of the steering for me",
      "likes": 11,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2028981764924788783",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "928990902701277184",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2028990782787731905"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2028989396930699539",
      "created_at": "2026-03-04 00:22:23+00:00",
      "text": "This is one of the best things I've ever done \n\n1. Install all my data from X\n2. Rag it \n3. Get LLMs to analyse my performance\n4. Give me advice on how to grow \n5. output with the visual-explainer skill. https://t.co/L7c2w6OCmM",
      "likes": 80,
      "retweets": 3,
      "replies": 5,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2028989396930699539",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2028986921360499094",
      "created_at": "2026-03-04 00:12:33+00:00",
      "text": "@Yampeleg 1. It forces the model to use a to-do constantly\n2. Good consistent LSP\n3, good skills to start off\n4. the hooks system is nice\n5. I can use Claude and Codex in the same session so prompt codex, then in same session switch to claude and resend history, jump between the two. \n6.",
      "likes": 43,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2028981764924788783",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "634339745",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2028984469382717585"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2028981764924788783",
      "created_at": "2026-03-03 23:52:04+00:00",
      "text": "Droid is the best agent harness based on outcomes, closely followed by Codex. I have stopped using everything else, mostly except for a few one offs. \n\nI would highly recommend trying it out with BYOK + VibeProxy https://t.co/6PsPM6RmSL\n\nYou can use all your subs in there https://t.co/pGOPZYAzeU",
      "likes": 314,
      "retweets": 14,
      "replies": 31,
      "quotes": 3,
      "conversation_id": "2028981764924788783",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2028970022702358701",
      "created_at": "2026-03-03 23:05:24+00:00",

    {
      "id": "2028838648670527692",
      "created_at": "2026-03-03 14:23:22+00:00",
      "text": "Any tech event organizers in London comment here, i need some help",
      "likes": 9,

    {
      "id": "2028836348967235995",
      "created_at": "2026-03-03 14:14:14+00:00",
      "text": "I don’t understand making AI respond to people on your Twitter or whatever. \n\nIt is so incredibly cringe and atrocious to have these things running around on social media. \n\nNo one likes it no one wants to read it",
      "likes": 46,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 9,

    {
      "id": "2028832266378453222",
      "created_at": "2026-03-03 13:58:00+00:00",
      "text": "Let me make your life easier. \n\n1. In claude code, codex, opencode etc.. setup this skill. https://t.co/tLZRUDlPOO\n\n2. Instead of prompting for something in 1 way, ask the model to generate variations of what you are trying to make. \n\n3. Use the frontend-design skill to get https://t.co/ta52MOmzfp",
      "likes": 995,
      "retweets": 63,
      "replies": 24,
      "quotes": 2,
      "conversation_id": "2028832266378453222",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {

      "text": "@george__wing I have loved it most for scripting, tbh. I just make it build out utility scripts and do/find things for me",

    {
      "id": "2028804722367324525",
      "created_at": "2026-03-03 12:08:33+00:00",
      "text": "This took 30 seconds, ON LOW thinking. The entire website works, the images get modified, I can download them, it has all the features. like IDEK how that's possible.\n\nThe idea with these fast models is trying to figure out how to pull what they know out of them, instead of",
      "likes": 13,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2028803750119211486",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2028803750119211486"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2028803750119211486",
      "created_at": "2026-03-03 12:04:42+00:00",
      "text": "This is one of the most amazing things I've ever seen. 1x speed. Codex Spark continues to bamboozle me. \n\nIt's difficult to operate but man if you can figure it out you can iterate faster than any other model on the market right now. https://t.co/ZtIxc60RZa",
      "likes": 77,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 5,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2028803750119211486",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2028803290587177251",
      "created_at": "2026-03-03 12:02:52+00:00",
      "text": "This is my goat here. \n\nOne of the most brilliant thinkers of our time, excellent communicator. I've lived around the globe and I feel like he gets it. https://t.co/UGqKjTxhoB",
      "likes": 21098,
      "retweets": 1943,
      "replies": 137,
      "quotes": 21,
      "conversation_id": "2028803290587177251",

    {
      "id": "2028751441205113301",
      "created_at": "2026-03-03 08:36:50+00:00",
      "text": "The dream of the tech industry is to allow the human consciousness to experience everything it ever wanted. \n\nVideos like these are meant as memes, or satire but really it’s a sign of what’s to come. \n\nAI, Metaverse, digital currency, video games, graphic cards, neuralink, https://t.co/uKoQKljkBy",

    {

      "text": "@VictorTaelin You want Pi",

    {
      "id": "2028530680666194267",
      "created_at": "2026-03-02 17:59:37+00:00",
      "text": "How come the only harness that could get GLM-5 working smoothly via oauth/key is opencode? \n\nGenerally speaking Opencode just works, I love that. https://t.co/ylwR7qGnfd",
      "likes": 196,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 12,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2028530680666194267",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2028529389663551784",
      "created_at": "2026-03-02 17:54:29+00:00",
      "text": "One of my biggest super-powers is using AI to crop, edit, stitch, overlay and improve videos and images. \n\nIt is wild I can do that. \n\nRe-motion &amp; ffmpeg are insanely useful to AIs, I will never do it myself again, even local models do this well. https://t.co/F7CbOad5Zx",
      "likes": 175,
      "retweets": 4,
      "replies": 13,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2028529389663551784",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2028498518059429898",
      "created_at": "2026-03-02 15:51:49+00:00",
      "text": "@fractal_friend I do use it a ton, and I do love it, I'd say Pro is the best value plan RN regardless of Spark. \n\nYou have to keep in mind that the model doesn't have vision, has shorter context, and is best used as a way to significantly speed up 5.3-codex via subagents for exploration etc..",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2028493180853792819",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1570197168614674433",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2028497755166101538"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2028497145599340608",
      "created_at": "2026-03-02 15:46:21+00:00",

      "retweets": 0,

      "created_at": "2026-03-02 15:30:36+00:00",
      "text": "This was all built with Codex, no looking at the code beyond the shape of it. \n\nWorks fully, messy but does the job. https://t.co/G7PGgfYmsX",

      "quotes": 0,

      "created_at": "2026-03-02 13:47:15+00:00",
      "text": "Damn, I might have to give Telegram another chance. https://t.co/QrJggrz5dx",
      "likes": 109,
      "retweets": 1,

    {
      "id": "2028466373354009003",
      "created_at": "2026-03-02 13:44:05+00:00",
      "text": "This week:\n\nVLLM\n\n1. Harden \n    - added Daytona for filesystem management. \n    - added rocm support\n    - sglang/vllm/llama.cpp support\n2. Started with the broadcasting to split models over instances, but this is extremely complex and above my current capacity. will revisit\n3. https://t.co/lBhWhzpVRc",
      "likes": 44,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 5,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2028466373354009003",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2028462662649061706",
      "created_at": "2026-03-02 13:29:20+00:00",
      "text": "When you send a message to an llm and something fails, you screenshot that prompt and response into another model to debug it will almost always not just fix the issue you ran into but also do whatever you prompted model A to do. \n\nIt's very interesting how common this is.",
      "likes": 5,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2028462662649061706",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2028456366277349853",
      "created_at": "2026-03-02 13:04:19+00:00",
      "text": "God damn https://t.co/UjMwAaP12b",
      "likes": 21,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2028456366277349853",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false

      "created_at": "2026-03-02 11:19:29+00:00",
      "text": "@_m0se_ @thefp4brain My preference is W4A16 autoround, it tends to hold up the best",

    {
      "id": "2028429910608413120",
      "created_at": "2026-03-02 11:19:11+00:00",
      "text": "@_m0se_ I need it quantized to 4bits, I send @thefp4brain on a mission to quantize your reap, so we will see. \n\nYou did a great job it holds up really really well, I had it do over 200 tool calls with 0 errors in 1 agent turn. It's very logical, outputs are strong, and can code &amp; reason",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,

    {
      "id": "2028426596068085894",
      "created_at": "2026-03-02 11:06:01+00:00",
      "text": "@wnmdev 8x 3090S. This is a compressed version of the biggest one. I am installing and testing the smaller ones from today - end of the week. \n\nI think they will all achieve similar performance, with less general intelligence.",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2028415828161442124",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1317947875867922432",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2028425678639583701"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2028418996677042558",
      "created_at": "2026-03-02 10:35:49+00:00",
      "text": "None of the code editors performance wise were built to withstand the sheer throughput of AI agents. \n\n1. Zed struggles with an issue when re-indexing and checking files, I have a memory crash every 3 days. \n2. VSCode and it's forks have issues with GPU acceleration, and crash 2",
      "likes": 110,
      "retweets": 5,
      "replies": 32,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2028418996677042558",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2028415828161442124",
      "created_at": "2026-03-02 10:23:14+00:00",
      "text": "300+ Tokens/s batch 8 https://t.co/yeSMCnBwuN",
      "likes": 40,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 4,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2028415828161442124",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false
    },

      "created_at": "2026-03-01 22:23:24+00:00",
      "text": "If this is the case we will have the best openclaw model, the best digital assistant, the best orchestrator. \n\nThis is going to be awesome. Nothing has powered me up more than the GPT-5 family of models https://t.co/pMPn3WNJUs",
      "likes": 129,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 6,
      "quotes": 0,

      "id": "2028230784931250470",
      "created_at": "2026-03-01 22:07:56+00:00",
      "text": "@Teknium Yeah I saw you have skills for coding agents I think that’s the right way to do it. What is really really interesting to me is the mlops skills. \n\nI am using the benchmarking one right now to benchmark the model that is operating Hermes lol. \n\nIt feels like a tutor to me rn",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,

      "id": "2028078680115642471",
      "created_at": "2026-03-01 12:03:31+00:00",
      "text": "@anasibnanwar the model selects the thinking budget, or you can configure it \n\nso you'd have 3 models \n\ngpt-5.3-codex-low\ngpt-5.3-codex-xhigh \ngpt-5.3-codex-medium \n\nall of this should be pointing the same model with different configs",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2028044153729192179",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1625280993966923777",
      "is_reply": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2028076710437261515"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2028074544611652013",
      "created_at": "2026-03-01 11:47:05+00:00",
      "text": "@anasibnanwar 128000 yes it’s automatic. \n\nYou can probably make a hook to modify thinking budgets you can also configure 3 gpt-5.3-codex with various thinking budgets",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2028044153729192179",

    {
      "id": "2028044153729192179",
      "created_at": "2026-03-01 09:46:20+00:00",
      "text": "I have been running Qwen-3.5-Plus reaped and quantized for the last few days\n\nThis is the first time I have successfully used a local model for coding on production projects. \n\nhttps://t.co/TKWgejkiVP\n\nHere you can see it doing complex full-stack tool calls and checks, not 1 tool https://t.co/tFTFrZsdfy",
      "likes": 403,
      "retweets": 20,
      "replies": 26,
      "quotes": 4,
      "conversation_id": "2028044153729192179",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
