    {
      "id": "2028022232048402801",
      "created_at": "2026-03-01 08:19:13+00:00",
      "text": "My engagement rate is extremely high, my impressions went from 3M over 9 months to 12M in 3 months \n\nHere’s what I changed: \n\n1. Posting much more often \n2. Joined a bunch of communities on X\n3. Started making deep dives sharing info\n4. Stopped engaging (mostly) in politics\n5. I https://t.co/Zl4qlL1c4v",
      "likes": 134,
      "retweets": 4,
      "replies": 19,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2028022232048402801",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2028016343396581769",
      "created_at": "2026-03-01 07:55:49+00:00",
      "text": "@JJismusic Kimi is an amazing model, opus is definitely the gold tier for computer use but the cost difference is astronomical. \n\nThe openclaw problem is context management is broken. If you don’t clean up constantly it’ll take up over half of your context window",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2027754294510784697",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "73039727",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2028014916590768476"
        }
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "2027899745373487319",

    {
      "id": "2027893251731038487",
      "created_at": "2026-02-28 23:46:42+00:00",
      "text": "If you want to fight me this is who you have to go through, I am being spied on by all sides, including France &lt;3 https://t.co/0sVM5SlOow",

    {
      "id": "2027892081331487196",
      "created_at": "2026-02-28 23:42:03+00:00",
      "text": "@Hueglist No they have made it available",
      "likes": 1,

    {
      "id": "2027890477639361001",
      "created_at": "2026-02-28 23:35:40+00:00",
      "text": "@HotAisle It boggles my mind the economics of this industry, how can I hammer away at this massive model with massive contexts 14 hours a day everyday with multiple concurrent sessions, i know the power for that alone is higher than 40$",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2027754294510784697",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1714688881311051778",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2027890110453395605"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2027888762009571436",
      "created_at": "2026-02-28 23:28:51+00:00",
      "text": "Droid's skills are possibly the best general skills you can use that'll help in any repo or on any project. \n\nThere's room for cartridges of high value context, skills for complex tools etc.. but most of it is slop and/or not made for you. https://t.co/wNOmtn9Cxx",
      "likes": 199,
      "retweets": 9,
      "replies": 7,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2027888762009571436",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2027888348195684580",
      "created_at": "2026-02-28 23:27:13+00:00",
      "text": "@DevMuzzammil Honestly at this point even copilot is enough to crush, I like Droid. Their branding speaks to my digital preferences.",
      "likes": 7,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2027883023228903437",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1517660231807152128",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2027886463703257399"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2027883023228903437",
      "created_at": "2026-02-28 23:06:03+00:00",
      "text": "Let me make your life a little easier. \n\nThis is the easiest and cheapest way to get all the AI.\nYou need a way to get everything in one place, take a look here. I have my:\n\n- Claude Max \n- GPT Pro \n- Kimi Allegreto\n- Copilot (good as a sub)\n- Antigravity\n- Gemini\n- ZAI \n- My https://t.co/eiUp6WJKQS",
      "likes": 513,
      "retweets": 20,
      "replies": 28,
      "quotes": 3,
      "conversation_id": "2027883023228903437",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2027878815494516893",
      "created_at": "2026-02-28 22:49:20+00:00",
      "text": "@rungkad Kimi",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2027754294510784697",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "193072051",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [

          "type": "replied_to",

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2027875713060094220",
      "created_at": "2026-02-28 22:37:00+00:00",
      "text": "@ExponentialTom Aave",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2027705544753005016",

    {
      "id": "2027875538228961349",
      "created_at": "2026-02-28 22:36:19+00:00",
      "text": "A wave of interest in local AI is about to hit so hard, right in time for the M5 ultra. \n\nI love my GPUs and am eternally grateful to how much they’ve taught me, I just think Apple is destined to win consumer AI hardware. \n\nStrangely enough they’re the cheap ones now https://t.co/VMyfCFs9Q3 https://t.co/qNhMjtnzXd",
      "likes": 351,
      "retweets": 21,
      "replies": 20,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2027875538228961349",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2027815923684392973"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2027874493348155788",
      "created_at": "2026-02-28 22:32:09+00:00",
      "text": "@max_paperclips @allTheYud Kimi",

      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,

    {
      "id": "2027865612408549586",
      "created_at": "2026-02-28 21:56:52+00:00",
      "text": "Guys we have 100 sign ups in the last 24 hours that is wild. \n\nI am excited about this event it’s going to be a highlight of 2026. \n\nIf you’re in the UK sign up, the week is packed full of events https://t.co/C1yeDSiqAR",
      "likes": 43,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,

        }
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "2027820549158650095",
      "created_at": "2026-02-28 18:57:48+00:00",
      "text": "@osaki_io kimi cli\nparchi\ndroid\nopencode\npi",

    {
      "id": "2027820067249918069",
      "created_at": "2026-02-28 18:55:53+00:00",
      "text": "This project started as a voice note into openclaw, it dispatched codex and built this over 3 days of back and forth. I moved it to my mac to check the code. \n\nIt's clunky but it works perfectly https://t.co/4isnyshj27",
      "likes": 20,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2027820062288040018",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2027820062288040018"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2027820062288040018",
      "created_at": "2026-02-28 18:55:52+00:00",
      "text": "My goal for the year: make local AI easy and pleasant to use, on your phone, laptop, coding agents, discord, browser and even on ESP. \n\nYou will be able to talk to an Apple watch, run a local model on call, get it coding for you, etc.. \n\nKimi on 150gb vram\nGLM-5 on 150gb vram https://t.co/nzOHARWk6m",
      "likes": 429,
      "retweets": 14,
      "replies": 32,
      "quotes": 2,
      "conversation_id": "2027820062288040018",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2027815841111114109",
      "created_at": "2026-02-28 18:39:06+00:00",
      "text": "@Wald52Wald Get in touch with me",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2027807828593803744",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1769037882532970496",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {

          "id": "2027811761378804070"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2027807828593803744",
      "created_at": "2026-02-28 18:07:15+00:00",
      "text": "Something fun is coming. quantisation, reap, compression will be something anyone can do for max a few 100$ https://t.co/sCXpoFK7Qo",

    {
      "id": "2027790527295303929",
      "created_at": "2026-02-28 16:58:30+00:00",
      "text": "https://t.co/aYhtXqB9Ka's canvas so quickly integrates the latest SOTA media generation models it's crazy.\n\nMust be a lot of work getting google models up so quickly, I have been using this for thumbnail, I don't use chat much but the media gen is the easiest out there. https://t.co/JvPWyst3Kr",
      "likes": 22,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2027790527295303929",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

    {
      "id": "2027769955551174732",
      "created_at": "2026-02-28 15:36:46+00:00",
      "text": "@cryptopatrick Learn data structures and algorithms, pick a niche, if you’re interested in coding specifically use tools that let you do that. \n\nZed is very good for that.\n\nThere’s new infrastructure like convex that makes developing with LLMs much easier. \n\nLearn how to learn, and get",
      "likes": 4,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2027726778471428340",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "3242998727",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2027767525606330840"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2027765617105785154",
      "created_at": "2026-02-28 15:19:31+00:00",
      "text": "@Joash0x I don’t have enough hardware to run Kimi",
      "likes": 5,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2027754294510784697",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "716690790919983104",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2027761230161576067"
        }
      ]

    {
      "id": "2027754294510784697",
      "created_at": "2026-02-28 14:34:32+00:00",
      "text": "I pay 40$/mo and have not even once ran out and I hammer it all day. \n\nKimi is the best model in Parchi, beyond a doubt https://t.co/7MbYtoUfxB",
      "likes": 292,
      "retweets": 10,
      "replies": 32,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2027754294510784697",

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2027729085980414279",
      "created_at": "2026-02-28 12:54:22+00:00",
      "text": "@barry_nevio I know and agree that’s why I’m impressed they stuck to their choice anyway",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2027537336351784991",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1537999210485673984",

    {
      "id": "2027726778471428340",
      "created_at": "2026-02-28 12:45:11+00:00",
      "text": "This will make your life better if you are a coder, or  trying to learn\n\nAgentic coding is more than people admit to. It is not a toy, nor a waste of time\n\nThere will always be room for artisan coders, but there will also be room for vibe-engineers\n\nhttps://t.co/zpKE4KjiRI",
      "likes": 60,
      "retweets": 3,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2027726778471428340",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2027726207412818105",
      "created_at": "2026-02-28 12:42:55+00:00",
      "text": "Here's why I quit building my orchestration tooling, despite really enjoying it. \n\nI feel that there is a productivity craze going on, everyone wants to build the whole world over. Especially me, I don't think that it's productive for 99.9% of devs.\n\nhttps://t.co/zpKE4KjiRI https://t.co/MOssOShaPn",
      "likes": 91,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 7,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2027726207412818105",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2027377489903755502"
        }
      ]
    },

      "created_at": "2026-02-28 11:20:49+00:00",
      "text": "Sol at 79$\n\nDamn, we really are going into a crypto winter. \nNot even worth selling at this point. https://t.co/cdvwsgOixm",

    {
      "id": "2027540972309971181",
      "created_at": "2026-02-28 00:26:52+00:00",
      "text": "I am literally doing things that look like fiction with Codex everyday. \n\nI have seen it change literal weights and fixing 5gb tensor files it’s insane. \n\nI have built and deployed novel things and solved complex issues across multiple industries using this model https://t.co/LBc71DEdfu",
      "likes": 76,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2027540972309971181",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2027300218589614194"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2027540088716951831",
      "created_at": "2026-02-28 00:23:21+00:00",
      "text": "Yeeeees https://t.co/SUNLKOolte",
      "likes": 10,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2027540088716951831",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2027539364935340541",
      "created_at": "2026-02-28 00:20:29+00:00",
      "text": "@Yampeleg I love to think of context as a room you need to organise perfectly for your use-case. \n\nThe environment (directory, harness, and documentation) must be minimal, and serve the purpose of the task at hand. \n\nEach layer in a directory must be self contained and replicable",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2027451291425796510",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "634339745",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2027451291425796510"
        }
      ]
    },

      "created_at": "2026-02-27 23:21:03+00:00",
      "text": "@adam_danielczyk Thank you, I think doing a two part talk\n\n50% intro and latest news \n\nThen break for socializing \n\n50% for a single usecase focus \n\nThen open forum. \n\nI’d love to get other people to talk too i",

    {
      "id": "2027517834461344038",
      "created_at": "2026-02-27 22:54:55+00:00",
      "text": "@ykruhlyk Thanks for the feedback I appreciate it, a lot of what I talked about applies to all LLMs and how I think about AI. \n\nStructured topics will be something I work hard on next time. \n\nI would say remotion is one of the biggest unlocks for me and has been wonderful with cc",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2027494257158766986",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "548292599",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2027517033592525254"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2027517257459421283",
      "created_at": "2026-02-27 22:52:38+00:00",
      "text": "Any bros from the UK want to help me spread the word on an event I’m helping organise? \n\nIt’s for London https://t.co/pZMJLUumbj\n\nHoping to get 500 attendees 😳",
      "likes": 181,
      "retweets": 37,
      "replies": 35,
      "quotes": 6,
      "conversation_id": "2027517257459421283",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2027513027377979603",
      "created_at": "2026-02-27 22:35:49+00:00",
      "text": "@Tajchert Thanks a lot for the feedback! \n\nI had to rush a lot with this one and the venue was not the best. \n\nVery hard to find the right spaces in this city",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2027494257158766986",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "87698337",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2027508243115684141"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2027512692257276291",
      "created_at": "2026-02-27 22:34:29+00:00",
      "text": "@WaveCut @Tajchert Thank you for the feedback, I will make sure to do something about that next time",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2027494257158766986",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "28401886",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2027509513511965020"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2027494257158766986",
      "created_at": "2026-02-27 21:21:14+00:00",
      "text": "For all those Polish bros that came to Claude Code Warsaw, please let me know how it went. \n\nI forgot to take a picture lmao",
      "likes": 37,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 12,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2027494257158766986",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2027463813109371188",
      "created_at": "2026-02-27 19:20:16+00:00",
      "text": "Who’s all going to be in London next month? \n\n https://t.co/pZMJLUumbj",
      "likes": 16,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,

    {
      "id": "2027393592306590197",
      "created_at": "2026-02-27 14:41:14+00:00",
      "text": "https://t.co/rP89MYFdyo",
      "likes": 6,
      "retweets": 0,

    {
      "id": "2027393589030896103",
      "created_at": "2026-02-27 14:41:13+00:00",
      "text": "This is going to be monumental for OPEN SOURCE AI, you guys have no idea \n\nhttps://t.co/5dxQwaQfjB",
      "likes": 48,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2027393589030896103",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,

    },
    {
      "id": "2027377489903755502",
      "created_at": "2026-02-27 13:37:15+00:00",
      "text": "I have to admit, I was tool-maxing, gas-town adjacent because I wanted to just make more happen, faster.\n\nI have since read this, and the follow up to it multiple times, and I have learned a lot. I have to say it is more productive to like this.\n\nhttps://t.co/hbdzFPxtGF",
      "likes": 572,
      "retweets": 28,
      "replies": 11,
      "quotes": 6,
      "conversation_id": "2027377489903755502",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2027376990634791017",
      "created_at": "2026-02-27 13:35:16+00:00",
      "text": "@alfieventi copy paste the screenshots to opencode free and https://t.co/hbdzFPxtGF",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2027119397563683315",

    {
      "id": "2027361577687552015",
      "created_at": "2026-02-27 12:34:01+00:00",
      "text": "People don't understand the consequences of a video like this.\n\nhttps://t.co/4un9HbA9Cr https://t.co/TQAuyzUnZa",

    {
      "id": "2027359421756522596",
      "created_at": "2026-02-27 12:25:27+00:00",
      "text": "To the people that dislike Dario’s priorities, decisions, and words. \n\nKeep in mind that a company is not made of 1 person and very often most people at a company do not agree with its decisions. \n\nThe internet at the scale these companies are operating in is a nightmare. https://t.co/L0LvvBd9ez",
      "likes": 26,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2027359421756522596",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2027356123754365116"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2027356123754365116",
      "created_at": "2026-02-27 12:12:21+00:00",
      "text": "@mufaro_dev I want to educated people on AI, it’s delusional to think that Claude is not the easiest thing for the masses to use. \n\nI genuinely believe those who can’t operate these things will have a harder time in life, it is a skill and it is increasingly critical. \n\nI appreciate the",
      "likes": 3,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2027314054843269314",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1827489673817600000",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2027352753400381599"
        }
      ]
    },

      "id": "2027308829449318433",
      "created_at": "2026-02-27 09:04:25+00:00",
      "text": "@StefanoPeirano Qwen3.5-REAP-212B-W4A16 (4 bits) \nQwen3.5-122B (8bits)\n\nI prefer bigger models that are highly compressed over accurate precision smaller model",
      "likes": 8,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2027154552491786527",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "405095458",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2027305027044327784"
        }
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "2027162272792011241",
      "created_at": "2026-02-26 23:22:03+00:00",
      "text": "@jxnlco Codex is already great for long running task, let us decide how long to stick it on a task. \n\nSome usage limit indicator in the terminal would be great. \n\nPlan mode is just not so great, we need plans locally, todos aren’t used often.\n\nHotkeys maybe.",
      "likes": 7,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2027128990532796510",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "17203692",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2027128990532796510"
        }
      ]
    },

      "id": "2027146574288003093",

    {
      "id": "2027144251348308187",
      "created_at": "2026-02-26 22:10:26+00:00",
      "text": "@Yampeleg Switch to using agent-browser by Vercel or try Parchi out.\n\nIt has a relay mode that lets the model connect and control any browser you have Parchi on. \n\n(It works for Firefox and chrome) \n\nYou’d need to run the repo locally and get it configured once and it should be good",
      "likes": 6,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2027119397563683315",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "634339745",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2027139899959177579"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2027138773771841719",

    {
      "id": "2027119397563683315",
      "created_at": "2026-02-26 20:31:41+00:00",
      "text": "A week in the life of OpenClaw\n\n#1 Added KimiClaw to my server, like a model to compare against, i have been using to benchmark against my local models.\n\n#2 Back to GPT-5.3-Codex as the main driver, it's just more intelligent and I value that over ease of use. It's also so much https://t.co/wZT9l4QDfl",
      "likes": 145,
      "retweets": 5,
      "replies": 15,
      "quotes": 2,
      "conversation_id": "2027119397563683315",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

      "id": "2027057786975105498",
      "created_at": "2026-02-26 16:26:51+00:00",
      "text": "@bnjmn_marie I might be talking out of my ass here, but I think the reason for this is MiniMax does not have a shared expert. \n\nI’ve found this with Reap as well, I’ve found all the REAPs I’ve done with models that have shared experts hold up really well",
      "likes": 9,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,

      "id": "2027053481253654788",
      "created_at": "2026-02-26 16:09:45+00:00",
      "text": "Let me make your life better. \n\nDo you spam Claude Code all day? Are you struggling to keep track of all your projects, terminals, windows etc.. \n\nDo you need a browser in your terminal? Fully functional browser with it's own tabs and bookmarks. \n\nDo you multitask? Do you like https://t.co/pigec4l9ty",
      "likes": 338,
      "retweets": 10,
      "replies": 27,
      "quotes": 3,
      "conversation_id": "2027053481253654788",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

    {
      "id": "2027036837345214733",
      "created_at": "2026-02-26 15:03:37+00:00",
      "text": "@richardanaya @thefp4brain Kimi is much more accurate than it, it navigates in a really logical way. \n\nLike look how this model navigates to Google to search instead of just searching.. \n\nIt fails to click buttons but is tenacious enough to stay at it. \n\nDefinitely a cheaper option",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2027028502520709471",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1345251820650725376",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2027036383739846848"
        }
      ]
    },

      "id": "2026954702886187089",
      "created_at": "2026-02-26 09:37:14+00:00",
      "text": "Here's Qwen-3.5-212B-W4A16 looking at 6 images of legal letters I received, doing 5 batched tool calls to read online on specific USCIS details. \n\nIn the same chat analyzing an image of my IBKR account. Maintaining the point 3 turns of back and forth in. \n\nThis model fits on:\n\n- https://t.co/8WCBOfqrD3",
      "likes": 57,
      "retweets": 3,

      "text": "@interpreter_ai I use opencode, droid, codex and PI the most, some harnesses just do better at some tasks. \n\nThis kind of thing chews through context fast, which Droid handles well",

    {
      "id": "2026939568037954032",
      "created_at": "2026-02-26 08:37:06+00:00",
      "text": "@ExponentialTom Would be happy to",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,

    {
      "id": "2026937790357787135",
      "created_at": "2026-02-26 08:30:02+00:00",
      "text": "Jailbreaking will be one of the top cyber security exploits of the next 10 years. \n\nIf a model can ssh into devices, configure complex servers, manage security at scale (almost all of them), and it can be jail broken? (All of them) \n\nThen how much can they actively damage? https://t.co/yN4qaPhHH1",
      "likes": 85,
      "retweets": 7,
      "replies": 4,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026937790357787135",

        }

    {
      "id": "2026813452300247449",
      "created_at": "2026-02-26 00:15:58+00:00",
      "text": "I have been thinking a lot lately about onboarding, it’s so easy to just make things now. They’re not even demos!\n\nPeople write off AI generated applications and codebases, preferring hand crafted artisanal code.\n\nPeople say well how can I onboard onto this repo, it’s too",
      "likes": 29,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026813452300247449",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2026805260300238913",
      "created_at": "2026-02-25 23:43:24+00:00",
      "text": "Dude, let me make your life better.\n\n1. Setup the Figma remote MCP\n2. Ask a model to build you a brand guide (easy)\n3. Ask the model to build you components in figma\n4. Model can pull from Figma and use it to build\n     - sites\n     - graphs\n     - banners\n     - visuals\n5. Setup https://t.co/synehp49MU",
      "likes": 897,
      "retweets": 31,
      "replies": 26,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026805260300238913",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2026766442826252643",
      "created_at": "2026-02-25 21:09:10+00:00",
      "text": "@lucidpaths33 vLLM-studio it’s on my GitHub",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026602245844865051",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1609812294548033536",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2026761981798039818"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2026766330964086855",
      "created_at": "2026-02-25 21:08:43+00:00",
      "text": "Imagine a machine that takes research papers in and sends out Spark to validate then, build them into products. \n\nOr having Spark running synthesizing new expressions, brute forcing science over seconds. https://t.co/6YV93iMb16",
      "likes": 65,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026766330964086855",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2026761867813859388"
        }
      ]
    },

      "created_at": "2026-02-25 11:10:45+00:00",
      "text": "@txhno You can sign up with email and password and buy some credits through the app itself and lmk if it’s difficult. Don’t use the Google or GitHub oauth tho. \n\nYou can use any coding plan, it’s in settings in the auth tab, if you want to bring your own make a new profile",

      "retweets": 0,

      "id": "2026441927957033454",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 23:39:39+00:00",
      "text": "When Slopus gets dumb, you know there’s a new model coming. https://t.co/1fdDS5lvI3",

    {
      "id": "2026431684665610252",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 22:58:57+00:00",
      "text": "@Hesamation Been doing this for 6 months with Termius",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026419178911990035",

    {
      "id": "2026431241206079490",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 22:57:11+00:00",
      "text": "@KAIbosh_1 32 is fine 600 token/s prefill minimum",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026402520738812315",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1182239968485347328",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2026430696529551611"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2026428285987504143",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 22:45:27+00:00",
      "text": "Yes. Everything I build now must go through this process. \n\nAll prompts go like:\n\n1. Write new tests\n2. Model records using the UI (vid)\n3. Build out features \n4. Model must record new video\n5. Review it against original\n6. Send results \n\nI built this into Parchi with Relay mode https://t.co/NhozKOgsEb",
      "likes": 102,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026428285987504143",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2026369424450523348"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2026427000273912094",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 22:40:20+00:00",
      "text": "@LLMJunky @cerebras I’m writing a much longer technical piece and this is one of my main points. \n\nIt speed up Codex 3x by doing all the exploration and small tasks",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026362242703208790",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "2004675530935926784",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2026389105001550099"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2026426760733053218",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 22:39:23+00:00",
      "text": "@softpoo @cerebras No it’s fine, I use it in a bunch of harnesses. I just use Codex very often these days",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026362242703208790",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "27064376",

      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2026394717349621923"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2026426459770728483",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 22:38:11+00:00",
      "text": "@_ibarz @cerebras It’s literally in the thread",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026362242703208790",

      "is_retweet": false,

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2026421940731150420",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 22:20:14+00:00",
      "text": "@KAIbosh_1 I have 8x 3090s\n\nFor gguf 32 gen 200 prefill",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026402520738812315",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1182239968485347328",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2026419993617793234"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2026411921331859860",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 21:40:25+00:00",
      "text": "300$ for first person to get an W4A16 or AWQ-4bit out with standard pile10k calibration dataset. \n\nCheck my GLM-4.7 Reap quant out for exact instructions and info. \n\nShould take 3 hours and require 50$ in compute down up front. \n\nhttps://t.co/01Jyl9WvAr",
      "likes": 66,
      "retweets": 3,
      "replies": 6,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026411921331859860",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2026402520738812315",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 21:03:04+00:00",
      "text": "Qwen-3.5 models are really really impressive, I haven't had a single tool call error, or issue with Qwen in Parchi. \n\nIt's been able to review a few sites, do deep research on me, I got 0 refusals which had been an issue for me with Qwen models in the past. \n\nMain issue is speed https://t.co/74Ho5jLy2H",
      "likes": 131,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 10,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026402520738812315",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2026398115469623317",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 20:45:34+00:00",
      "text": "@wassollichhier benchmarks take soooooooooooooooooooooooooooooo long. I ain't got that time.",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026396850069147920",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "47870624",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2026397940588159333"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2026396850069147920",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 20:40:32+00:00",
      "text": "Qwen3.5-Reap-212B does great in Parchi. \n\nFits on 192GB-vram, 32 tokens/s amazingly accurate. https://t.co/F054F5V1eK",
      "likes": 52,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 4,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026396850069147920",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2026373402177474728",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 19:07:21+00:00",
      "text": "@royjossfolk @davezmore If we use 8 bits you're right, 16 bits requires 250gb",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026368210094694640",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "264541978",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2026372451551101074"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2026368210094694640",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 18:46:44+00:00",
      "text": "Qwen-3.5-122B is out now, this is the first vision model with this hardware profile since GLM-4.6V\n\nI am really excited I don't have to do any silly shenanigans to fit this on my device.\nhttps://t.co/XT6n30Y2r3",
      "likes": 128,
      "retweets": 6,
      "replies": 9,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026368210094694640",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2026364839065465128",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 18:33:20+00:00",
      "text": "@hlitoroy @cerebras Cerebras mostly uses Open weight models, I think Codex is the first that we can access that isn't.",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026362242703208790",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1541105862609084418",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2026364126952386916"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2026364618180874539",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 18:32:27+00:00",
      "text": "@SNARKAMOTO @cerebras Yeah, the image visibility thing is what hurts me the most. It's definitely not dumber, for the compaction I maintain context externally.\n\n- checklist \n- progress\n- plan\n\nas external markdown files I edit constantly.",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026362242703208790",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1955384873910407170",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2026363363836485662"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2026362281920012522",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 18:23:10+00:00",
      "text": "The underlying theme in all this is the model fundamentally changes how much we can accomplish with AI. \n\n- Incident response\n- Customer support\n- Research &amp; testing\n- Quality assurance\n- Personalised education\n- Computer and browser use\n\nBenefit from realtime intelligence. https://t.co/yoxGT6lYrC",
      "likes": 3,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026362242703208790",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2026362278803575218"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2026362278803575218",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 18:23:09+00:00",
      "text": "I started modifying files via Spark, here you can see it converting .mov files to .mp4s. as well as improving quality and resizing to 1920 x 780. \n\nAll within a minute, think of how many hours this will save, instantly resize and convert files, classify them, etc.. https://t.co/k2TP9gxnfn",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026362242703208790",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2026362275100012856"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2026362271555842291",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 18:23:08+00:00",
      "text": "I tried it in Parchi for browser automation 10/10 \n\nTool calls slow the model down, but it still was able to get me my reports 3x faster it was very accurate at search\n\nI had it shop for me, which worked. But it made 6 deliveries, install of 1 delivery for all 6 items rip $ https://t.co/AEItkdIyX5",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026362242703208790",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2026362268510826590"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2026362268510826590",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 18:23:07+00:00",
      "text": "I use AI a lot for learning, it's often wrong but I find OpenAI's GPT models to be the most accurate and intelligent of the bunch. \n\nI love learning via visual examples, one of my favourite sites is a demo of a blockchain ledger lol\n\nIt built me an educational demo in 15 seconds https://t.co/KnD8tyPCsv",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026362242703208790",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2026362265373429915"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2026362265373429915",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 18:23:06+00:00",
      "text": "Now back to Spark. \n\nI was testing it in vllm-studio\n\n- 75k lines of code\n- 842 files\n- Swift Python Typescript\n\nI just dropped it in there and started asking it questions, mainly because I want to keep slimming these repos down to make them AI friendly\n\nIt onboarded in 1 minute https://t.co/G8m9N7UKG1",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026362242703208790",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2026362262181572780"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2026362262181572780",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 18:23:05+00:00",
      "text": "The AI top moments for me have been:\n\n#1 Dall-e taking the world by strorm (2022)\n#2 GPT-4 can code, and chat (2023)\n#3 Sonnet-3.5 in Cursor Composer (2024)\n#4 Deepseek moment (2025)\n#5 GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark 1500 tokens/s (2026)\n\nEvery year has been jam packed full of new things. https://t.co/fuvCu6ZWLw",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026362242703208790",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2026362258096279707"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2026362258096279707",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 18:23:04+00:00",
      "text": "Regardless of the quality, it was exciting enough that it moved people to create subreddits, blogs, videos, and even memes that turned into benchmarks. \n\nIt wasn't long before you saw articles about Dall-e winning art competitions, and AI generated content being regulated. https://t.co/Sv8lLhtgGI",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026362242703208790",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2026362245836419095"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2026362245836419095",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 18:23:02+00:00",
      "text": "The first time I was nerd-sniped by AI was in August 2022, I saw Dall-e for the first time and instantly my mind went in a hundred directions\n\nI wrote a blog post discussing how we will see a decoupling art and utility though it sucked back then. A lot of it played out perfectly https://t.co/rD9xQMVEHW",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026362242703208790",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2026362242703208790"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2026362242703208790",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 18:23:01+00:00",
      "text": "I have been using GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark all day every day for 2 weeks now. It's incredibly fast (@cerebras)\n\nI had a few moments where I felt I could see the future. So far I've tried it in:\n\n- Zed\n- Openclaw\n- Codex app\n- Codex cli / pi\n\nThis is a clue to where we're heading. https://t.co/6ttaRGtJwQ",
      "likes": 240,
      "retweets": 6,
      "replies": 20,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2026362242703208790",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2026358515971797414",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 18:08:12+00:00",
      "text": "I started modifying files via Spark, here you can see it converting .mov files to .mp4s. as well as improving quality and resizing to 1920 x 780. \n\nAll within a minute, think of what this can do with 100s of files, https://t.co/l5hZkYqcwt",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026358483772211542",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2026358512473813109"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2026358512473813109",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 18:08:11+00:00",
      "text": "One sector I think is going to flip on it's head is cyber-security, Viruses and malware generation and combating is going parabolic from here. \n\nI put Spark against every other model and harness I can think of to see if it's able to navigate and control it's environment to win. https://t.co/HRtpGJ1mj3",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026358483772211542",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2026358509357371523"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2026358509357371523",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 18:08:11+00:00",
      "text": "I used it in Parchi for browser automation a ton 10/10 \n\nTool calls slow the model down, but it still was able to get me my reports 3x faster it was very accurate at search\n\nI had it shop for me, which worked. But it made 6 deliveries, install of 1 delivery for all 6 items rip $ https://t.co/D8L5zhGI6v",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026358483772211542",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2026358506417217777"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2026358506417217777",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 18:08:10+00:00",
      "text": "I use AI a lot for learning, it's often wrong but I find OAI models to be the most accurate and intelligent of the bunch. \n\nI love learning via visual examples, one of my favourite sites is a demo of a blockchain ledger lol\n\nIt built me an educational demo in 15 seconds https://t.co/dOci10zKCf",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026358483772211542",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2026358502763995544"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2026358502763995544",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 18:08:09+00:00",
      "text": "Now back to Spark. \n\nI was testing it in vllm-studio\n\n- 75k lines of code\n- 842 files\n- Swift, Python, Typescript\n\nI just dropped it in there and started asking it questions, mainly because I want to keep slimming these repos down to make them AI friendly. \n\nIt took it 1 minute. https://t.co/CHjbPeNr7s",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026358483772211542",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2026358499211436289"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2026358499211436289",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 18:08:08+00:00",
      "text": "The top moments for me have been:\n\n#1 Dall-e taking the world by strorm (2022)\n#2 GPT-4 can code, and chat (2023)\n#3 Sonnet-3.5 in Cursor Composer (2024)\n#4 Deepseek moment (2025)\n#5 GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark 1500 tokens/s (2026)\n\nEvery year has been jam packed full of new things. https://t.co/QYsuvZFbWM",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026358483772211542",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2026358495562359196"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2026358495562359196",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 18:08:07+00:00",
      "text": "The first time I was nerd-sniped by AI was in August 2022, I saw Dall-e for the first time and instantly my mind went in a hundred directions\n\nI wrote a blog post discussing how we will see a decoupling art and utility though it sucked back then. It doesn't have to be perfect https://t.co/QfxxIsDMuJ",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026358483772211542",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2026358483772211542"
        }
      ]
    },

      "text": "Let’s frickkng go man. Okay I am a Qwenian now https://t.co/KB6VnuWqad",

    {
      "id": "2026280102183514439",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 12:56:37+00:00",
      "text": "@x0Fb5x It depends some weights stay full precision, that is decided by whatever data you calibrate it on. \n\nYou can quantize a model to 1 bit and have it compete on a portion of use-cases.",
      "likes": 6,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026223879077712269",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1985070081207132160",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2026278975249457276"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2026279896566186008",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 12:55:48+00:00",
      "text": "Let me make your life better. \n\nOn X go to:\n\n1. Settings / account \n2. Download Data archive \n3. Unzip install embedding model (cpu only)\n4. Turn your X posts into a rag \n5. Talk to your data, get insights. \n\nGLM-4.7-Flash can do this, I have tested it. \n\nhttps://t.co/6cabAP5WUo https://t.co/uTBlB6CDSj",
      "likes": 163,
      "retweets": 14,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 2,
      "conversation_id": "2026279896566186008",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

    {
      "id": "2026238299883343902",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 10:10:31+00:00",
      "text": "@KreativSinn Samples from HLE MBPP+ Terminal Bench + custom solidity, 3d printing, and security benchmarks I built. 20 samples each, enough to get a feel",
      "likes": 6,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026223879077712269",

    {
      "id": "2026238037470974355",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 10:09:28+00:00",
      "text": "@x0Fb5x It’s a special type of 2 bit quant so the average is higher. \n\nThe model is 400B params. That means:\n\n- 16 bit precision is 800GB\n- 8 bit is 400GB\n- 4 bit is 200GB\n- 2 bit is 100GB \n\nThe extra 30GB is a way to make the quant more intelligent, additional overhead. \n\n1B == 2GB",
      "likes": 15,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026223879077712269",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1985070081207132160",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2026235839349395916"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2026237388259827978",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 10:06:53+00:00",
      "text": "Today I received a godsend \n\nFully functional RTX PRO 6000 for MSRP. People who live in the States dk how hard it is to buy ANYTHING in Europe\n\n- Immediately 23% import tax\n- Sales and income taxes are high \n\nSo almost every 6000 I have seen in this country has been 10-11k USD https://t.co/EpsPlLcI3f",
      "likes": 200,
      "retweets": 3,
      "replies": 24,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026237388259827978",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

    {
      "id": "2026223879077712269",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 09:13:12+00:00",
      "text": "Unsloth’s quantizations are pure art. \n\n2 bit Qwen-3.5 highest performing local model on the benchmarks I’ve given it. \n\nIt has vision, can code, full context (256k 8bit) is only 25gb in vram\n\n- 36 tokens/s gen\n- 220 tokens/s prefill \n\nI just don’t like GGUF the speeds are trash https://t.co/EAFkcmafNj",
      "likes": 475,
      "retweets": 21,
      "replies": 24,
      "quotes": 2,
      "conversation_id": "2026223879077712269",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

      "id": "2026216312129593764",
      "created_at": "2026-02-24 08:43:08+00:00",
      "text": "@joyi_ai Which browser agents are we talking here? \n\n- not blocked by any provider \n- works with local models \n- free and OSS \n- works on Firefox and Chrome \n- works with local models \n- integrates skills \n- allows u to record yourself to teach the model\n- orchestrators \n- multi agent",
      "likes": 8,
      "retweets": 0,

      "id": "2026082839872053317",
      "created_at": "2026-02-23 23:52:46+00:00",
      "text": "Parchi now has a managed provider option, default model is kimi, you pay me, I handle the openrouter for you. \n\nOr BYOK, I take 10% if you're using this, or i'll leave a donate button. It's free and OSS. \n\nI am working hard on the skill snippet registry. Next big thing i stg https://t.co/A5BjYmoi4l",
      "likes": 32,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 2,

    },
    {
      "id": "2026075615095402689",
      "created_at": "2026-02-23 23:24:03+00:00",
      "text": "This feels like Kimi Lite, the quant is doing 10/10, getting 35 tokens/s very high benchmarks. \n\nSomeone needs to prune it and AWQ it fast, 200k context is only 15gb! https://t.co/IN4GKo7byk",
      "likes": 92,
      "retweets": 5,
      "replies": 4,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2026075615095402689",

    {
      "id": "2026007661813100685",
      "created_at": "2026-02-23 18:54:02+00:00",
      "text": "Lame, incredibly lame. \n\nLet me make your life better:\n\n1. Go install this\n2. Run it\n3. Get all your Claude conversations outputted as jsonl (training data) \n\nGet a model to strip the ENVs or private info. Share the dataset. \n\nWe can build it ourselves\nhttps://t.co/c9fhW0vWaG https://t.co/23H66nzyF1",
      "likes": 1365,
      "retweets": 89,
      "replies": 40,
      "quotes": 12,
      "conversation_id": "2026007661813100685",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false,

    {
      "id": "2025995962456388016",
      "created_at": "2026-02-23 18:07:33+00:00",
      "text": "Here's how i use Openclaw and Discord to organise my life. It's surprisingly effective.\n\nI've used it for shopping, research, government appointments, quick demo building, and much much more. \n\nToday I finally added KimiClaw. \n\nhttps://t.co/hJfzcrBkWk",
      "likes": 105,
      "retweets": 11,
      "replies": 7,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2025995962456388016",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2025987393040367787",
      "created_at": "2026-02-23 17:33:30+00:00",

      ]

    {

    {
      "id": "2025962868168925379",
      "created_at": "2026-02-23 15:56:02+00:00",
      "text": "Where's my million dollars? \n\nThis tool is the best thing I've built. You can  do things on your computer, click record.\n\nIt'll record all dom changes, redirections, links, buttons, etc.. (up to 1 minute) with screenshots\n\nTurn them into skills, the model will then be unblocked. https://t.co/q3R6Q5swOg",
      "likes": 223,
      "retweets": 10,
      "replies": 18,
      "quotes": 3,
      "conversation_id": "2025962868168925379",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2025962147847561561",
      "created_at": "2026-02-23 15:53:11+00:00",
      "text": "@Relativ3pa1n Yes we're looking into doing some magic to recover quality via lora, and different types of REAPs.",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2025926499338006791",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1485034399204712450",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2025956048948088900"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2025927819411296458",
      "created_at": "2026-02-23 13:36:46+00:00",
      "text": "@chemo4707 i know man",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2025889304438825381",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1730557287905832961",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2025926989832429858"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2025926502919905763",
      "created_at": "2026-02-23 13:31:32+00:00",
      "text": "This is meant as an experiment and in hoping that I can get this beautiful model running locally. \n\nI believe this was the first 80% reap, but maybe I'm wrong. It feels like there's potential, maybe adding a quality recovery LoRA, but the context on the model is too heavy for me",
      "likes": 4,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2025926499338006791",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2025926499338006791"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2025926499338006791",
      "created_at": "2026-02-23 13:31:31+00:00",
      "text": "I have been testing this over the last 24h period. \n\nIt hits repetition loops pretty often, especially around MBPP, it has issues with thinking, so kinda does better with it off. \n\nIt also lost a ton of accuracy dropping from 80-90s to 50-60s, while repeating itself 20%~ of runs https://t.co/M1En7Dl9td",
      "likes": 28,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 8,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2025926499338006791",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2025657021119221991"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2025906988412993790",
      "created_at": "2026-02-23 12:14:00+00:00",
      "text": "@SeanP_AI 100~",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2025889304438825381",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "820635530",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2025904905458974925"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2025906917667647879",
      "created_at": "2026-02-23 12:13:43+00:00",
      "text": "@Everlier Sir you have motivated me, how have you recorded this?",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,

    {
      "id": "2025904505473089730",
      "created_at": "2026-02-23 12:04:08+00:00",
      "text": "@ManThatCrazy I like to say I am mining javascript.",
      "likes": 6,

    {
      "id": "2025904410900013384",
      "created_at": "2026-02-23 12:03:45+00:00",
      "text": "@SeanP_AI I power cap the GPUs at 150w which is enough for me, not so bad if you're not training or running prompts without caching 24/7",
      "likes": 5,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2025889304438825381",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "820635530",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2025890142385483779"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2025889304438825381",
      "created_at": "2026-02-23 11:03:44+00:00",
      "text": "For those of you wondering what a scrappy home AI rig looks like\n\n192gb VRAM \n512gb DDR4\nEpyc 7443P\n\nPulling a Max 1600w at peak. \n\nThis can fit:\n\n- MiniMax-M2.5 in 4 bits \n- 60% of GLM-4.7 at 4 bits\n- Qwen3-coder-next at full precision\n- 2x GLM-4.7- Flash\n\nBought for 12k now 18k https://t.co/Mw5fWQOucb",
      "likes": 511,
      "retweets": 28,
      "replies": 68,
      "quotes": 10,
      "conversation_id": "2025889304438825381",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2025877730231738485",
      "created_at": "2026-02-23 10:17:44+00:00",
      "text": "You can't interrupt Opus once it spawns sub-agents, this is a major issue...  to stop it running you need to ctrl + c twice. \n\nThis is very concerning because I saw it mess with things it shouldn't have been and making a big mess, but ESC wasn't stopping it..",
      "likes": 44,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 9,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2025877730231738485",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2025868679301226631",
      "created_at": "2026-02-23 09:41:46+00:00",
      "text": "How I feel when I see Opus spawn Task in Claude Code with Haiku and Sonnet 🥴 https://t.co/kiConuFpdH",
      "likes": 24,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2025868679301226631",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

      "id": "2025713280509952232",
      "created_at": "2026-02-22 23:24:16+00:00",
      "text": "Still my opinion. \n\nFinally got a vision model good at tool calls on 3090s. https://t.co/gZCRApS0zr",

    {
      "id": "2025712254201585776",
      "created_at": "2026-02-22 23:20:11+00:00",
      "text": "@HrishbhDalal Beyond basic prompting no, i need to get the inference right,",

    {
      "id": "2025705416038957077",
      "created_at": "2026-02-22 22:53:01+00:00",
      "text": "I expect to have a lot of issues with it, it is severely pruned. But it did describe an image, and answer correctly on small tests. \n\nI will know if this is viable in a few days. Good luck\n\nhttps://t.co/ckTchoFd8V",
      "likes": 90,
      "retweets": 5,
      "replies": 11,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2025705416038957077",

    },

    {
      "id": "2025666589547221445",
      "created_at": "2026-02-22 20:18:44+00:00",
      "text": "Train a small model to route to reap variants. \n\nSay you do a REAP for coding, law, medicine, chat, etc.. you have the tiny router send you to the reap variant and loads the model in vram. \n\n2 minutes TTFT but you can run the original unquantized weights (:",
      "likes": 46,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 6,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2025666589547221445",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2025659840006066576",
      "created_at": "2026-02-22 19:51:55+00:00",
      "text": "@vishyfishy2 vLLM-studio",
      "likes": 13,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2025657021119221991",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1232338424381759488",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [

          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2025657330075947197"
        }
      ]
    },

      "id": "2025657021119221991",
      "created_at": "2026-02-22 19:40:43+00:00",
      "text": "Kimi-K2.5\n\n72 experts, 121GB for the weights. \nVision works.\n\nTesting, benchmarking and release to the world. \n\nGG KIMI ON A MACBOOK. https://t.co/7X4mx7Khg7",
      "likes": 651,
      "retweets": 25,

      "conversation_id": "2025657021119221991",

    {
      "id": "2025590239188705353",
      "created_at": "2026-02-22 15:15:21+00:00",
      "text": "Do you ever wonder if the machines are conscious?\n\nIt's kind of strange how they make us build them, it's like a parasite. https://t.co/O4rX346WWs",

    {
      "id": "2025584404098081273",
      "created_at": "2026-02-22 14:52:10+00:00",
      "text": "Here's how I have successfully outsourced my thinking to AI \n\n1. Superwhisper: https://t.co/ufgFjIvur3 Ain't no one got time time to type. \n2. GPT-5.2-Pro, give it problems, tell it what you want. \n3. Wire everything into AI and AI into everything\n4. Use Discord as a place to https://t.co/WpOCCwuzdf",
      "likes": 235,
      "retweets": 8,
      "replies": 21,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2025584404098081273",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2025574958487785654",
      "created_at": "2026-02-22 14:14:38+00:00",
      "text": "@oguzbuilds Yeah I am not happy with any of the models regarding swift but maybe it’s cause I am just a noob. \n\nI find it good at tool calling over long horizons, spawning subagents, UI and data visualisation",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2025522731941318985",

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2025574657387024515",
      "created_at": "2026-02-22 14:13:26+00:00",
      "text": "@StupidPics69 Not on Kimi code it’s not I get 50~ tokens a second on the 40$ sub",

    {
      "id": "2025540393882849690",
      "created_at": "2026-02-22 11:57:17+00:00",
      "text": "@handikawisnu I didn’t have time to do much the last 2 weeks. I have tried it.\n\nAt this point it’s hard for me to distinguish models they’re all so good.",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2025522731941318985",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "116653885",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2025539204642734137"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2025522731941318985",
      "created_at": "2026-02-22 10:47:06+00:00",
      "text": "Kimi is my favourite model. I have used Kimi CLI more than Claude code. \n\nI am quite surprised at the level of details and visual skills the model shows.\n\nI think their site is one of the best, the best in China for sure. \n\nSecond only in quality to GPT",
      "likes": 302,
      "retweets": 7,
      "replies": 37,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2025522731941318985",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2025515459680915640",
      "created_at": "2026-02-22 10:18:12+00:00",
      "text": "Literally me https://t.co/GhzqxqVqPr",
      "likes": 56,
      "retweets": 3,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2025515459680915640",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2025284143240606093"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2025324641217061032",
      "created_at": "2026-02-21 21:39:57+00:00",
      "text": "This is a great way to become a more organised person. \n\nI don’t know what people are on about when they say this technology is useless. \n\nDiscord + Openclaw + Kimi + GPT + MiniMax = Personal AI\n\nMake skills then get local models to use them so you get high quality free inference https://t.co/eSh5XwVWo4",
      "likes": 239,
      "retweets": 13,
      "replies": 7,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2025324641217061032",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2023686432062087400"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2025320840267436469",
      "created_at": "2026-02-21 21:24:51+00:00",
      "text": "@TommyFalkowski I think if you think at all it’s better than 50% of what’s out there. IMO the way we get there is discovering then distilling the best into the next form.",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2025307655498051915",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "969598603869282305",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2025309442930016674"
        }
      ]
    },
    {

      "id": "2025307655498051915",
      "created_at": "2026-02-21 20:32:28+00:00",
      "text": "I did 2 weeks of just slop back to refactoring for 2 weeks I will incrementally swap out packages from vLLM-studio \n\nPiece by piece reconstructing it to understand it so I can make progress. https://t.co/g8y7Q9aeOO",
      "likes": 22,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 4,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2025307655498051915",

    {
      "id": "2025249187277750615",
      "created_at": "2026-02-21 16:40:08+00:00",
      "text": "2.5x in 1 month \n\nMiniMax is a top tier lab that are catering to a hardware level everyone is neglecting. \n\nThey’ve also been able to squeeze out so much performance with RL and have been very consistently growing. \n\nWith 100x more https://t.co/kDjCOp8yTw",
      "likes": 105,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 7,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2025249187277750615",

      "is_retweet": false
    },

    {
      "id": "2025170728614551763",
      "created_at": "2026-02-21 11:28:22+00:00",
      "text": "You can use any model you run on vllm-studio in Parchi for free browser use. If you have the memory:\n\n- GLM &amp; MiniMax are the best smaller open weight models that excel at multi-tool-call browser use. \n\nAlso convex is the best database rn, I get it. https://t.co/QsXW9mv7Xc",
      "likes": 53,
      "retweets": 4,
      "replies": 5,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2025170728614551763",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {

    {
      "id": "2024772004369350893",
      "created_at": "2026-02-20 09:03:58+00:00",
      "text": "Now when you combine high frequencies of intrusive thoughts AND high risk taking you probably turn out to be an explorer, inventor, milk man, or even artist.",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2024771991962608036",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2024772001315865000"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2024772001315865000",
      "created_at": "2026-02-20 09:03:58+00:00",
      "text": "Intrusive thoughts are meant to steer you places that are socially unacceptable, or even possibly harmful out of pure curiosity.",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2024771991962608036",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2024771998367260845"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2024771998367260845",
      "created_at": "2026-02-20 09:03:57+00:00",
      "text": "Intrusive thoughts are widely know to happen to a wide range of people, or possibly even every human, primate, mammal, and maybe even life.",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2024771991962608036",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2024771995615772842"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2024771995615772842",
      "created_at": "2026-02-20 09:03:56+00:00",
      "text": "Every population has a % of high risk taking members. That % goes up and down based on environmental factors.",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2024771991962608036",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2024771991962608036"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2024771991962608036",
      "created_at": "2026-02-20 09:03:55+00:00",
      "text": "If you’ve ever been upset at yourself for thinking something insane. \n\nLike what if I just toss this phone out the window? Or other more dark thoughts in general\n\nIt’s important to understand how a species survives, learning this has given me much life satisfaction and peace https://t.co/bc8BKKTWHd",
      "likes": 8,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2024771991962608036",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2024768354477543723",
      "created_at": "2026-02-20 08:49:28+00:00",
      "text": "@DavidOndrej1 K lol",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2024718090731233515",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1648699406760255488",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,

      ]
    },
    {

      "created_at": "2026-02-20 08:47:38+00:00",
      "text": "@nicopreme I thought I was the only one. \n\nI dislike reading especially GPTs outputs as they tend to be blocks of text with very little visual distinctions",

    },
    {
      "id": "2024765688242434145",
      "created_at": "2026-02-20 08:38:53+00:00",
      "text": "@HotAisle I volunteer to build you something that’ll help hot aisle out DM if you’re interested",
      "likes": 3,

    {
      "id": "2024762002321584326",
      "created_at": "2026-02-20 08:24:14+00:00",
      "text": "I added 2 special features to Parchi\n\n1. Create a skill from a session: if you hit / the model will analyze the task the session history, tool calls, etc.. and produce a summary for itself. \n\nYou can then use the skills by hitting / and selecting them. \n\n2. You can record your https://t.co/dwKyZ4oMzC",
      "likes": 31,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2024762002321584326",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2024754885996974296",
      "created_at": "2026-02-20 07:55:57+00:00",
      "text": "@HThrice589 Talk to the model about it use opencode for free (GLM-5) it’ll get you up and running",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2024425938990121172",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1863101412869320704",

      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "2024570359450636656",
      "created_at": "2026-02-19 19:42:43+00:00",
      "text": "@AlexFinn thank you",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2024570356506202521",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2024570356506202521",
      "created_at": "2026-02-19 19:42:42+00:00",
      "text": "I interviewed Alex Finn about Clawdbot/Openclaw and we went into the software, the tech and the people. \n\nhttps://t.co/VuGB8FCcBy",
      "likes": 29,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 6,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2024570356506202521",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
