    {
      "id": "2024426992758657483",
      "created_at": "2026-02-19 10:13:01+00:00",
      "text": "@Everlier I think it's the best one on the market, pure capability wise.",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2024234767818899499",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2024426896302199070"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2024426896302199070",
      "created_at": "2026-02-19 10:12:38+00:00",
      "text": "@Everlier Going to wire users into openrouter.. i'd rather buy a bunch of subs or deal directly with Kimi, MiniMax or ZAI but I think it'd be complex to build out. \n\nNo data retention, accounts are only used for membership, you don't need to sign in if you BYOK",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2024234767818899499",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "133797694",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2024425983244214709"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2024426535114199158",
      "created_at": "2026-02-19 10:11:12+00:00",
      "text": "@txhno It works everywhere",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2024250232490360874",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1167159140310872065",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2024425552795566114"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2024425964990591021",
      "created_at": "2026-02-19 10:08:56+00:00",
      "text": "Intelligence has always been abundant, we have always known how to harness it\n\nYou have the ability to do anything, build anything, be anything. This is the time to work on yourself\n\nI can't believe my luck that I was born in a time where I can make a rock build worlds for me. https://t.co/pgUjBzKyXU",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2024425938990121172",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2024425956572602561"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2024425956572602561",
      "created_at": "2026-02-19 10:08:54+00:00",
      "text": "For those who fear monger, or say there's no magic in this technology:\n\nSpend 1 week using it 2+ hours a day in focused sessions on a dream of yours, see what happens. \n\nIntelligence is now a commodity, you have jet engines that can do incredible things, you can power for free. https://t.co/LpCDNJBIFL",
      "likes": 6,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2024425938990121172",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2024425952571183485"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2024425952571183485",
      "created_at": "2026-02-19 10:08:53+00:00",
      "text": "To be a power use of any system requires 3 things:\n\n1. Read the manual, your questions were answered.\n2. Spend time understanding the components.\n3. Experience it, use it, fail, learn, play.\n\nNo video or experience with a person will give you what you need to build something. https://t.co/g8IipEcZcS",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2024425938990121172",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2024425948716699697"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2024425948716699697",
      "created_at": "2026-02-19 10:08:52+00:00",
      "text": "Good websites, apps, games, and hardware have three things in common. \n\n1. They are useful enough to justify their cost.\n2. They onboard you slowly, show you what's possible. \n3. They give the freedom to do things your way. https://t.co/Dk5Xcjwj3a",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2024425938990121172",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2024425945050857717"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2024425945050857717",
      "created_at": "2026-02-19 10:08:51+00:00",
      "text": "You have 3 options when you've chosen a framework:\n\n1. Slop together something broken for fun. \n2. Spend serious hours building something good. \n3. Pay to use someone else's implementation, which was probably built for them. https://t.co/LAFIrbc6CL",
      "likes": 4,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2024425938990121172",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2024425938990121172"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2024425938990121172",
      "created_at": "2026-02-19 10:08:50+00:00",
      "text": "I need to do another OpenClaw post.\n\nI see both sides of people saying it's crap or they have a team working for them. \n\nWith any framework you are given building blocks, typically with some templates to work from. \n\nYou have a friend in your computer, it's your job to build it. https://t.co/eRlF8yXvPN",
      "likes": 81,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 10,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2024425938990121172",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2024420166147191173",
      "created_at": "2026-02-19 09:45:54+00:00",
      "text": "@Everlier @ZenMagnets The price is high enough to avoid insane surge of interest, and it's low value enough that the labs don't care for it. (I guess?)",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2024244426067808694",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "133797694",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2024418303335112811"
        }
      ]
    },
    {

    {

      "created_at": "2026-02-19 08:31:07+00:00",
      "text": "Weekend video. \n\nTaking apart my AI machine to clean it up. \n\nIt’s been about 6 months since I put it together and despite frequent vacuuming, air filters and elevated platform, it’s still dusty AF \n\nHopefully I don’t fry anything https://t.co/pdK5kDv21x",

    {
      "id": "2024383791255392608",
      "created_at": "2026-02-19 07:21:21+00:00",
      "text": "@HotAisle It’s been incredible I used it to build the AMD and multinode inference features that are in my open PR for vLLM-studio. \n\nI was running 3 models on the GPU (GLM-4.7, Ace, and Whisper large) without a single issue. \n\nI will see if it can quantize and prune this weekend thank you",
      "likes": 7,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2024380816592425045",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1714688881311051778",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2024380816592425045"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2024376485469982891",
      "created_at": "2026-02-19 06:52:19+00:00",
      "text": "@HotAisle I have been really pleasantly surprised by the GPU provided by Hot Aisle, I used it for some 20 hours without wasting much credits",
      "likes": 5,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2024195600917680542",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1714688881311051778",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2024195600917680542"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2024375352387063955",
      "created_at": "2026-02-19 06:47:49+00:00",

    {

      "created_at": "2026-02-19 00:03:28+00:00",

    {
      "id": "2024273441260503473",
      "created_at": "2026-02-19 00:02:52+00:00",
      "text": "RT @_djdumpling: new blog! What methodologies do labs use to train frontier models?\n\nThe blog distills 7 open-weight model reports from fro…",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 286,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2024273441260503473",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,

          "id": "2024203932709552352"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2024270352059170868",
      "created_at": "2026-02-18 23:50:35+00:00",
      "text": "I’ve been using Qoder for a few days. I like it a lot. Same agent stuff but their offering of models, framing, and product suite is so nice. \n\nI think it makes sense to collapse Kimi, GLM and MiniMax coding plans in 1 with Qoder for some people. \n\nNot a pitch just an observation",
      "likes": 30,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2024270352059170868",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2024263423928713660",
      "created_at": "2026-02-18 23:23:03+00:00",
      "text": "We could be stacking our GPUs but u playing. https://t.co/6OR2m7x4tc",
      "likes": 29,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2024263423928713660",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2024255179810316662",
      "created_at": "2026-02-18 22:50:18+00:00",
      "text": "@amatelic93 i'm not sure actually, could be since I have access to it",

    {
      "id": "2024250232490360874",
      "created_at": "2026-02-18 22:30:38+00:00",
      "text": "I recommend you try 1 month of Kimi's 20$ sub, it's incredible. You can get a pre-configured Clawdbot ready to be plopped into Telegram\n\nThe model is also in the top 3 best models to have in Openclaw, it can reason very well, has good vision and is fun to talk to\n\nlmk how it go https://t.co/BExVPP1pur",
      "likes": 280,
      "retweets": 6,
      "replies": 35,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2024250232490360874",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2024244426067808694",
      "created_at": "2026-02-18 22:07:34+00:00",

    {
      "id": "2024234767818899499",
      "created_at": "2026-02-18 21:29:11+00:00",
      "text": "Parchi launch date will be Friday/Monday. \n\nYou can pay 10$ p/m main model will be Kimi-K2.5 with Qwen-3.5 / GLM / MiniMax as secondary options.\n\nUsage will be billed by api pricing for now, unless Kimi / MiniMax / ZAI / Qwen want to bless me with 2 weeks free usage.\n\nSkills, and https://t.co/tMfKPYT3gF",
      "likes": 56,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 12,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2024234767818899499",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

    {
      "id": "2024084360698224681",
      "created_at": "2026-02-18 11:31:31+00:00",
      "text": "Let me make your life better.\n\nHave you ever had to resize images, trim, change formats, etc..\n\nI have to do this daily, ffmpeg exists I just don't have the time to nerd out. \n\nGPT-5.3-Codex-Spark resized 5 imgs w perfect quality in 1 min, i just used to make this vid work on x https://t.co/2SUKbCdW0t",
      "likes": 67,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2024084360698224681",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2024059682139070515",
      "created_at": "2026-02-18 09:53:28+00:00",
      "text": "@4shadowed Onboarding is the moat bro. \n\nOkay but to be serious a framework is only as good as its starter kits.\n\nYou’ve reached an audience that feels like openclaw is the way for them to get in on the agentic promise of AI. \n\nUnfortunately getting them configured in a sandbox doesn’t help",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2024018401702690819",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1181339960516976640",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2024018401702690819"
        }
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "2024046905043382518",
      "created_at": "2026-02-18 09:02:41+00:00",
      "text": "I built a worm (never released) which is a harness for any LLM with the only goal of the harness is guiding the LLM to find a new host.\n\nIt scans open ports, any servers or communication channel and tries to find the next device to replicate to. \n\nIt’s not that hard and very https://t.co/sb9XpxAf93",
      "likes": 121,
      "retweets": 8,
      "replies": 5,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2024046905043382518",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2023923695484346553"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2024043117733716427",
      "created_at": "2026-02-18 08:47:38+00:00",
      "text": "Open weight models are not any worse than frontier, you should try out openrouter/free \n\nOne model in particular I think everyone should try is aurora alpha (; https://t.co/imtKV2DA2z",
      "likes": 68,
      "retweets": 4,
      "replies": 8,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2024043117733716427",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [

          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2023683244412088766"
        }
      ]
    },
    {

    {
      "id": "2023906506706764097",
      "created_at": "2026-02-17 23:44:48+00:00",
      "text": "@TheAhmadOsman Good question, here in Europe a 6000 goes for about 10-11k usd. It’s criminal. \n\nI can get double the vram for the price, also I haven’t seen anyone running it on social media it’d make banger context. \n\n- 3090s cheap and good vram inefficient power wise\n- 6000s 40% more",
      "likes": 38,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 6,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2023899610301751619",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "248951926",

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2023905232757944774",
      "created_at": "2026-02-17 23:39:44+00:00",
      "text": "@AI_with_Eric Not in Europe it ain’t",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2023899610301751619",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "2014419785933520896",
      "is_reply": true,

    {
      "id": "2023899610301751619",
      "created_at": "2026-02-17 23:17:23+00:00",
      "text": "I’ve made my decision. Swapping out 4 RTX 3090 for 2x RTX 4090D Super 48GB mods from China.\n\nI was considering A40s and Teslas but I want to get off ampere and get onto hopper. \n\nIt’s the best deal overall in Nvidia \n\nWish me luck, all will be recorded and shared online. https://t.co/9J05stpUZN",
      "likes": 279,
      "retweets": 5,
      "replies": 47,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2023899610301751619",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

      "retweets": 20,
      "replies": 4,
      "quotes": 0,

      "text": "@victormustar Only in Codex, which I point my browser. Parchi + https://t.co/JaxdovYe8E + kimi + spark is the best experience for browsing. \n\nNot even just because i built it, I built it cause the rest suck",

    {
      "id": "2023849837545091504",
      "created_at": "2026-02-17 19:59:37+00:00",
      "text": "@MrV_777 Faster inference is very good for a few things:\n\n1. In the loop coding: you can productively work on the file level\n2. Iteration lets you find UI vibes faster\n3. Review + docs + types don't feel like a hassle\n4. Learning is more fun.",
      "likes": 5,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2023834453659771313",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1006282357878525954",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2023846676923117827"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2023847705492246713",
      "created_at": "2026-02-17 19:51:08+00:00",
      "text": "@valenxi_ Mostly OAI rn",

      "conversation_id": "2023834453659771313",

    {
      "id": "2023847084437483790",
      "created_at": "2026-02-17 19:48:40+00:00",
      "text": "Asking Codex (GPU) to find and kill Codex (@cerebras) https://t.co/xJFxktzsuD",
      "likes": 133,
      "retweets": 7,
      "replies": 5,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2023847084437483790",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2023844654660415920",
      "created_at": "2026-02-17 19:39:01+00:00",
      "text": "@alexhooketh It's a different way of working, it requires you to be deeply in the loop but you can iterate on features so fast. \n\nRunning it in cerebras-cli w gpt pro sub they have a Ralph agent that refactored and produce 1 feature in Swift! within 3 minutes. \n\nSame task on Opus was 45",
      "likes": 3,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2023834453659771313",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1287755940054302722",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2023842893954511213"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2023843443689353401",
      "created_at": "2026-02-17 19:34:12+00:00",
      "text": "GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark is now my favourite model, watch how it automated research masterfully, within 2 minutes, managing tabs. \n\nUsing plan mode effectively, making logic decisions, surviving compactions. \n\nI have done sooooo many things with this model, game changing. https://t.co/It3dzs8T6s https://t.co/f2CyGhdk9W",
      "likes": 193,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 15,
      "quotes": 5,
      "conversation_id": "2023843443689353401",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2023829750083424707"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2023842441464614983",
      "created_at": "2026-02-17 19:30:13+00:00",
      "text": "@alexhooketh you need to use external plan files and checklists i mention this in my video (:\n\ni struggled with it to start too",
      "likes": 4,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2023834453659771313",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1287755940054302722",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2023840960766243058"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2023834453659771313",
      "created_at": "2026-02-17 18:58:29+00:00",
      "text": "I have officially entered into accepting to pay for 2 Pro plans, this model is insane. \n\nI have never felt so locked in. https://t.co/BrSgS1U5Iq",
      "likes": 82,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 15,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2023834453659771313",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2023829750083424707",
      "created_at": "2026-02-17 18:39:48+00:00",
      "text": "I show off why ChatGPT-Pro is the best AI subscription, and demo the latest model GPT-5.3-Codex Spark\n\nI am genuinely in love with this model, this is one of my best videos to date. \n\nhttps://t.co/khCgNPu78a",
      "likes": 44,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2023829750083424707",

    },

    {
      "id": "2023718952459776041",
      "created_at": "2026-02-17 11:19:31+00:00",
      "text": "@deeperflows 48 Hours isn't just for the pruning, though. \n\nReally to be specific, it actually takes about 12 to 24 hours to get both prunes and quantizations. made let's say about five or ten of them.\n\nIt depends on the size of the model. The reason I need eight H200s is because of the",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2023680978003091914",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1116610601885716480",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2023712115320918286"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2023711730593931431",
      "created_at": "2026-02-17 10:50:49+00:00",
      "text": "just like you can prune &gt; quantize you can quantize then prune, or prune an existing quant.",
      "likes": 13,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2023711730593931431",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2023711429455736916",
      "created_at": "2026-02-17 10:49:38+00:00",
      "text": "@deeperflows 5x the samples minimum would take 15 days if nothing fucks up.",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2023680978003091914",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1116610601885716480",

      "referenced_tweets": [
        {

          "id": "2023710591622492407"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2023711285586678241",
      "created_at": "2026-02-17 10:49:03+00:00",
      "text": "@scholzmx Built on vllm first, but right now it works with llama.cpp, sglang, and vllm.\n\nSoon with ROCm, then next with apple.",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2023680581305856437",

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2023686770085245287",
      "created_at": "2026-02-17 09:11:38+00:00",
      "text": "@gonizahavy merging today/tomorrow",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2023680581305856437",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "103910996",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2023685872374186108"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2023686453499249042",
      "created_at": "2026-02-17 09:10:23+00:00",
      "text": "Issues:\n\n- Even with all this context management is still a PITA, it's really hard when the model just forgets shit\n- Technical challenges and crashes here and there.\n- It's only as useful as the amount of time you pour in it",
      "likes": 28,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 6,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2023686432062087400",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2023686450609357071"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2023686450609357071",
      "created_at": "2026-02-17 09:10:22+00:00",
      "text": "5. Fun:\n\n- Music channel, fed it all my favorite music and a feedback system with thumbs up and down so it can on cron recommend me new music based on feedback + my taste\n- Research channel, on heartbeat fetches me latest interesting research pap\n\nThread 5/6 https://t.co/eiBGMPYulR",
      "likes": 13,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2023686432062087400",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2023686446402425245"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2023686446402425245",
      "created_at": "2026-02-17 09:10:21+00:00",
      "text": "4. Logistics:\n\n- config channel which just shows you all the openclaw configs, crons, models, etc..\n- Logs channel which just forwards all openclaw logs.\n\nThread 4/6 https://t.co/4wmmXZ3DGg",
      "likes": 13,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2023686432062087400",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2023686441809650100"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2023686441809650100",
      "created_at": "2026-02-17 09:10:20+00:00",
      "text": "3. Shopping:\n\n- Setup GPU-Mac watchdog to catch\n- Yesterday Openclaw made it's first purchase and delivery to my house autonomously\n- Helped me get a domain.\n\nThread 3/6 https://t.co/UNnTUbWuNG",
      "likes": 12,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2023686432062087400",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2023686436269003087"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2023686436269003087",
      "created_at": "2026-02-17 09:10:19+00:00",
      "text": "2. Code:\n\n- Setup a tapoki thread forwarder\n- Setup a RAG over my top 30 repos to find common issues, inefficiencies and areas for growth.\n- Infra channel with all my devices, codebases, etc..\n\nNot so happy with the recommendations, will change it more.\n\nThread 2/6 https://t.co/WJ06dXuDHS",
      "likes": 16,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2023686432062087400",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2023686432062087400"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2023686432062087400",
      "created_at": "2026-02-17 09:10:18+00:00",
      "text": "New Openclaw hacks.\n\n1. Task management:\n\n- Use forum for long term life tasks, each post under forum is it's own session\n- Use the new feature to allow openclaw to build you UI components in discord\n- Feed it your problems, ambitions, goals, context, socials. \n\nThread 1/6 https://t.co/1VsOMP4ix2",
      "likes": 705,
      "retweets": 54,
      "replies": 32,
      "quotes": 6,
      "conversation_id": "2023686432062087400",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2023680978003091914",
      "created_at": "2026-02-17 08:48:37+00:00",
      "text": "I tried pruning Qwen-3.5-plus on my 3090s. \n\nThe problem? 192 samples will take 3 days to finish since i have to do cpu offloading. \n\nIn order to get something high quality i need 48 hours with 8x H200s \n\n): https://t.co/QRfHoCAPUs",
      "likes": 79,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 11,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2023680978003091914",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2023680581305856437",
      "created_at": "2026-02-17 08:47:03+00:00",
      "text": "I love this project, it's really visually grown on me, now if only i can get the onboarding right. \n\nI dislike running GGUFs. they have worse prefill speeds, but very few people doing AWQ on new models. https://t.co/vrYKjdomsW",
      "likes": 33,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 5,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2023680581305856437",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

    {
      "id": "2023528347842867262",
      "created_at": "2026-02-16 22:42:08+00:00",
      "text": "I just did a 3 minute Ralph run with Cerebras-cli and GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark\n\nIt implemented theming into vllm-studio's switft apps! This isn't easy for models, Swift is a weak spot. \n\nI love this Ralph &lt;&gt; Cerebras thing. https://t.co/1iFh5W0E3q",
      "likes": 103,
      "retweets": 3,
      "replies": 6,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2023528347842867262",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

      "text": "@m_chirculescu Yes, I got it working with olmo-7B but don't have the compute to work on larger models which is the whole point. \n\nI will release stuff when I have the time, also it's not bait :p I have been doing this for a while.",

      ]

      "id": "2023523910671814946",
      "created_at": "2026-02-16 22:24:30+00:00",
      "text": "I feel like vllm-studio is getting better, the onboarding needs help, I need someone to help me make the onboarding better... https://t.co/kd6k59lHJr",

    {
      "id": "2023521514826465502",
      "created_at": "2026-02-16 22:14:59+00:00",
      "text": "Qwen3-coder-next \n\nI am really surprised how well it holds up, I haven't had any dumb issues with it yet, I've gotten it to work in Parchi albeit a little slow. \n \nPrefill on GGUFs is a big issue, but it's doing pretty well. It's handling swift-ui pretty well and doesn't have https://t.co/q95jweh7tS",
      "likes": 135,
      "retweets": 6,
      "replies": 12,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2023521514826465502",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

      "text": "@QuixiAI @AI_with_Eric It depends, it preserves only whatever you leave in, so if you calibrate on code and agentic it retains those abilities I used GLM-4.7 extensively and built projects with it",

    {
      "id": "2023461958234587163",
      "created_at": "2026-02-16 18:18:19+00:00",
      "text": "@melvynx These benchmarks don’t tell the whole story. \n\nGPT-5.*-Pro is the most intelligent model to interact with, it’s not on any of these because it’s too expensive to run. \n\nGLM has always felt to me a better writer and reasoner than Claude, the personality difference is enough",
      "likes": 13,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2023247685441212655",

          "id": "2023453245147115624"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2023430534236680205",
      "created_at": "2026-02-16 16:13:27+00:00",
      "text": "@AI_with_Eric This is amazing, thank you",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2023404153943507356",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "2014419785933520896",

      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2023428481355493699"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2023423323066216645",
      "created_at": "2026-02-16 15:44:48+00:00",
      "text": "@AI_with_Eric What did you try it for? and what was not so great about it?",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2023404153943507356",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "2014419785933520896",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2023413582629896624"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2023423034129035553",
      "created_at": "2026-02-16 15:43:39+00:00",
      "text": "@TCGsealed I would love to interview anyone at OpenAI, Anthropic, or Google should they be interested.",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2023145389583659216",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1665723757615251456",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2023419266629042584"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2023404153943507356",
      "created_at": "2026-02-16 14:28:37+00:00",
      "text": "https://t.co/SRVD3cULCz\n\ni want to prune this bad boy. \n\n40% prune 4bit quantization, best vision model on 192gb vram, I am sure.",
      "likes": 129,
      "retweets": 4,
      "replies": 5,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2023404153943507356",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2023403898992517624",
      "created_at": "2026-02-16 14:27:37+00:00",
      "text": "Do you do frontend development? \n\nIs keeping your styles consistent getting tiresome? \nClaude/GPT will typically not use consistent styles unless specifically prompted to. \n\nOne way I've found them to build better is by forcing theme to implement a theme system before anything. https://t.co/ls8yYzBQG3",
      "likes": 88,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 14,
      "quotes": 2,
      "conversation_id": "2023403898992517624",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2023385747550822776",
      "created_at": "2026-02-16 13:15:29+00:00",
      "text": "All aside, I still think Dario and Anthropic folks are very very smart and have built something incredible. \n\nhttps://t.co/LdgRNoc1RD",
      "likes": 6,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2023385747550822776",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2023385134230024308",
      "created_at": "2026-02-16 13:13:03+00:00",
      "text": "@Everlier Sounds just like me. \n\nThis is probably one of the most valuable environments for RL.",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2023377659984511118",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "133797694",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2023383614545658173"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2023377659984511118",
      "created_at": "2026-02-16 12:43:21+00:00",
      "text": "I successfully completed an end to end shopping session using exclusively LLMs. \n\nIt worked, but there were a lot of barrier that were both surprising and well expected. \n\nI saw it successfully click through a cookie, add 6 relevant items I requested to the basket. \n\nIt created https://t.co/c5fnhRTUAh",
      "likes": 34,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 4,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2023377659984511118",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2023366117251555768",
      "created_at": "2026-02-16 11:57:29+00:00",
      "text": "@Everlier It’s incredible. Poland has one of the best governments.",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2023363766222463434",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "133797694",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2023365523283886208"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2023366016336585206",
      "created_at": "2026-02-16 11:57:05+00:00",
      "text": "@souvikpng_ @AScully789 1. Install your X data from settings \n2. Run a rag on top of it, to make it easy to parse\n3. Write a skill on dealing with X data from scanning the data structures.\n\nThen have a model in chunks turn all that into jsonl",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2023339209579446756",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "2008896104868564992",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2023365265841680833"
        }
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "2023163146450399640",
      "created_at": "2026-02-15 22:30:57+00:00",
      "text": "Kimi-K2.5 is the best model at browser use, it's been able to handle complex setups, purchasing stuff, working on Stripe. \n\nScraping, QA, etc.. I love it. I am making it the default model and making a paid experience for Parchi but will keep it OSS.\n\nhttps://t.co/WjjiiK4Wy7 https://t.co/uosGiWBX1q",
      "likes": 387,
      "retweets": 16,
      "replies": 16,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2023163146450399640",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {

    {
      "id": "2023067431548096870",
      "created_at": "2026-02-15 16:10:37+00:00",
      "text": "I had such a great time talking to Leo from FactoryAI. Droid is a very good harness and worth paying attention to\n\nI especially recommend trying it with cliproxyapi and using /readiness-report\n\n#60 - Leo Tchourakov: How Factory built Droid. A top AI agent\nhttps://t.co/pkmaSeV4Q7",
      "likes": 29,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 4,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2023067431548096870",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

    {
      "id": "2023042696424927683",
      "created_at": "2026-02-15 14:32:19+00:00",
      "text": "@tharshan_09 Openclaw can use codex CLI to do things",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,

      "text": "@johntheyoung check my channel",

    {
      "id": "2023008142179668349",
      "created_at": "2026-02-15 12:15:01+00:00",
      "text": "Not everything has to be so serious.\n\nHere's my Openclaw setup:\n\n#1 Discord is the best tool for organizing and communicating with the models:\n\n- Use channels for system prompts &amp; cron outputs\n- In channels, use Threads as sessions \n- Setup stautsline in Voice groups, since they https://t.co/OjN15yiyjY",
      "likes": 399,
      "retweets": 26,
      "replies": 21,
      "quotes": 4,
      "conversation_id": "2023008142179668349",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

    {
      "id": "2022649171128520910",
      "created_at": "2026-02-14 12:28:36+00:00",
      "text": "How I use ZAI models, the good and the bad, full review out on youtube, I am very honest about the pros and cons and I love both Cerebras and ZAI. \n\nIt's good to know what you're getting into, and how to use things right.\nhttps://t.co/BKhIYvIEXO https://t.co/agqmW4juGj",
      "likes": 82,
      "retweets": 3,
      "replies": 5,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2022649171128520910",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2022631094235877546",
      "created_at": "2026-02-14 11:16:46+00:00",
      "text": "@Kaiyes_ Good man",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2022620402204230016",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1526972136",

      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [

          "type": "replied_to",

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2022628788056522855",
      "created_at": "2026-02-14 11:07:36+00:00",
      "text": "People don't bother reading the docs. \n\nJust because a company is Chinese doesn't mean they want to hurt you, steal your data, or whatever. \n\nI have heard of 0 cases where a Chinese company hurt Americans, no leaks, they've never done anything to bother us.\n\nSo every time a https://t.co/JsU988nlY6",
      "likes": 701,
      "retweets": 51,
      "replies": 47,
      "quotes": 4,
      "conversation_id": "2022628788056522855",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2022620402204230016",
      "created_at": "2026-02-14 10:34:17+00:00",
      "text": "I have a confession to make, I haven’t shared this openly online in a long time.\n\nWhen I was in my late teens I just gave up on life, I felt like I would never do anything worthwhile. \n\nI went completely off the rails, I was taking amounts of substances that would kill a small https://t.co/21RIGhWX7v",
      "likes": 405,
      "retweets": 12,
      "replies": 37,
      "quotes": 4,
      "conversation_id": "2022620402204230016",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2022605865694445699",
      "created_at": "2026-02-14 09:36:31+00:00",
      "text": "The kings are where they belong https://t.co/7J6r7SoNlA",

      "retweets": 5,

      "conversation_id": "2022605865694445699",

      "created_at": "2026-02-14 00:00:32+00:00",
      "text": "I interviewed Leo from Droid. \n\nHere's my interview with a tech lead at one of my favorite AI agent harnesses. \n\nhttps://t.co/pkmaSeV4Q7",

    {
      "id": "2022396952588242948",
      "created_at": "2026-02-13 19:46:22+00:00",
      "text": "MiniMax-M2.5 \n\n\"make me a 3d game\"\n\nit decided on the synthwave aesthetic, pretty much nailed it, the controllers work, this was 1 shot. https://t.co/BIi7mHVtLS",
      "likes": 58,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 7,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2022396952588242948",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2022327583040532536",
      "created_at": "2026-02-13 15:10:43+00:00",
      "text": "@jwangkun @MiniMax_AI Let's fucking go man. Thanks for tagging me",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2022310932693897628",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "411433517",

    },
    {
      "id": "2022285738570654192",
      "created_at": "2026-02-13 12:24:26+00:00",
      "text": "@nisten Hey, I really appreciate this comment. I had to read it a few times before I could answer. \n\n1. I am using Ampere which doesn't get a lot of the benefits of blackwell. \n\n2. I am very much so aware that most likely this is a huge waste of time, but I have the hardware and can",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2021250793345548417",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "15220945",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2021965259775295488"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2022283864027107342",
      "created_at": "2026-02-13 12:17:00+00:00",
      "text": "People approach a slow model (GPT 3 months ago)\n\nHow they approach a fast model (say Sonnet) then get frustrated with it, and dump it. \n\nThe same is true vice versa. \n\nFast/Small/Low-context/local-models can often do better at some types of tasks: \n\n1. QA / Testing\n2. Retrieval https://t.co/Olxw7udrSo",
      "likes": 25,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2022283864027107342",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2022276528017494171"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2022240882221875290",
      "created_at": "2026-02-13 09:26:12+00:00",
      "text": "If you want to use agents for building, regardless of your experience level I highly recommend watching this video, if you're more experienced skip to minute 5 \n\nhttps://t.co/v4P6qhY8kO https://t.co/rVUupoIuHH",
      "likes": 86,
      "retweets": 4,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2022240882221875290",

      "id": "2022202592299036904",
      "created_at": "2026-02-13 06:54:03+00:00",
      "text": "Benchmarks 25%~ loss 10% the size. \n\nI need to bring the loss down to 5% \n\nThis took a 14gb model to 1.4gb https://t.co/vbdrzJIzJ0 https://t.co/ur8V4khHxB",

    {
      "id": "2022102064902320607",
      "created_at": "2026-02-13 00:14:35+00:00",
      "text": "Best open weight labs:\n\n#1 MiniMax: they squeeze every last drop of intelligence out of such a small model \n\n#2 Kimi: Their latest model is so good at computer use, I don’t think most people have tried it in Parchi, it doesn’t fail.\n\n#3 GLM: This is probably the most intelligent https://t.co/51IpbJzwaN",
      "likes": 297,
      "retweets": 14,
      "replies": 20,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2022102064902320607",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

      "created_at": "2026-02-13 00:09:17+00:00",

      "text": "@bridgemindai Speed is the issue right now. \n\nThe model is excellent, I had it do a complex migration from Hetzner &amp; digital ocean onto AWS with maybe 3 prompts for 2 hours of work",

      "id": "2021957431774056753",
      "created_at": "2026-02-12 14:39:52+00:00",
      "text": "@moonlght12891 to apply research on model compression for novel methods that haven't been tried yet",

      "retweets": 0,

    {
      "id": "2021957205151363358",
      "created_at": "2026-02-12 14:38:58+00:00",
      "text": "@bitdeep_ To translate into a good model unless I get significantly more compute than I have access to. That's the problem. I can do this with a 7B model all day, but that doesn't mean it's good",
      "likes": 4,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2021947003383718256",

    },
    {
      "id": "2021957061563814027",
      "created_at": "2026-02-12 14:38:24+00:00",
      "text": "@tomcocobrico Experimenting with two different methods but first yes we will quantize it down to 1.58 bits then we will do both a distillation and also a LoRa. So these are two different approaches to improve the performance of the model.",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2021250793345548417",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "731697937",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2021955962563936369"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2021953188400685289",
      "created_at": "2026-02-12 14:23:00+00:00",
      "text": "Looking for sponsors to get GLM-5 running on ampere in 120gb for weights with 95% accuracy in agentic/coding workflows.\n\nsponsorship will buy compute for reproducing research \n\nI can't do this alone. \n\nhttps://t.co/rAz3mKFucV https://t.co/nMSCkCKr3y",
      "likes": 93,
      "retweets": 10,
      "replies": 6,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2021953188400685289",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2021947003383718256"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2021951029760405596",
      "created_at": "2026-02-12 14:14:26+00:00",
      "text": "I haven't used claude in forever https://t.co/otKyJV6xZ5",
      "likes": 44,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2021951029760405596",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2021947003383718256",
      "created_at": "2026-02-12 13:58:26+00:00",
      "text": "It's working... \n\nBut i am so compute starved right now, I can only experiment with 7b models. \n\n48 hours of work https://t.co/kxQttR8CTJ https://t.co/Cu78eJZgHp",
      "likes": 290,
      "retweets": 10,
      "replies": 18,
      "quotes": 2,
      "conversation_id": "2021947003383718256",

      "is_retweet": false,

        }
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "2021937607350116735",
      "created_at": "2026-02-12 13:21:06+00:00",
      "text": "Do you use AI for frontend development? \n\nLet me make your life easier. \n\nProvide a simple scope of what you want to change and ask the model to generate 10 html files, each with 4 variations of the feature.\n\nCherry pick from samples.\n\nThis saves countless hours reprompting https://t.co/BUuyIlojEI",
      "likes": 278,
      "retweets": 8,
      "replies": 18,
      "quotes": 2,
      "conversation_id": "2021937607350116735",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

    {
      "id": "2021881289322729787",
      "created_at": "2026-02-12 09:37:18+00:00",
      "text": "I gave my clanker a credit card, hetzner CLI access, namecheap, an email, a crypto wallet. \n\nLet's see how it goes. I used a digital card with 165$ monthly limit on it, no damage it can do. https://t.co/7eQOhrnXXq",

    },

    {
      "id": "2021871620999012378",
      "created_at": "2026-02-12 08:58:53+00:00",
      "text": "RT @louszbd: i felt agentic engineering era is coming\n\nclaude opus 4.6 and gpt-5.3 codex got me thinking coding models have entered a new e…",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 40,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2021871620999012378",

    {
      "id": "2021871381562950137",
      "created_at": "2026-02-12 08:57:56+00:00",
      "text": "Throwing another Hail Mary out there to the world.\n\nI want to work on:\n\n1. Applying and scaling novel compression methods for LLMs \n2. Implementing kernels for DSA on ampere hardware \n\nThis is my Ethereum address (works for all evm blockchains), I’m happy to share a Solana/BTC https://t.co/zmrL835PMq",
      "likes": 69,
      "retweets": 5,
      "replies": 12,
      "quotes": 5,
      "conversation_id": "2021871381562950137",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2021865182776230072",
      "created_at": "2026-02-12 08:33:18+00:00",
      "text": "My most delusional idea yet. \n\nImplement DSA support for Ampere sm86 architecture. Let’s see how good these models really are at coding. \n\nNovel compression research + ampere support = Deepseek at home. https://t.co/K67fMyw32f",
      "likes": 74,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 7,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2021865182776230072",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

      "id": "2021720729059045804",
      "created_at": "2026-02-11 22:59:18+00:00",
      "text": "@royjossfolk it takes time once you get a venv that works you save it and lock it with a venv, don't deal with it yourself run codex/opencode(free) and claude code to figure it out for you. \n\nThe speed difference is insane, 20k+ prefill speeds.",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2021708828937719952",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "264541978",

      "is_retweet": false,

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2021718777139015873",
      "created_at": "2026-02-11 22:51:32+00:00",
      "text": "@royjossfolk CUDA 12.8 + vllm v0.15.0 (use docker)\n\nOllama is too slow.",

      "id": "2021715800432779469",
      "created_at": "2026-02-11 22:39:43+00:00",
      "text": "@royjossfolk What's the issue exactly? \n\nyou need to use \n\ntp=4 pp=2 \ntool parser glm47 \nreasoning parser glm45",

      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2021708828937719952",

          "id": "2021713506614059303"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2021711704124465424",
      "created_at": "2026-02-11 22:23:26+00:00",
      "text": "@royjossfolk I'd happily look at any issues you run into just make an issue https://t.co/6eM8JChgl8",

    {
      "id": "2021711499488592156",
      "created_at": "2026-02-11 22:22:37+00:00",
      "text": "@royjossfolk I mean it works, I'm just not prioritizing making it as seemless to setup as lmstudio. \n\nTry it out, if u use claude code or codex or whatever, even opencode with kimi for free, tell them to set it up for you, should work in 15 mins.",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2021708828937719952",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "264541978",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2021711198471725352"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2021710890974773307",
      "created_at": "2026-02-11 22:20:12+00:00",
      "text": "Did you buy a Mac Mini for openclaw? Let me make your life better. \n\n1. Install Zed IDE\n2. settings.json and drop this: \"use_system_window_tabs\": true\n3. click + on the top, add all your repos \n4. click + do add remote directory\n5. enter your ip or tailscale network ip\n6. use https://t.co/n7FQVSJP5r",
      "likes": 676,
      "retweets": 49,
      "replies": 27,
      "quotes": 4,
      "conversation_id": "2021710890974773307",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

    {
      "id": "2021654377958875430",
      "created_at": "2026-02-11 18:35:38+00:00",
      "text": "Here's how I use agents to work on 8 projects a time \n\nI think this is a unique video, no one else is doing it like this. I promise you'll double your outputs. \nhttps://t.co/HUkxDIHWas",
      "likes": 85,
      "retweets": 7,
      "replies": 11,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2021654377958875430",

    },
    {
      "id": "2021630064610406773",
      "created_at": "2026-02-11 16:59:02+00:00",
      "text": "Let me make your life better. \n\n1. Open https://t.co/HhzcaldVyt \n2. Sign in with X \n3. Click your PFP \n4. Click tasks\n5. Define a task and the interval to run it at.\n6. It'll run and email/notify you at your desired interval\n\ni use this for\n\n- Sales\n- Research\n- X content\n- etc. https://t.co/LYH7sA3Ga9",
      "likes": 82,
      "retweets": 5,
      "replies": 6,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2021630064610406773",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

      "id": "2021509940054274052",
      "created_at": "2026-02-11 09:01:42+00:00",
      "text": "@VictorTaelin Swift is one of their weakest points, actually. \n\nIt’s been miserable trying to build anything on IOS, I don’t care to learn it. \n\nReact native would be better but then its react 😭 and you get useEffect slop everywhere. \n\nIt’s so over",

    {
      "id": "2021352678627152306",
      "created_at": "2026-02-10 22:36:48+00:00",
      "text": "@0xTwat @shawmakesmagic Thanks for pointing this out",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,

    {
      "id": "2021303625562091717",
      "created_at": "2026-02-10 19:21:52+00:00",
      "text": "This is how I automate almost everything (with varied degrees of success)\n\nI have been using mostly Kimi these days and have automated everything from:\n\n- organizing 19k emails down to 300\n- provisioning cloud environments\n- filling out government form\n\nhttps://t.co/JwZI1iMP7a",
      "likes": 91,
      "retweets": 6,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2021303625562091717",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {

      "text": "@joesuarzag @shawmakesmagic I use way too much tokens to use OpenRouter, my monthly token spend based on API usage is 8-10k usd",

    {
      "id": "2021287509540421734",
      "created_at": "2026-02-10 18:17:50+00:00",
      "text": "My only use for Claude now is Clawdbot, it is literally just not the best at anything anymore, just an overall good model. \n\n- Kimi-K2.5 is better at automating computer use, especially browser use, I am using Parchi more than Claude chrome extension &amp; BETTER at frontend\n\n- GPT https://t.co/tpgLNTh3Cn",
      "likes": 711,
      "retweets": 39,
      "replies": 60,
      "quotes": 6,
      "conversation_id": "2021287509540421734",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2021282695037243555",
      "created_at": "2026-02-10 17:58:42+00:00",
      "text": "@ZenMagnets Already reproduced the example, scaling it to larger models will be a challenge, but yes. \n\nI have 5 other papers i want to apply that try to do the same thing differently. One will hit.",
      "likes": 20,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2021250793345548417",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "61599733",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2021266853163606367"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2021282367516672097",
      "created_at": "2026-02-10 17:57:24+00:00",
      "text": "@StamblerLev I'll look into it, i am a tourist here just interested in applying the research.",

      "retweets": 0,

    },
    {
      "id": "2021282247815368727",
      "created_at": "2026-02-10 17:56:56+00:00",
      "text": "@pierrelezan yes",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,

      "conversation_id": "2021250793345548417",

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2021250793345548417",
      "created_at": "2026-02-10 15:51:56+00:00",
      "text": "I am working on implementing a new quantization method that should reduce hardware needs by 92% with relatively low loss in performance \n\nHere's the research paper, very interesting.. \nhttps://t.co/AxxTzulrzB\n\nIf this works out we can run Deepseek on 1 rtx 6000 https://t.co/o0qkCjKPaj",
      "likes": 831,
      "retweets": 64,
      "replies": 46,
      "quotes": 8,
      "conversation_id": "2021250793345548417",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2021190442348335281",
      "created_at": "2026-02-10 11:52:08+00:00",
      "text": "@wassollichhier @AxcanNathan @CudaSlayer https://t.co/pydvKT4HtU\n\n```\n\nvllm serve \\\n+  /path/GLM-4.7-REAP-218B-A32B-AWQ-4bit \\\n+  --served-model-name glm-4.7 \\\n+  --tensor-parallel-size 8 \\\n+  --max-model-len 200000 \\\n+  --max-num-seqs 4 \\\n+  --gpu-memory-utilization 0.9 \\\n+  --kv-cache-dtype fp8 \\\n+",
      "likes": 4,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2021166238647427122",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "47870624",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2021189948536127795"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2021187319169003766",
      "created_at": "2026-02-10 11:39:43+00:00",
      "text": "@AxcanNathan @CudaSlayer They run models in full precision I need to prune and quantize the glm-4.x series to run it. \n\nAlso support OSS",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2021166238647427122",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1563793183829622785",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2021187124465184888"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2021170196765925687",
      "created_at": "2026-02-10 10:31:41+00:00",
      "text": "@voiddotdev @shawmakesmagic Codex is much more intelligent and better at coding than Claude, that use to come at the cost of speed and personality but they've changed that drastically. \n\nGPT-5.3-Codex is just as fast as Opus if not faster, there's a new personality mode \"friendly\" you can access via their",
      "likes": 4,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2021116908087746629",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "2010741281438474240",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2021169536968364147"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2021167169094971670",
      "created_at": "2026-02-10 10:19:39+00:00",
      "text": "@CudaSlayer MiniMax\nGLM\nStep \nGPT-OSS",
      "likes": 9,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2021166238647427122",

    {
      "id": "2021166277654421900",
      "created_at": "2026-02-10 10:16:06+00:00",
      "text": "@Niyxuis @shawmakesmagic I use Droid, Pi and Opencode. \n\nSo I can switch between them all on the fly, so MiniMax is great for a quick fix, scraping data, cleaning up (it’s very fast) \n\nGLM is great at devops &amp; deep reasoning especially in Droid since they’ve built significantly around it. \n\nClaude is",
      "likes": 24,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2021116908087746629",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1254242216459087873",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2021157995372003595"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2021139603831366085",

    {

      "text": "@shawmakesmagic I stopped using cursor long ago, Zed + Codex Pro + Opus Max + Kimi 40$ + 10$ MiniMax + 6$ Zai + 20$ droid + 20$ cursor \n\n496$ p/m",

    {
      "id": "2021010604647915979",
      "created_at": "2026-02-09 23:57:31+00:00",
      "text": "Quantising Step-3.5-flash for vllm on Prime Intellect\n\nWish me luck. https://t.co/bNRKbBh1hd",
      "likes": 84,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 10,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2021010604647915979",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,

    },
    {
      "id": "2021010145728446864",
      "created_at": "2026-02-09 23:55:41+00:00",
      "text": "https://t.co/BzwpAVV9OO",
      "likes": 10,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2021010063083909303",

    },
    {
      "id": "2021010063083909303",
      "created_at": "2026-02-09 23:55:22+00:00",
      "text": "Parchi my browser extension has a few new features. \n\n- Multi model orchestration \n    - Use multiple models with various profiles to automate tasks let a non-vision model send screenshots to vision models \n    -  Deep research swarms \n\n-  Relay mode + cli tool + skill \n    - https://t.co/o7SHA1Cb2F",
      "likes": 44,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 4,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2021010063083909303",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2021008385877897609",
      "created_at": "2026-02-09 23:48:42+00:00",
      "text": "- ROCm support \n- TTS / STT  (call mode like chatgpt with ace-step &amp; whisper-v3-large) \n- Image / Video support \n\nAlso have something really special cooking, you will be able to share a link with a friend/public broadcast for someone to share GPUs with to run larger model from https://t.co/kJcjboPLjJ",
      "likes": 56,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 6,
      "quotes": 2,
      "conversation_id": "2021008385877897609",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2020983496420782232",
      "created_at": "2026-02-09 22:09:48+00:00",
      "text": "@badlogicgames GPT-5.3-Codex medium with \"friendly personality\" does decent at",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2020981776731279847",

    {
      "id": "2020977835134025916",
      "created_at": "2026-02-09 21:47:18+00:00",
      "text": "@Everlier @shawmakesmagic They have 50+% cache hits and probably cache queries between users at scale",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2020944263224361411",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "133797694",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2020976498552004918"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2020973878630031433",
      "created_at": "2026-02-09 21:31:35+00:00",
      "text": "@shawmakesmagic GPT-5.2 was 28 tokens a second \nGPT-5.2-Codex 34 \n\n20 tps generation and something like 230 tps prefill means that it’ll take 15 mins to process 1 million in and 2.6 hours to process 1M out (in the case of 0 cache hits)\n\nKimi gets 25tps if it’s serving 1 session, down to to 20tps",
      "likes": 45,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 4,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2020944263224361411",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1830340867737178112",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2020944263224361411"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2020879962568266215",
      "created_at": "2026-02-09 15:18:23+00:00",
      "text": "I am 0xSero on \n\n- X\n- Youtube \n- Reddit \n- HuggingFace \n- Github \n\nVery grateful to X for giving me the handle, I worked so hard for this. \n\nAlso I finally got the ability to \"Earn\" on YouTube. https://t.co/YExbkIzeIH",
      "likes": 52,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 7,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2020879962568266215",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

      "created_at": "2026-02-08 21:47:31+00:00",
      "text": "@RyanCraigMartin You'll love it, you can use the free antigravity models via cliproxyapi or opencode and plug it into zed",

    {
      "id": "2020614920447176984",
      "created_at": "2026-02-08 21:45:12+00:00",
      "text": "Guys, I just tried Step-3.5-Flash locally. \n\n- 200B params \n- 11B active params \n- ON llama.cpp 600tps prefill 58tps generation \n- SOOOOO good. \n\nI had it setup my cliproxyapi and it did it 1 shot, no support needed. \n\nIncredible model, try it out https://t.co/5KWrYc2ytq",
      "likes": 288,
      "retweets": 11,
      "replies": 31,
      "quotes": 2,
      "conversation_id": "2020614920447176984",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2020604531923665392",
      "created_at": "2026-02-08 21:03:56+00:00",
      "text": "@carloslondrez @dhh You get great prefill tps but for generation both the DGX and Mac are weak",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2020422289892745384",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "2235124310",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2020548174356582875"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2020604353745494285",
      "created_at": "2026-02-08 21:03:13+00:00",
      "text": "@DenLoginoff @dhh I need me 8 of those. I’m going to sell of my stack soon and pick up 2",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,

    {
      "id": "2020604244097925471",
      "created_at": "2026-02-08 21:02:47+00:00",
      "text": "@alexhooketh @dhh 6000s \n3090s\nA40s \n\nEtc etc",

    {
      "id": "2020529059134869875",
      "created_at": "2026-02-08 16:04:01+00:00",
      "text": "@bernhard_me @AlexFinn You can rent a Ferrari while driving a Sedan",
      "likes": 17,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,

    {
      "id": "2020480609148084654",
      "created_at": "2026-02-08 12:51:30+00:00",
      "text": "I checked the OpenCode desktop app today and it has gotten significantly better. \n\nAll the existing desktop apps don't support opening files and checking your file tree for some reason. Kudos to their team &amp; Adam. https://t.co/xoEOnTON2R",
      "likes": 284,
      "retweets": 5,
      "replies": 26,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2020480609148084654",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2020480312753422719",
      "created_at": "2026-02-08 12:50:19+00:00",
      "text": "Today's youtube video is about Zed IDE, which I believe is the best for AI, it's the only IDE that let's you manage multiple projects while you code still\n\nIt's AI framework (ACP) is top tier, gives you a cursor like exp for free I recommend it.\nhttps://t.co/VJGG8lKxRT https://t.co/6UrlCtOh2M",
      "likes": 171,
      "retweets": 4,
      "replies": 12,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2020480312753422719",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2020457629898756560",
      "created_at": "2026-02-08 11:20:11+00:00",
      "text": "Parchi has gotten nicer. \n\nI added a relay feature, yet to be battle tested. \nI think I want to add multi-model, workflows and downloads next. https://t.co/UlsGdr508X",
      "likes": 15,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2020457629898756560",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2020444851704070568",

    {
      "id": "2020240100286718248",
      "created_at": "2026-02-07 20:55:48+00:00",
      "text": "The most aesthetic lab. https://t.co/MMEC89QlEr",

    {
      "id": "2020239139656872375",
      "created_at": "2026-02-07 20:51:59+00:00",
      "text": "Every 5 minutes I come across another kill application of AI. \n\nIf that’s what they meant by tired of winning I’m raw by now https://t.co/O1koPGpXXr",
      "likes": 43,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 5,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2020239139656872375",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2020237835912315081",
      "created_at": "2026-02-07 20:46:48+00:00",
      "text": "vLLM-studio will fully support AMD GPUs and roc-m within the next 7 days. \n\nDevelopment started today. Thanks to a generous grant from HotAisle.\n\nIf you’re interested in getting this running on your hardware DM. https://t.co/EGLI4ePAZL https://t.co/7BHH5i2jVN",
      "likes": 132,
      "retweets": 10,
      "replies": 13,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2020237835912315081",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2018831898068582867"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2020233081966457304",
      "created_at": "2026-02-07 20:27:55+00:00",
      "text": "This is the way, your data is valuable and currently easily accessible download it and let a local model rip. https://t.co/ML9HUIqLaM",
      "likes": 101,
      "retweets": 3,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2020233081966457304",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2020227911941525671"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2020227066952647000",
      "created_at": "2026-02-07 20:04:01+00:00",
      "text": "@VictorTaelin It’s gobbling up ram &amp; cpu time like crazy, I stopped using it besides for things only Claude can do, I went from constantly running out of usage on my 20x max plan to barely using 50% of it in less than a month. \n\nI love the model I think the harness has gotten so slop",
      "likes": 15,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2020213554771587213",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "272239060",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2020222190143078478"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2020225062981255548",
      "created_at": "2026-02-07 19:56:03+00:00",
      "text": "I can’t stress enough how much this world is going to change over the next 5 years. \n\nI think the people using these agents can see that while they are currently incapable of fully replacing people they have already made it possible to reduce headcounts significantly.\n\nThe last https://t.co/WQw4Cfh7TY",
      "likes": 96,
      "retweets": 5,
      "replies": 7,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2020225062981255548",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2020139129460134216"
        }
      ]
    },

      "id": "2020122231901241706",
      "created_at": "2026-02-07 13:07:26+00:00",
      "text": "New default model in openclaw is GTP-5.3-Codex in friendly personality mode. \n\nSince they doubled the rate limits it’s more than worth it. Model has gotten better at computer use. https://t.co/OpDiECNAWa",
      "likes": 78,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 11,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2020122231901241706",

      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

    {
      "id": "2020060866436329673",
      "created_at": "2026-02-07 09:03:36+00:00",
      "text": "I have been using VPS for AI over a year now. It’s fine but it’s such a pita to manage. \n\nI dislike the just get a VPS crowd it could be a good idea tho https://t.co/mOgNditCDB",

    },
    {
      "id": "2020060006519804382",
      "created_at": "2026-02-07 09:00:11+00:00",
      "text": "@OwenDFowler @sama The reality of the situation is that should be up to the human to consider. Almost every time this happens it is working on a separate module in the repo.",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2019813802049696064",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1328167567672819713",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,

        {
          "type": "replied_to",

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2020059673194266901",
      "created_at": "2026-02-07 08:58:51+00:00",
      "text": "This is real, and there are much much bigger players getting into this game. \n\nApple specifically will win the AI hardware race https://t.co/CS9GNNIAnN",
      "likes": 165,
      "retweets": 10,

    {
      "id": "2019857589341663465",
      "created_at": "2026-02-06 19:35:51+00:00",
      "text": "This bad boy came in so clutch today. \n\nThe internet provider where I live lost service for 8 hours today, that didn’t slow me down 1 bit.\n\nNow here’s the problem, I need more GPUs, I need to get Kimi running locally. That’d be GG https://t.co/3xBN0jxOAg",
      "likes": 131,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 32,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2019857589341663465",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2019857141641941447",
      "created_at": "2026-02-06 19:34:04+00:00",
      "text": "I lost my internet for 8 hours today, one of the worst days of my life 🥴 \n\nImagine how hard life is as an online worker when no online.. \n\nOne of the best usecases for local AI NGL",
      "likes": 31,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2019857141641941447",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2019829046763483580",
      "created_at": "2026-02-06 17:42:26+00:00",
      "text": "@sama 1. Make them faster \n2. Make them optionally more agreeable or train an agreeable version\n3. Codex for me still freaks out if any files are changed by another model while codex is running it’s very annoying\n4. Make gpt-5.x-pro available to pro members",
      "likes": 17,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2019813802049696064",

        }
      ]
    },

      "id": "2019682459315802127",
      "created_at": "2026-02-06 07:59:56+00:00",

    {
      "id": "2019477220935246014",
      "created_at": "2026-02-05 18:24:24+00:00",
      "text": "I am way more excited about GPT-5.3-Codex than any other model including the latest Opus.\n\nThe speed of the OAI models has at least doubled in the last month, and having the ability to go between 🤓 mode and 😎 mode is huge.\n\nI have crossed over to the right side, where what I https://t.co/69tmVIfjva",
      "likes": 53,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 8,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2019477220935246014",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2019473488642470120"
        }
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "2019375993538916673",
      "created_at": "2026-02-05 11:42:09+00:00",
      "text": "Been using GLM-4.7-Flash for my Clawdbot it works just fine, it's keeping track of the news for me, creating PDFs on trends every 2 hours\n\nIt's managing it's browser, for a model running on so little hardware I wasn't expecting it to handle the types of tasks that it does https://t.co/4pS94B9aLi",
      "likes": 150,
      "retweets": 7,
      "replies": 17,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2019375993538916673",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2019359419260432449",
      "created_at": "2026-02-05 10:36:18+00:00",

    {
      "id": "2019336953921995231",
      "created_at": "2026-02-05 09:07:01+00:00",
      "text": "@Alex_Car12 @willccbb 1. You don’t need to use the ACP panel just use the terminal\n2. You can add your provider and api keys DIRECTLY into the Zed agent which gives you the full history \n3. The window tab feature is the main reason this is my daily driver now. I can have 10 projects in 1 window",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2018924175633908156",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "848807305093283842",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2019333739013112160"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2019329319282024850",
      "created_at": "2026-02-05 08:36:41+00:00",
      "text": "Still thinking about this.\n\nI was a bit anxious going in, I am very confident in what I bring to the table, and genuinely think I am on the more cracked end of the agentic coding niche.\n\nWhat helped a lot was writing pseudo code which took me 15 minutes to solve in natural https://t.co/bMJ1YXBsMd https://t.co/9pucrOMQbz",
      "likes": 81,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 7,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2019329319282024850",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2019183385663389991"
        }
      ]
    },
