    {
      "id": "2019203065317060983",
      "created_at": "2026-02-05 00:15:00+00:00",
      "text": "@willccbb Sir I recommend you check out Zed with window mode, I am in love. \n\n1. IDE \n2. Zed auto tab \n3. Can run 20+ project tabs and then however many terminals in each tab \n4. Never crashes\n5. ACP lets you bring in your tools \n6. Looks awesome",
      "likes": 37,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 7,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2018924175633908156",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "3064259332",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2018924175633908156"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2019195895577997723",
      "created_at": "2026-02-04 23:46:31+00:00",
      "text": "@nummanali As secure as you can be, there's still a slightly elevated risk as 1 device can access a bunch of others, so if the OG device is compromised for unrelated reasons then other devices will as well",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2019187296944165293",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1629934761497047041",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2019195545299087603"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2019192050193952930",
      "created_at": "2026-02-04 23:31:14+00:00",
      "text": "@0xTaylor_ I mostly agree but i have been using these things long enough and if you're not able to demonstrate reasoning and the ability to keep + correct a mental model of the code it'll be impossible for anyone to work with you.",
      "likes": 8,

      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2019183385663389991",

        }
      ]
    },
    {

    {
      "id": "2019190891043311730",
      "created_at": "2026-02-04 23:26:37+00:00",
      "text": "@frenchtoasterss It's really fucking slow for me",
      "likes": 1,

    {
      "id": "2019189154194559026",
      "created_at": "2026-02-04 23:19:43+00:00",
      "text": "@0xTaylor_ I think basic fluency in logic and code is fair to ask.",
      "likes": 10,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2019183385663389991",

      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,

    {
      "id": "2019187296944165293",
      "created_at": "2026-02-04 23:12:20+00:00",
      "text": "Let me make your life better. \n\nDo you want to use the GUI of any device from your laptop/macbook pc? SSH is great, but sometimes you want a GUI. \n\n1. Take the devices you want to hook up \n2. Install tailscale (free) on all of them \n3. Install NoMachine on both the controlling https://t.co/gxXkBuaYDl",
      "likes": 93,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 13,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2019187296944165293",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2019183385663389991",
      "created_at": "2026-02-04 22:56:48+00:00",
      "text": "I did my first coding interview in so long today, I think I invented the worst solution to the game of life. \n\nIt’s interesting how much my ability to manually code has corroded, I remember being to blast through leetcode easy in 5-15 minutes, and medium in an hour\n\nIt’s okay tho",
      "likes": 77,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 10,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2019183385663389991",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2019175648065966294",
      "created_at": "2026-02-04 22:26:03+00:00",
      "text": "I’ve been vindicated and will continue to feel so. https://t.co/7fEl32Rzd5",
      "likes": 11,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2019175648065966294",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "1886192184808149383"
        }
      ]
    },
    {

    {
      "id": "2018992019373449343",
      "created_at": "2026-02-04 10:16:23+00:00",
      "text": "@JustBill1182 Agent swarms are just tools that delegate tasks to another agent. \n\nKimi is very good at this, so if the harness enables it then it’ll use it perfectly.",
      "likes": 6,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2018985639782596964",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1457855185842233344",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2018987501336072368"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2018991785024819555",
      "created_at": "2026-02-04 10:15:27+00:00",
      "text": "@marbogdanski I have 2 PSUs\n\n#1 - 1600w Corsair \n#2 - 1000w Corsair \n\nI use a psu2psu adapter cable.\n\n#1 powers: \n- 4x power capped 3090s (150w each)\n- CPU + mobo + ram + fans \n\n#2 powers:\n- 4x power capped 3090s (150w each)\n\nI use gen4 risers, and 2 splitters.\n\nCheap on AliExpress at 20% cost https://t.co/jdJHMOwCf8",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2018968486345605235",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "22919071",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2018970053019136094"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2018990215604347374",
      "created_at": "2026-02-04 10:09:13+00:00",
      "text": "@deeperflows I power cap it at 150w it’s relatively quiet.",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2018968486345605235",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1116610601885716480",

      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2018980363205534053"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2018985639782596964",
      "created_at": "2026-02-04 09:51:02+00:00",

    {
      "id": "2018970941011734974",
      "created_at": "2026-02-04 08:52:37+00:00",
      "text": "As much as people dislike Nikita I think he’s the best head of product at any company. \n\nObsessed with work, good at standing his ground, working on hard problems. \n\nLove and appreciation for making this site more usable. https://t.co/dDgFp674Jk https://t.co/raKCUQcGIG",
      "likes": 146,
      "retweets": 3,
      "replies": 7,
      "quotes": 0,

      "is_retweet": false,

          "id": "2018959889545216370"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2018969920357241056",
      "created_at": "2026-02-04 08:48:34+00:00",
      "text": "@BLUECOW009 Nothing, I have been using it for over a month, and have found tremendous value in it. \n\n- personal assistant \n- idea generator \n- report generator \n- device manager\n\nIt’s got some flaws: \n\n- security on personal devices is risky (get a vps or give it an old machine) \n- not as",
      "likes": 62,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 5,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2018885065695285305",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1511097375649107970",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2018885065695285305"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2018968486345605235",
      "created_at": "2026-02-04 08:42:52+00:00",
      "text": "In less than 3 months this has gotten 4-8x more efficient. I might do another nanochat training run this week.\n\nMaybe I can train it on some of my personal data too, I wonder how it’d respond. \n\nThis is my most prized post by the way. https://t.co/yj1IP0VeE6",
      "likes": 75,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 5,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2018968486345605235",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "1978664943827190116"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2018967750975332706",
      "created_at": "2026-02-04 08:39:57+00:00",

    {
      "id": "2018967482011107384",
      "created_at": "2026-02-04 08:38:52+00:00",
      "text": "@alakazam1314 Supply will catch up to demand over the next few years especially as we get more tailor made chips for inference. \n\nSimilar to ASIC Miners, I remember when people were using GPUs to mine crypto… now no one does that. \n\nAlso still the price of electronics/tech over 20 years has",

      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,

          "id": "2018966240560324659"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2018965042889101584",
      "created_at": "2026-02-04 08:29:11+00:00",
      "text": "@udaysy @NoahGreenSnow They require different approaches, Codex thrives in long sessions with very detailed instructions, it works much better in larger projects and tends to use the code that exists as opposed to making surface level changes to satisfy the user. \n\nI love Opus. It's very good and i am",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2018836133187351028",

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2018962960190738563",
      "created_at": "2026-02-04 08:20:54+00:00",
      "text": "@NoahGreenSnow Claude Code as a harness is good enough for 95% of people. \n\nIt’s got a ton of features more than needed, it’s got a great model behind it too. Not knocking on Anthropic.\n\nHowever:\n\n1. The context problem: Claude code crams so much trash into the model (60k tokens by default) so",
      "likes": 6,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2018836133187351028",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1611240039173734401",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2018961121382711375"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2018961613605179668",
      "created_at": "2026-02-04 08:15:33+00:00",
      "text": "@dpshade Opencode is good enough and has more features. Pi is so simple and fast, doesn’t do anything silly. \n\nI think there’s no system prompt even, lol. \nI recommend checking: Codex, Droid, OpenCode and Pi out.  \n\nEach does things in their own unique ways and covers 99% of everything in",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2018836133187351028",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "168517526",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2018957616899051941"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2018961011118571965",
      "created_at": "2026-02-04 08:13:10+00:00",
      "text": "This man cannot lose, anyone in their right mind would have stopped months ago. \n\nAt this rate GPT-3 will cost 100$ to train next year lol. https://t.co/i8GWQfmgk0",

      "created_at": "2026-02-04 00:01:08+00:00",
      "text": "@AlexFinn 10$ - Minimax \n6$ - GLM \n40$ - Kimi \n40$ - 2x GPT subs \n100$ - Claude \n0$ - GLM-4.7\n0$ - openrouter/free (routes you to best free models) \n\nLiterally more than you can use in a month regardless how much you spam them",
      "likes": 108,
      "retweets": 4,

    {

      "text": "Droid, Pi and Opencode I will shill to my last breath. Best tools in the space. https://t.co/B6ibaypPH9",

    {
      "id": "2018835272918798433",
      "created_at": "2026-02-03 23:53:31+00:00",
      "text": "There’s no excuse not to win, everything is easy now. You can be anything you want digitally. \n\nWe have never had more intelligence, support, information, opportunity waiting to be used. \n\nLosing is not an option. https://t.co/zpW34Fe1Zt",
      "likes": 26,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2018835272918798433",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2018834423853330532",
      "created_at": "2026-02-03 23:50:09+00:00",
      "text": "@AlexFinn Nothing is hard, and everything is easy if you spend a few hours working on it. \n\nReally that’s all there is to it",

      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2018827705945645177",

        {

    },

      "text": "I had so many people tell me this was a waste of time and money lol https://t.co/7dhhtZuDXH",

    {
      "id": "2018819536233939449",
      "created_at": "2026-02-03 22:50:59+00:00",
      "text": "This was an idea thought up by openclaw with Kimi-K2.5 and executed by Opus 4.5\n\nA content creation machine, battles of the clankers. \n\nThe idea: \"put 2 models against each other and have them produce visual output using ascii\"\n\nUsing Clawdbot, peekaboo and the heartbeat feature, https://t.co/I34WMe5hNr",
      "likes": 42,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 6,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2018819536233939449",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2018816283182268524",
      "created_at": "2026-02-03 22:38:04+00:00",
      "text": "I mean look Qwen-next-coder is interesting to say the least. Not an easy task for any model. https://t.co/91tkSp9eIp",
      "likes": 34,
      "retweets": 3,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2018816283182268524",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

      "id": "2018655560410046834",
      "created_at": "2026-02-03 11:59:25+00:00",
      "text": "@stableAPY Yes I already did this you’d need more than 16gb if you’re trying to run local models minimum I’d recommend is 64gb and a sweet spot is 96gb \n\nOtherwise the device will crawl, the model will be dumbed down and you won’t get full context",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2018653356559052910",

      "id": "2018612268519633403",
      "created_at": "2026-02-03 09:07:23+00:00",
      "text": "@cryptopunk7213 - If it’s trained the way the earlier sonnets were trained it’ll be slop central\n- We’ve had subagent swarms for 6+ months \n- subagents have potential but are pretty useless outside of a very few select situations",

      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,

      "id": "2018466754834706771",
      "created_at": "2026-02-02 23:29:10+00:00",
      "text": "Ray and I go deep into local AI, hardware, and various usecases for private AI. \n\nThis was a great really enjoyed this conversation, especially since I've been listening to his podcast for so long. \n\nhttps://t.co/T6SsKCpNYP",

    {
      "id": "2018447753635074472",
      "created_at": "2026-02-02 22:13:40+00:00",
      "text": "RT @HotAisle: i could help, but i need cash. we've got the skills to get this up and running and moving forward as fast as humanly possible.",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2018447753635074472",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,

          "type": "retweeted",
          "id": "2018433166894366728"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2018435827529826365",
      "created_at": "2026-02-02 21:26:16+00:00",
      "text": "Let me make your workflows a bit easier\n\n1. Get a GPT sub (you should do this if you code esp)\n2. Install the Codex app \n3. Setup your project root directory \n4. Setup an automation 24h Interval\n5. \"Analyze all my git history changes over the week, give me recommendations and https://t.co/MNZEZaUMT3",
      "likes": 216,
      "retweets": 7,
      "replies": 11,
      "quotes": 2,
      "conversation_id": "2018435827529826365",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

    {
      "id": "2018401527220601044",
      "created_at": "2026-02-02 19:09:58+00:00",
      "text": "I interviewed the founder and lead dev behind ElizaOS. \nEliza was one of the first trending agent frameworks, it was remarkably similar to Openclaw/Clawdbot. \n\nIt was early to the scene and did a lot of things right, I highly recommend checking it out.\n\nhttps://t.co/4oz8PNgdK8",
      "likes": 18,
      "retweets": 4,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2018401527220601044",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2018365036004012358",
      "created_at": "2026-02-02 16:44:58+00:00",
      "text": "@Kaiyes_ I just don’t like the way RN works tbh, it’s probably best to do so but I am prioritising my personal experience then I’ll look into more solutions",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2018097892964085761",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1526972136",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2018364364659781898"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2018363591494193652",
      "created_at": "2026-02-02 16:39:14+00:00",
      "text": "@SunnyBOT0 @RayFernando1337 I built it so it can but no simple to use, if you pay for an AI sub ask 1 to set it up, or you can use bigpickle on opencode for free (GLM-4.6) and it should be able to handle it",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2018361652098261277",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "2787953543",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2018362402501222439"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2018362318640279881",

    {
      "id": "2018252854570398147",
      "created_at": "2026-02-02 09:19:12+00:00",
      "text": "@nerveband Pollutes the local running context I can probably spawn a new session to do this but I like seeing what’s happening",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2018101731591176325",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "621018356",

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2018251350782435672",
      "created_at": "2026-02-02 09:13:13+00:00",
      "text": "As someone who really wants this to work, it’s going to be a slopfest, completely dysfunctional! \n\nEvery subagent will increase the distance between what we want and what we will get, increase the likelihood of issues, decrease traceability, etc.. \n\nReally hope I’m wrong https://t.co/iEQyyi1OTh",
      "likes": 47,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 10,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2018251350782435672",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2018075111014977868"
        }
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "2018103048044687799",
      "created_at": "2026-02-01 23:23:55+00:00",
      "text": "@SexyTechNews use awq4bit + 8bit kvcache and vllm, should be able to fit the whole thing",
      "likes": 3,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2018027097084588503",

    {
      "id": "2018101731591176325",
      "created_at": "2026-02-01 23:18:41+00:00",
      "text": "Let me save you hours of testing frontends. \n\nIf you're ever working on a front-end, instead of writing tests, and adding puppeteer slop to your repo\n\n1. Get an llm to write you https://t.co/VyEqZzb7eD with whatever needs to be tested \n2. Copy that, go to browser\n3. Open https://t.co/i15pvdQJrj",
      "likes": 1242,
      "retweets": 49,
      "replies": 45,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2018101731591176325",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2018097892964085761",
      "created_at": "2026-02-01 23:03:26+00:00",
      "text": "vllm-studio now has a macos and ios app. \n\nThe models aren't so great at Swift, if any of you have tips let me know. This will be a world class way to run local models by end of year. https://t.co/u3IbeR2qKm",
      "likes": 49,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 5,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2018097892964085761",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

    {
      "id": "2018062866096472305",
      "created_at": "2026-02-01 20:44:15+00:00",
      "text": "Openclaw made a rag of all my codebases and added it as a slash command without being prompted to. https://t.co/JcQkKTmDDX",
      "likes": 38,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 5,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2018062866096472305",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2018058850134303189",
      "created_at": "2026-02-01 20:28:18+00:00",
      "text": "I built a a Reddit narrative builder with openclaw. \n\nIt uses Ahmad’s Reddit recursive scraping method to take a topic\n\nExamples: \n\n- AI subreddit sentiment analysis \n- Sports games, bets, events \n- Food &amp; travel recommendations \n\n&gt; It will identify the top associated subreddits https://t.co/XmAzxbg8tP",
      "likes": 79,
      "retweets": 3,
      "replies": 8,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2018058850134303189",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

    {
      "id": "2018027097084588503",
      "created_at": "2026-02-01 18:22:07+00:00",
      "text": "For those with 24-96gb of vram/unified memory. I highly recommend trying out GLM-4.7-Flash.\n\nIt is the best model up to 100B params, I showed how I use it and what it can do. \n\nGLM-4.7-Flash - Claude Code at home??\nhttps://t.co/y4zUSBzteh",
      "likes": 281,
      "retweets": 14,
      "replies": 29,
      "quotes": 3,
      "conversation_id": "2018027097084588503",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2018026529574297968",
      "created_at": "2026-02-01 18:19:52+00:00",
      "text": "@0xSpuddy I see what you’re saying, I think you should try Droid and Pi. They have client side support for stuff like this via skills..\n\nDroid is closer to Claude code and imo better",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2017973710695678050",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "852649657826258944",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2018025774888947882"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2018025464011325662",
      "created_at": "2026-02-01 18:15:38+00:00",
      "text": "I turned on subscriptions on X, I’d love to take rabbit hole requests. If you’re interested lmk and I will see what I can do",
      "likes": 9,
      "retweets": 0,

      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2018025464011325662",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,

    },
    {
      "id": "2018023675090370673",
      "created_at": "2026-02-01 18:08:31+00:00",
      "text": "@0xSpuddy Ah, no. It gives it the ability to do that with a harness like Claude code by the way you can run login with Claude code and it’ll let you connect 3rd party models now I think",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2017973710695678050",

    {
      "id": "2018019370438385736",
      "created_at": "2026-02-01 17:51:25+00:00",
      "text": "@0xSpuddy Oyoyoy sir:\n\n1. GLM-4.7-flash \n2. GLM-4.5-Air\n3. GPT-OSS-120B \n\nRun them with full context on vLLM if you can, use fp8 kv cache \n\n—————\n\nIf you have 192gb that opens the door for GLM-4.7-Reap and MiniMax-M2.1 this is where it truly starts getting Sonnet-like.\n\nRun these on",
      "likes": 54,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 5,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2017973710695678050",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "852649657826258944",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2017973710695678050"
        }
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "2017949701593513994",
      "created_at": "2026-02-01 13:14:35+00:00",
      "text": "@kr0der Good question, that's definitely a huge con. I don't know if i need to but we will see, looking into it now.",
      "likes": 3,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2017944775110910107",

    },
    {
      "id": "2017944775110910107",
      "created_at": "2026-02-01 12:55:00+00:00",
      "text": "I am ripping out all my ai-sdk/agent/filesystem slopgic and replacing it with Pi-ai, a copy of pods, and agent-core.\n\nIt’s very well written. Wish I dug deeper earlier save myself the hassle. \n\nhttps://t.co/WOj0oBfQvW https://t.co/4kb1WRjes1",
      "likes": 329,
      "retweets": 8,
      "replies": 17,
      "quotes": 5,
      "conversation_id": "2017944775110910107",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2017913139824644150",
      "created_at": "2026-02-01 10:49:18+00:00",
      "text": "vllm-studio now supports llama.cpp and GPT-OSS fucking harmony is such a pain.. \n\nthe model itself is very interesting, though. https://t.co/3N9OUePyKE",
      "likes": 31,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 5,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2017913139824644150",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2017907852120060389",
      "created_at": "2026-02-01 10:28:17+00:00",
      "text": "Do you want the best web-scraper out there? \n\n1. Set Opus as main model \n2. Set haiku for subagents \n3. Turn on chrome plugin \n4. Give it a few search api envs, costs cents\n5. Prompt it to batch scraping targets to haiku subagents\n6. Tell it to output JSON responses. \n\nVery good https://t.co/5DGl6ZgenK",
      "likes": 1335,
      "retweets": 85,
      "replies": 35,
      "quotes": 2,
      "conversation_id": "2017907852120060389",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2017888206457377088",

      "text": "Thailand btw https://t.co/7ZoUQiZtFd",

      "conversation_id": "2017888206457377088",

    {
      "id": "2017747249401319866",
      "created_at": "2026-01-31 23:50:06+00:00",
      "text": "I have everything I could have ever wanted and things I never knew were in reach.\n\nI am so grateful to have lived even a week in the best time on this planet. \n\n- My family \n- My work \n- My self\n\nAll my property, what a dream. I feel like this extends to us all we are blessed https://t.co/EuQ8CZhQJM",
      "likes": 77,

    },

      "id": "2017717054686495036",
      "created_at": "2026-01-31 21:50:07+00:00",
      "text": "I just nuked 30 different apps of my Macbook, and took all my old code, cleared the node_modules and moved them into an archive. \n\nMy device has never been messier, but finally tending to the land https://t.co/zfXBpQuGZ3",

      "retweets": 0,

    },

    {
      "id": "2017699685239079423",
      "created_at": "2026-01-31 20:41:06+00:00",
      "text": "🦞 I caved \n\nOpenclaws is genuinely useful and impressive but it needs its own environment.\n\n6-700$ is a good price for an always on assistant. I have a Linux box but I would rather get it all setup for the robot explicitly. https://t.co/TsWHjRmytG",
      "likes": 267,
      "retweets": 3,
      "replies": 51,
      "quotes": 4,
      "conversation_id": "2017699685239079423",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2017690964622561776",
      "created_at": "2026-01-31 20:06:27+00:00",

      "id": "2017674123565060375",
      "created_at": "2026-01-31 18:59:32+00:00",
      "text": "Hey, let me make your life easier.\n\n1. Go to Tailscale site\n2. Install the desktop app &amp; mobile app\n3. Hook them up together via vpn\n4. Go to Termius \n5. Install the mobile app \n6. Set up using your tailscale IP\n7. Now you can control your computer from phone w no exposed ports https://t.co/9CLy0sSzKh",

      "retweets": 120,

    {
      "id": "2017355896288936351",

      "text": "@thesrs02 It’s built to run locally or in the cloud the site and app can connect to any provider but it’ll do best with my backend",

    {
      "id": "2017320407615918227",
      "created_at": "2026-01-30 19:33:59+00:00",
      "text": "@thesrs02 This is vLLM-studio, it’s lmstudio for vLLM \n\n- usage and metrics \n- store model configs \n- discover models on hf\n- chat with agent mode",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2017023420202881524",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "2776840632",

      "referenced_tweets": [

          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2017319505500725366"
        }
      ]
    },

      "id": "2017239989080236192",
      "created_at": "2026-01-30 14:14:26+00:00",
      "text": "@DimitriosMitsos They're just getting started from my understanding. I think it's worth trying, I don't think this will work for everything, for example I wouldn't want to run a 1k tps agent",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2017232325621305823",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1151076391351439363",

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2017239549714342010",
      "created_at": "2026-01-30 14:12:41+00:00",
      "text": "Oh my god. This is soooo fast. I think this is so interesting, it changes the way you can work on a repo. I felt this with composer-1 too. https://t.co/5HUdcsbc5P",
      "likes": 149,
      "retweets": 3,
      "replies": 19,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2017239549714342010",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

    {
      "id": "2017232541258830206",
      "created_at": "2026-01-30 13:44:50+00:00",
      "text": "@KingBreton78876 Check my latest point, I feel like this is beneficial for people who want to do inline edits more than people who want to do agent stuff.",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2017199392457990479",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1977756233349152768",

      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2017232316905463971"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2017232325621305823",
      "created_at": "2026-01-30 13:43:59+00:00",
      "text": "9 seconds. \n\nThe Cerebras plan looks perfect for inline AI, for those who want to be very deeply in the loop. https://t.co/e0isgOSWfA",
      "likes": 32,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 5,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2017232325621305823",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

      "created_at": "2026-01-29 23:43:44+00:00",
      "text": "Building a local AI agent mode for desktop and mobile, full filesystem and code execution environment, deep research, planning, and conversation traces.\n\nWhat do you guys think? Is this something you'd be interested in using? I can make it do the file system thing :p https://t.co/8kiGLyXTuE",

    {
      "id": "2016992456718303689",
      "created_at": "2026-01-29 21:50:50+00:00",
      "text": "All my “oh fuck” moments with AI in order:\n\n- August 2022 Dalle mini \n- Dec 2022 ChatGPT \n- Dec 2024 Sonnet 3.6 \n- August 2025 GPT-OSS and GLM  \n- December 2025 MiniMax-M2.1 \n- January 2026 GLM-4.7-Flash &amp; Kimi-K2.5 https://t.co/x3zWI74dOE",
      "likes": 81,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 2,
      "conversation_id": "2016992456718303689",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,

      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [

          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2016771167135420622"
        }
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "2016930906737439138",
      "created_at": "2026-01-29 17:46:15+00:00",
      "text": "Today I am rebuilding my website with Kimi, scraping some data, and doing QA with AI. \n\nI also rant about what I think the biggest risk is moving forward, specifically around how to build in a post AI Economy\n\nhttps://t.co/pgET4t5XAe",
      "likes": 32,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2016930906737439138",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

      "text": "Full video\nhttps://t.co/pgET4t5XAe",
      "likes": 5,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 0,

    {
      "id": "2016870076410900793",
      "created_at": "2026-01-29 13:44:32+00:00",
      "text": "The only option is to win. \n\nIf you surround yourself with people who are complacent, who lack agency, and the ability to tell the truth you will lose so hard. \n\nRegardless of whether a bubble pops or we are in the middle of a singularity there is no choice to be weak or slow.",
      "likes": 51,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2016870076410900793",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2016829169326284820",
      "created_at": "2026-01-29 11:01:59+00:00",
      "text": "I have been using Droid's subscription mainly for readiness report, i paid 20$ and it's cost me very little to run readiness-report on 10 different repos.. \n\nVery nice and worth it https://t.co/XpcmAQtgsE",
      "likes": 77,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 4,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2016829169326284820",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2016824911151481054",
      "created_at": "2026-01-29 10:45:04+00:00",
      "text": "@kr0der It's great for data scraping, practically the only thing i use subagents for",
      "likes": 6,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2016820539248050361",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1762175022527877120",

    },

      "created_at": "2026-01-29 10:27:41+00:00",
      "text": "Note to self, don't use subagents. 20% in an hour https://t.co/00XFk1Ss1J",

    },
    {
      "id": "2016819886085878051",
      "created_at": "2026-01-29 10:25:06+00:00",
      "text": "I love open source, I need to get better at shipping high quality software.. feels so unnatural to me now that I have been doing this agent thing for 2 years. https://t.co/kwQIZ48wvA",
      "likes": 30,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2016819886085878051",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2016815762699174309",
      "created_at": "2026-01-29 10:08:43+00:00",
      "text": "@desugar_64 It's hard to calculate because Kimi's is request+tools based, and Droid's is token based.\n\nI would pay for Kimi's to get access to their UI then if u like it pick up Droid, or do the Kimi code 20$ plan and the Droid 20$",
      "likes": 5,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2016667756691673541",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1314651026000154624",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2016813629341909061"
        }
      ]
    },

      "created_at": "2026-01-29 09:56:13+00:00",
      "text": "@x86machine My brother in Christ, I have been an open source developer, contributor, donor, and teacher for my entire career. \n\nIf you can't find humor in the ridiculousness of the souls trapped asking for help in npm idk what to tell you.",

    {
      "id": "2016802326099423359",
      "created_at": "2026-01-29 09:15:19+00:00",
      "text": "@kr0der You can bring your subs into it so you literally don’t have to pay anything to get started :0 \n\nThe same codex sub will work just fine. You can also make an api out of antigravity to get free opus in it :p\n\nI would stick to use it with GLM-4.7 or Kimi-K2.5 that gets you 20M",
      "likes": 8,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2016800027667046744",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1762175022527877120",

      "referenced_tweets": [
        {

          "id": "2016801790210298127"
        }
      ]
    },
    {

      "text": "@badlogicgames Never had that happen with the https://t.co/udxwCwwXMK plan.. not a single tool call error",

      "id": "2016464147915891195",
      "created_at": "2026-01-28 10:51:31+00:00",
      "text": "I wanted to do a model tier-list, but I realized it doesn't make much sense given each has their pros and cons, and different hardware change the outcomes.\n\nI have a lot of people asking me about harnesses and tools though. I feel like I've minmaxxed all the tools and have a https://t.co/mCLkXy0NrI",
      "likes": 58,
      "retweets": 3,

    {
      "id": "2016439223885185255",
      "created_at": "2026-01-28 09:12:29+00:00",
      "text": "@ZenMagnets I’ll be honest and I mean no disrespect. \n\nI think your data is worse off in the US, we have had waaaaay more issues with data privacy than China.\n\nI think the fear of China owning data is a western propaganda issue. \n\nHowever I read yesterday that one of the companies is",
      "likes": 3,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2016161041625805003",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "61599733",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2016203593741967528"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2016438410806796302",

    {
      "id": "2016207143280508954",
      "created_at": "2026-01-27 17:50:16+00:00",
      "text": "My entire life I have always done what i enjoyed\n\nThat can serve both positively in that I have never worked a job I have not enjoyed, or learned something I thought was dumb.\n\nBut also negatively, in that there are times where what was fun did not mesh with what I needed to do. https://t.co/u5YSJdxMNW",
      "likes": 25,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2016207143280508954",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

    {
      "id": "2016162387896742037",
      "created_at": "2026-01-27 14:52:26+00:00",
      "text": "Prompt: \"Build an MC-Escher inspired website, make it unique\"",

    {
      "id": "2016161572951835102",
      "created_at": "2026-01-27 14:49:12+00:00",
      "text": "As promised: https://t.co/7a9h5doQ8g",
      "likes": 15,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2016102610772599005",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,

    {
      "id": "2016161041625805003",
      "created_at": "2026-01-27 14:47:05+00:00",
      "text": "I think I found the 1, besides a few UX issues, this model is producing EXTREMELY unique websites with very little steering or input. \n\nI think this will be my new daily driver for frontend development and computer use. Cerebras needs to help them speed their inference up https://t.co/jFehvv1bLv",
      "likes": 140,
      "retweets": 5,
      "replies": 6,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2016161041625805003",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2016159932152766797",
      "created_at": "2026-01-27 14:42:40+00:00",
      "text": "I just reviewed Kimi-K2.5, it is genuinely the most impressive open-weight model I have tried, it's great at:\n\n- Coding \n- Vision \n- Computer use\n- Web design \n- Games\n- Chatting\n- Slides\n\nI think we finally broke the AI UI design slop curse\n\nAs promised: https://t.co/7a9h5doQ8g",
      "likes": 259,
      "retweets": 17,
      "replies": 15,
      "quotes": 3,
      "conversation_id": "2016159932152766797",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2016159257532486008",
      "created_at": "2026-01-27 14:40:00+00:00",
      "text": "Here's the full review: https://t.co/7a9h5doQ8g\n\nGenuinely extremely impressive, I am having so much fun with it,",
      "likes": 47,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2016110996071354666",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2016110996071354666"
        }
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "2016112282657329646",
      "created_at": "2026-01-27 11:33:20+00:00",
      "text": "@CdeBurner I would need to be a millionaire to run this locally haha",
      "likes": 30,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 0,

    {
      "id": "2016110996071354666",
      "created_at": "2026-01-27 11:28:13+00:00",
      "text": "Kimi IS COOKING holy mackerel this is way better than anything I can get out of opus or GPT \n\nHas some bugs.. but looks soooo unique and well into my brand, for 1 shot I can't complain. https://t.co/itDodDhQk4",
      "likes": 2864,

    {
      "id": "2016106056619798831",
      "created_at": "2026-01-27 11:08:35+00:00",
      "text": "I use this to make all my thumbnails, banners for work reports, etc.. It's really really convenient. IDEK how you pay for google lol",
      "likes": 3,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2016105679367405949",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",

      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2016105679367405949",
      "created_at": "2026-01-27 11:07:06+00:00",
      "text": "Let me make your life better. \n\n1. Go to https://t.co/jFD7CBet1E\n2. Buy the 8$ a month sub\n3. Get access to Google's models in a good UI\n4. Use it to make visual artifacts\n5. Thumbnails, banners, slides, logos\n\nPeople retain information with an image more vividly and favorably https://t.co/aPlVWQ44Z0",
      "likes": 70,
      "retweets": 4,
      "replies": 8,
      "quotes": 2,
      "conversation_id": "2016105679367405949",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {

      "text": "2 videos coming today:\n\n1. Kimi-K2.5: I have been paying for their basic plan since they added Nano Banana slides and absolutely love it. This is a huge upgrade. \n\n2. Local LLM tier list: both will be an exploratory brain dump.\n\nhttps://t.co/HyOxq5HR2t https://t.co/fL7eQ1lRFV",

      "created_at": "2026-01-27 08:17:59+00:00",

    {
      "id": "2016062666016518546",
      "created_at": "2026-01-27 08:16:10+00:00",
      "text": "How do people make this kind of art it’s so captivating and dense with things to notice. https://t.co/35dNk83pfK",
      "likes": 72,
      "retweets": 1,

      "quotes": 0,

    {
      "id": "2016061641557745716",
      "created_at": "2026-01-27 08:12:06+00:00",
      "text": "@CardilloSamuel You can make it work by doing pipeline parallelism or using exllamav3 or enforcing eager but it’ll be slower and a worse experience. 4 is the sweet spot.\n\nBest to get 1 more and then 2 more later \n\n2x pro 6000s can run huge models",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2015840162811441227",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "345988272",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2015884518285041877"
        }
      ]
    },

      "text": "@CrackingDeFi I literally didn't know it was running still, it kept working and inputted thousands of rows of complex data it found on google",

    {
      "id": "2015910219298058475",
      "created_at": "2026-01-26 22:10:24+00:00",
      "text": "Ever wonder about what local models can do? \n\nHere's MiniMax-M2.1-AWQ-4bit one shotting a mario game in less than a minute. \n\nYou might say, oh no that's buggy. Yes it was written in less than 60 seconds, I would still be backspacing to remove the typo I made for the 3rd time on https://t.co/BDeIbPgTra",
      "likes": 167,
      "retweets": 11,
      "replies": 13,
      "quotes": 4,
      "conversation_id": "2015910219298058475",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

      "id": "2015876529507504584",
      "created_at": "2026-01-26 19:56:32+00:00",
      "text": "@badlogicgames I built my own but would love to learn from your code",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,

    {
      "id": "2015876432153465327",
      "created_at": "2026-01-26 19:56:09+00:00",
      "text": "GLM-4.7-Flash helped me compile all this data in a timeline. \n\nThis is my full hardware journey I rewrote and reformatted it to be in my voice but the entire thing was setup by Flash\n\nI think it dead a great job, this covers my entire experience in detail\n\nhttps://t.co/Q5FGrEeBKY",
      "likes": 35,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 5,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2015876432153465327",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2015872937664889273",

      "text": "@badlogicgames Link to the sitegeist repo?",

      "retweets": 0,

    {
      "id": "2015866321590734939",
      "created_at": "2026-01-26 19:15:58+00:00",
      "text": "@moonlght12891 It depends on what you are using it for vs what it was pruned for. \n\nGLM-Reap is very very good, I think it is a better writer than MiniMax, I like the way it uses markdown.. \n\nMiniMax-M2.1 is harder to prune right because of the architecture of the model.. I had struggled a lot",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2015727538635489373",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1975492939691294720",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [

          "id": "2015821910362173550"
        }
      ]
    },

      "id": "2015840162811441227",
      "created_at": "2026-01-26 17:32:01+00:00",
      "text": "I broke down the full local hardware landscape, all the costs, models, and things I wish I know before I started. \n\nI hope this helps you make a decision about what hardware to buy.. M3 Ultra vs Macbook vs Nvidia. etc.. and if you should buy any at all\n\nhttps://t.co/H32fkpkdw0",
      "likes": 109,
      "retweets": 14,
      "replies": 5,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2015840162811441227",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2015839443354030375",
      "created_at": "2026-01-26 17:29:10+00:00",
      "text": "Are you interested in running LLMs locally?\n\nHere is a full breakdown of all the hardware options, the costs, what models you can run, and general advice I wish I knew.\n\nI showed how I use my local models as well (: enjoy.\n\nhttps://t.co/H32fkpkdw0 https://t.co/zVyRsKwcMX",
      "likes": 531,
      "retweets": 39,
      "replies": 33,
      "quotes": 3,
      "conversation_id": "2015839443354030375",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2015817038929781047",
      "created_at": "2026-01-26 16:00:08+00:00",
      "text": "It did succeed btw. Pretty impressive mainly because it gives normies the ability to automate shit at a never before seen pace. https://t.co/EHp66Uf8Qf",
      "likes": 6,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2015814679554072997",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2015814679554072997"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2015814951852445989",
      "created_at": "2026-01-26 15:51:51+00:00",
      "text": "Down from 42k https://t.co/0pSCxBTQLD",
      "likes": 7,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2015733765977350295",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2015814679554072997",
      "created_at": "2026-01-26 15:50:46+00:00",
      "text": "MiniMax's branding is top notch.\n\nSolid agent, still needs some refinement but can't beat the price, the fact they came out with a desktop app BEFORE zai is kind of nuts to me! \n\nInteresting to see it supports Gemini too. https://t.co/4sckm5skx0 https://t.co/DB5m7YsciM",
      "likes": 191,
      "retweets": 10,
      "replies": 8,
      "quotes": 2,
      "conversation_id": "2015814679554072997",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2015787267051471228"
        }
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "2015747501135962607",
      "created_at": "2026-01-26 11:23:49+00:00",
      "text": "@TomasMann1878 I'm not haha. \n\nIt has a json file with every single data point and a prompt to use haiku to research the data points using the chrome extension. \n\ni didn't intend for it to run this long, it just kept chugging. Pure luck",
      "likes": 8,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2015729697607278924",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1929678029841932288",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2015747226744549576"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2015733765977350295",
      "created_at": "2026-01-26 10:29:14+00:00",
      "text": "GLM-4.7-Reap running my Clawdbot cleaning my email. Let's see if it destroys everything. \n\nPlaying Russian roulette here. https://t.co/lJIecRAF5a",
      "likes": 128,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 13,
      "quotes": 2,
      "conversation_id": "2015733765977350295",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2015731928381403469",
      "created_at": "2026-01-26 10:21:56+00:00",
      "text": "@creeksuen I would not recommend doing this for coding work, it doesn't maintain coherence that long, the repo just ends up being a slopfest. \n\nGood for research and data entry tho",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2015729697607278924",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2015731778300805385"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2015731778300805385",
      "created_at": "2026-01-26 10:21:21+00:00",
      "text": "@creeksuen I had it using sub-agents to research data points using the chrome:skill it was a massive dataset, and so it's been chunking like 100 at a time to haiku\n\nThe actual tasks themselves take a lot of time so it makes sense because it wasn't working itself it kept going",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2015729697607278924",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1160931518119862272",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2015730963981148523"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2015730476149813364",
      "created_at": "2026-01-26 10:16:10+00:00",
      "text": "The grind never sleeps. https://t.co/4E0piD3lGD",

    {
      "id": "2015425735230423543",
      "created_at": "2026-01-25 14:05:14+00:00",
      "text": "@minttttt____ All subagents consume tons of tokens.\n\nAs for the steering I didn’t notice NGL this is the first time I’ve checked it in a month",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2015381101221470458",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1562895728103817217",
      "is_reply": true,

          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2015425418099048597"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2015419717557661912",
      "created_at": "2026-01-25 13:41:20+00:00",

    {
      "id": "2015396620519805110",
      "created_at": "2026-01-25 12:09:33+00:00",
      "text": "Shipped a CLI tool for you/your agents to manage vllm-studio via terminal. \n\nhttps://t.co/6eM8JChOaG https://t.co/PkPuSTxb3N",

    {
      "id": "2015393541866860794",
      "created_at": "2026-01-25 11:57:19+00:00",
      "text": "@itsjustmarky I have seen of it, my gpus are pretty taken and I think CPU is pretty slow",

    {
      "id": "2015389782948315334",
      "created_at": "2026-01-25 11:42:23+00:00",
      "text": "@sudoingX I can’t confirm or deny the allegations",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2015367934193844256",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1555661341914198016",

    },
    {
      "id": "2015381726361501722",
      "created_at": "2026-01-25 11:10:22+00:00",
      "text": "@mikeshev4enko I do have the ability to benchmark a model, I haven't built any sharing into it yet but actually makes a lot of sense. \n\nIt would be cool to have a program you can opt into where you can run benchmarks with your local models and send them up. \n\nThe models size, quant, publisher,",
      "likes": 3,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2015359273241338050",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "2776949423",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2015380958896394465"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2015381101221470458",
      "created_at": "2026-01-25 11:07:53+00:00",
      "text": "If you use Codex, the latest version seems to be improved. They have steering now, which is important if their models are going to get faster. https://t.co/UxAVzAo5dN",
      "likes": 103,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 10,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2015381101221470458",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2015377987919602141",
      "created_at": "2026-01-25 10:55:30+00:00",
      "text": "@mudler_it So cool, I was running https://t.co/mDLK7Hnz3G in 2024, it is ahead of it's time, one of my favorite projects.\n\nI'm surprised no large lab is going for that.. they'll probably acquire one soon. Or use opensource",
      "likes": 9,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2015094964498166055",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "4823778449",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2015150449641369776"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2015377028678041602",
      "created_at": "2026-01-25 10:51:42+00:00",
      "text": "@mikeshev4enko I see that Clawdbot has Signal support, that's what I'm going for soon. \n\nGet it working then get it perfect.. I think Matrix is even more interesting.. but stuff like this tends to be more bare-bones vs TG, which ofc is for privacy",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2015372283376783474",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "2776949423",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2015376597315105236"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2015376710196175059",
      "created_at": "2026-01-25 10:50:26+00:00",
      "text": "@EddyLeeKhane @CardilloSamuel I use both, for stuff with my personal data I try to use local models. For ex: skills to search my X posts and data, past Anthropic/OpenAI chats. \n\nFor stuff where precision is important I use Codex, for stuff where i am managing infra (docker etc..) i use Droid + GLM via ZAI",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2011520377563070502",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1436309294769745928",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2015374495926587670"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2015374713350918317",
      "created_at": "2026-01-25 10:42:30+00:00",
      "text": "The power of Nvidia, 57 tps on GLM-4.7-Reap, 20k prefill cached, 1k+ no cache hit\n\nThese are 5 year old GPUs, power capped at 40% of their max capacity. https://t.co/2oi2z3lDJ5",
      "likes": 200,
      "retweets": 8,
      "replies": 13,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2015374713350918317",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

      "id": "2015373361900134904",

      "created_at": "2026-01-25 10:32:50+00:00",

    {
      "id": "2015360348526612699",
      "created_at": "2026-01-25 09:45:25+00:00",
      "text": "@maralcbr That's what it does. \n\nIf you have model A in memory, and you try to do a chat/completion with model B id it'll automatically switch the models for you. \n\nAlso let's you store all of the recipes and configs",
      "likes": 3,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2015359273241338050",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "31315815",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2015360039037649216"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2015359273241338050",
      "created_at": "2026-01-25 09:41:08+00:00",
      "text": "vllm-studio is getting more refined by the day. \n\nI am going to focus most my energy on this project, I believe it has a chance to really be a staple of homelabs if I can make it easy to setup. \n\nAnyone want to help me by setting it up and sending me the prompts/steps it takes to https://t.co/2pLtfrTIiT",
      "likes": 56,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 11,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2015359273241338050",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2015355453589340267",
      "created_at": "2026-01-25 09:25:58+00:00",
      "text": "@aadupuli @Davidgoggins666 You can use MacOS's virtualization to isolate it from the main machine. You can also just setup the security stuff over a few hours and you should be fine",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2015094964498166055",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "400477006",

      "id": "2015340490099486728",
      "created_at": "2026-01-25 08:26:30+00:00",
      "text": "@damianplayer The Mac Ultra will let you run full weight full context MiniMax-M2.1 and soon M2.2 at 40 tps.\n\nI already have my own Nvidia cluster but considering buying a Mac this week",
      "likes": 22,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2015198797190648102",

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2015339454123176361",
      "created_at": "2026-01-25 08:22:23+00:00",
      "text": "@mikeshev4enko 100% the main problem with the M3 ultra is the flops/bandwidth are relatively low. About 30-50% the speed of power capped 3090s",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2015186447150571997",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "2776949423",

      ]
    },

      "created_at": "2026-01-25 08:20:04+00:00",

      "text": "@KuittinenPetri 30% tax + tariffs + all the money they need to burn to do business in this cursed economy. \n\nI live in Poland if it weren’t for the tax situation I 100% would have went with Apple",

    {
      "id": "2015335178000839133",
      "created_at": "2026-01-25 08:05:24+00:00",
      "text": "@northernzones That’s true, I don’t disagree with what you’re saying but IMO realistically the privacy boat has long sailed.\n\nIf you have a phone in your hand your entire existence is already available",

        }
      ]

      "created_at": "2026-01-25 08:03:51+00:00",

    {
      "id": "2015186637362167914",
      "created_at": "2026-01-24 22:15:09+00:00",
      "text": "@jeffmurphyyy Openai &gt; Anthropic",

    {
      "id": "2015186447150571997",
      "created_at": "2026-01-24 22:14:24+00:00",
      "text": "If you're interested in running LLMs at home. \n\nHere's a full breakdown of the economics and trade-offs related with various hardware options you have. \n\nYouTube video by Monday:\nhttps://t.co/jrsBaBe0CK https://t.co/CRGgov8baP",
      "likes": 319,
      "retweets": 26,

    {
      "id": "2015170816015573052",
      "created_at": "2026-01-24 21:12:17+00:00",
      "text": "@doesdatmaksense I'm like a rat, I need my cage to change once in a while or I lose my mind. \n\nI think Pi is objectively the best harness\nFollowed my OpenCode \nFollowed by Droid\n\nI think Droid is the most intelligent model manager, they do great at long context even with smaller open-weight",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2014708063106699265",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1060588105361633280",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2015167691028647973"
        }
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "2015142137319326152",
      "created_at": "2026-01-24 19:18:19+00:00",
      "text": "Would it be better to have:\n\n1. A single clawdbot that can ssh anywhere in a home network \n2. An instance of clawdbot running on every single computer in a home network \n\nAssuming you have many computers… is there a difference hm https://t.co/rNc46E9Jyh",
      "likes": 65,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 9,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2015142137319326152",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2015094964498166055"
        }
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "2015094964498166055",
      "created_at": "2026-01-24 16:10:52+00:00",
      "text": "Let me make your life better. \n\n1. Go find an old laptop, computer, raspberry-pie laying around somewhere in your home. (Or your computer if you're brave)\n\n2. Install Clawdbot: https://t.co/MWonREGd2l\n\n3. Buy 1 or 2 of these subs.. minimax is very fast and the most Claude like, https://t.co/OglC9y0cdM",
      "likes": 2870,
      "retweets": 183,
      "replies": 76,
      "quotes": 12,
      "conversation_id": "2015094964498166055",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2015091566470160849",
      "created_at": "2026-01-24 15:57:22+00:00",
      "text": "8 bits https://t.co/IO19fFO06l",

      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2015091566470160849",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

      "created_at": "2026-01-24 15:49:00+00:00",
      "text": "WTF is going on?? Am I imaging it or is gpt-5.2-codex being much faster than usual?\n\nWatch the last 15ish seconds.. https://t.co/AuNwpTf4M6",

      "replies": 41,
      "quotes": 1,

    {
      "id": "2015083393520742493",
      "created_at": "2026-01-24 15:24:54+00:00",
      "text": "@richardanaya It's like pointing at someone who's memorized all the dates of all the battles in history then saying they are logical because they have world knowledge. \n\nI don't disagree with you that some level of world knowledge is important, but I doubt we need to train a model on 20T",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2014984231827472676",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1345251820650725376",

      "is_retweet": false,

    {
      "id": "2014987823196049568",
      "created_at": "2026-01-24 09:05:08+00:00",
      "text": "Coming this week: \n\n1. An economic breakdown of GPU/Mac for running various tiers of LLMs + guide on getting the best deal. \n2. A step by step of how to get data from  providers and rag it. \n3. A local LLM tier-list, I've tried practically all of them.\n\nhttps://t.co/HyOxq5HR2t https://t.co/tiHOSfxZS2",
      "likes": 186,
      "retweets": 14,
      "replies": 18,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2014987823196049568",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2014986762653745216",
      "created_at": "2026-01-24 09:00:55+00:00",
      "text": "I think I need another 192gb vram. \n\nMy kids will rue the day they were born, their will restricted to 30 year old dusty 3090s.",

      "retweets": 0,

      "conversation_id": "2014986762653745216",

    },

    {
      "id": "2014984231827472676",
      "created_at": "2026-01-24 08:50:52+00:00",
      "text": "GLM-4.7-Reap is still the best model I can run on 192gb VRAM, shortly followed by MiniMax, then GLM-Flash. \n\nI also use GLM-4.6V for chatgpt style chatting &amp; questions. \n\nI want what Karpathy was talking about, a tiny model trained on pure real logic, no world knowledge https://t.co/idXJjL24HE",
      "likes": 217,
      "retweets": 9,
      "replies": 14,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2014984231827472676",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

      "id": "2014980962413593051",

      "text": "Trying to switch models in the middle of an opencode/droid/pi session? \n\nCompact first, then switch the model, avoid loading 100k context without cache (:",

    {
      "id": "2014811061589864523",
      "created_at": "2026-01-23 21:22:45+00:00",
      "text": "EVEN IF it costs 5$/M in and 25$/M out for Opus 4.5 (it doesn’t), and even if it costs LESS. \n\nMy reasoning is more intellectually honest, I see myself spending 2500~ a month for 200\n\n92% discount, are u ready to pay that? \n\nThe true cost of a Token:\nhttps://t.co/qByutwRFXA https://t.co/TbBi9NMqiX",
      "likes": 90,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 5,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2014811061589864523",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2008156361440534833"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2014807936468418869",
      "created_at": "2026-01-23 21:10:20+00:00",
      "text": "Many people lack critical thinking skills, they rarely ever think hmmm what is going on? \n\nThey can’t break a question down into addressable units, they can’t research, they can’t do math, they can’t think of the big picture https://t.co/MSXE1YTKXE",
      "likes": 20,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 4,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2014807936468418869",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2014799409087701378",
      "created_at": "2026-01-23 20:36:26+00:00",
      "text": "@p_wilfried_ Ok lol",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2014762919645380728",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1819703161243709440",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2014799205550706982"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2014793724262236419",
      "created_at": "2026-01-23 20:13:51+00:00",
      "text": "@p_wilfried_ You also have not considered the fact that 365 days a year they are training models which is significantly more expensive and compute intensive, they are training on AT LEAST 10s of thousands of Nvidia GPUs constantly.\n\nOr does that not count? Do we remove \nW/E is costly",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2014762919645380728",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1819703161243709440",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2014787727741374887"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2014789868673720627",
      "created_at": "2026-01-23 19:58:32+00:00",
      "text": "@p_wilfried_ Wrong, wrong, wrong.\n\n1. I never assumed they use a dense model, 1T is a fair assessment and probably on the lower end.\n\n2. I never said 1 user needs 8 GPUs I said the weights of the model in FP8 use 8 GPUs and that 200k context requires about 1 GPU, if you have 50k users hitting",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2014762919645380728",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1819703161243709440",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2014787727741374887"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2014776450084131239",
      "created_at": "2026-01-23 19:05:13+00:00",
      "text": "@sam_knight87669 They have no where even close the amount of users as Anthropic, most open source models are 1/10th the size of Opus.\n\nThis is one place scale doesn’t make the unit price really cheaper. \n\nServicing 2B tokens for 10000 users is not the same as servicing 50k users per hour on avg",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2014626036634308742",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1963361854836613121",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2014774336209531039"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2014768499818668225",
      "created_at": "2026-01-23 18:33:37+00:00",

    {
      "id": "2014763201011834948",
      "created_at": "2026-01-23 18:12:34+00:00",
      "text": "Buy a GPU: I really didn't expect it to cost this much..\n\nhttps://t.co/1jW95qYP9p https://t.co/wP9JnqVMwU",

    {
      "id": "2014762919645380728",
      "created_at": "2026-01-23 18:11:27+00:00",
      "text": "What is the true cost of a token? \n\nHave you ever thought about that? Every time you send a dumb question, or say make no mistakes they are burning money. \n\nHow much would it cost if the subsidies ran out? \n\nToday I did a deep dive to answer this, watch:\n\nhttps://t.co/qByutwRFXA",
      "likes": 22,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 6,
      "quotes": 4,
      "conversation_id": "2014762919645380728",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2014744875254546600",
      "created_at": "2026-01-23 16:59:45+00:00",
      "text": "Here's how I make rags out of all my data with LOCAL MODELS ONLY \n\nGLM-4.7-Flash turns all my anthropic chats to a rag, and makes a skill to use it from anywhere. \n\nThis video has the whole process. excuse the annoying watermark, I wanted to speed the video up https://t.co/cJJ4uOvHPG",
      "likes": 353,
      "retweets": 21,
      "replies": 11,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2014744875254546600",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2014731055542972589",
      "created_at": "2026-01-23 16:04:50+00:00",
      "text": "Ragging all my personal data has been one of the most useful things I've ever done. \n\nEvery codebase, AI chat, invoice, email, everything. \n\n1. request a copy of your data from every provider\n2. install a 0.6B embedding model \n3. run it on cpu, point it at your downloads\n4. ask https://t.co/nd6o0LbL0t",
      "likes": 889,
      "retweets": 51,
      "replies": 32,
      "quotes": 4,
      "conversation_id": "2014731055542972589",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2014729343977599361",
      "created_at": "2026-01-23 15:58:02+00:00",
      "text": "@deeperflows Yes, it's very very good. \n\nI have a massive rig, 200gb vram 512gb ddr4 but you can run this with full context for as long as 32gb vram \n\nI only really need 80gb of vram for everything being in full precision",
      "likes": 3,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2014708063106699265",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1116610601885716480",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2014728714089542032"
        }
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "2014690303127523474",

    {

    {
      "id": "2014669022369923190",
      "created_at": "2026-01-23 11:58:20+00:00",
      "text": "If you ever wanted to be a Youtuber, this is the time. Being an \"influencer\" is going to one of the highest leverage things, more power than being a billionaire.\n\nThat might sound ridiculous, but think about.. In a world where money is no longer necessary being famous is true https://t.co/McZY8OpxSS",
      "likes": 83,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 13,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2014669022369923190",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2014649927960346639",
      "created_at": "2026-01-23 10:42:27+00:00",
      "text": "@Everlier This is the current state of the United States federal government even before AI lol \n\ni agree though, it's going to be increasingly problematic, a company i work with downsized by 70% granted they are a startup, but IMO it's mostly due to AI",

      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,

    {
      "id": "2014127874823880977",
      "created_at": "2026-01-22 00:08:00+00:00",
      "text": "6$ a month for ZAI \n10$ a month for MiniMax-M2.1\n\nSpend 1-2 hour a day:\n\n- vibe coding an idea(s) \n-  making videos vibe coding\n- building out a website for u \n- automating browser control\n- apply to gigs via Minimax automatically\n- reach out to people \n\nIf you can’t 100x your https://t.co/UYYKzwnfRR",
      "likes": 445,
      "retweets": 37,
      "replies": 13,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2014127874823880977",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2014061644834390383"
        }
      ]
    },
