      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2014115437676511370",
      "created_at": "2026-01-21 23:18:35+00:00",
      "text": "@matanSF I am building out an inferencing management system similar to lm-studio. I started it over the holidays and was just pushing slop so I can test local models.\n\nIt’s gotten so sloppy that I wasn’t able to make progress, over the last week Droid has helped me really deeply clean up",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2014039273721213256",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1353833967221313539",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2014062223056924721"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2014114842743828758",
      "created_at": "2026-01-21 23:16:13+00:00",
      "text": "@chkn_little I am using the BF16 model",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2014056143140552909",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1485333250528665602",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2014114643560554591"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2014112124683927599",
      "created_at": "2026-01-21 23:05:25+00:00",
      "text": "This is a compilation of my entire hardware journey from start to now. 4 months of posts, analytics, benchmarks.\n\nAll served to you be GLM-4.7-Flash, it’s quoting my own writing, keep that in mind.\n\nhttps://t.co/Q5FGrEeBKY",
      "likes": 12,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2014112124683927599",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2014110644451082338",
      "created_at": "2026-01-21 22:59:32+00:00",
      "text": "@zcbtrak #1 Pi\n#2 Opencode &amp; Droid \n#3 Codex",
      "likes": 4,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2014098946444976175",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1852751287185780736",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2014110229122740433"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2014110198441361491",
      "created_at": "2026-01-21 22:57:46+00:00",
      "text": "@fatihozgen85 Does it do that??!",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2014056143140552909",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "196265221",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2014108721413030308"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2014110056032165964",
      "created_at": "2026-01-21 22:57:12+00:00",
      "text": "Here you go, GLM-4.7-Flash took my x content, vector searched it, found all the details around my local hardware journey and turned it into a blog post\n\nIt then ran typecheck, build, then pushed to master, watched it deploy, then gave me the link. \n\n1. Digital assistant \n2.",
      "likes": 77,
      "retweets": 8,
      "replies": 9,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2014110056032165964",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2014108398262899181",
      "created_at": "2026-01-21 22:50:37+00:00",
      "text": "@luisrudge No I use vllm-studio, which i made.",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2013920636427448758",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "56385199",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2014107780790038937"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2014108341530669236",
      "created_at": "2026-01-21 22:50:23+00:00",
      "text": "@Wald52Wald skill issue",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2014056143140552909",

    {
      "id": "2014107749680894398",
      "created_at": "2026-01-21 22:48:02+00:00",
      "text": "Every CLI tool should have interactive shells.. this is crazy, you can run long projects like this... \n\nMaybe I'm over-complicating it https://t.co/mXOz4VF9PU",
      "likes": 21,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2014107749680894398",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2014105149916983524",
      "created_at": "2026-01-21 22:37:42+00:00",
      "text": "GLM-4.7-Flash using vector search against my X history, tweets, and writing to create a full blog post on my local hardware journey. \n\nLet's see what it cooks up https://t.co/1Af9N81x9N",
      "likes": 61,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 6,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2014105149916983524",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2014098946444976175",
      "created_at": "2026-01-21 22:13:03+00:00",
      "text": "I wanted /readiness-report in Pi. \n\nFactory was nice enough to publish this Droid feature setup in their docs. I wanted to get it into other harness because it's so good. \n\nIf you haven't tried Droid yet, try their free trial and run /readiness-report, it's really worth it. https://t.co/eExYDll9u0",
      "likes": 36,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 4,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2014098946444976175",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2014096346236452964",
      "created_at": "2026-01-21 22:02:43+00:00",
      "text": "@JemeryWiz - n8n\n- optimize speed\n- data analysis \n- drafting \n- copying opus runs",
      "likes": 10,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2014056143140552909",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1250288735414972417",

        }
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "2014056752560341181",
      "created_at": "2026-01-21 19:25:23+00:00",
      "text": "What can GLM-4.7-Flash do?\n\nhttps://t.co/y4zUSBzteh",
      "likes": 18,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 2,
      "conversation_id": "2014056752560341181",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,

      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2014056143140552909",
      "created_at": "2026-01-21 19:22:58+00:00",
      "text": "I just had to make a new video of GLM-4.7-Flash \n\n- Helping me refactor VLLM studio \n- Did a data analytics report for work \n- Managed to search my tweets \n- Made me a fully playable Pacman in 1 shot\n- Great at browser use\n\nThis model is too good to be this small, the full thing https://t.co/EyRmsb7pWu",
      "likes": 328,
      "retweets": 26,
      "replies": 30,
      "quotes": 4,
      "conversation_id": "2014056143140552909",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2014050789073756632",
      "created_at": "2026-01-21 19:01:42+00:00",
      "text": "@oorusr 8x RTX 3090s \n\n50-80 tps gen",
      "likes": 6,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2013911569428586656",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1695155152183787520",

    {
      "id": "2013927406793372146",

    {
      "id": "2013926724220711020",
      "created_at": "2026-01-21 10:48:42+00:00",
      "text": "I have these scripts I use for data entry at my work, I typically use Opus and Sonnet for all of it since they're the best at this kind of thing. \n\nToday I had Opus do a data entry run and document the steps in a file https://t.co/4XFXMw3N8X then spun up Pi with GLM-4.7-Flash and https://t.co/M5IX3biywL",
      "likes": 82,
      "retweets": 3,
      "replies": 9,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2013926724220711020",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2013925956247863497",
      "created_at": "2026-01-21 10:45:39+00:00",
      "text": "@FaisalFailed Pi is very good I would try it, it's basically free BYOK or subscription only. It's very customizable, and super simple to use. \n\nI love switching between Pi, Opencode, and Droid. I haven't really used CC much.",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2013920636427448758",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1379041029656092681",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2013925131177885991"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2013925614487560645",
      "created_at": "2026-01-21 10:44:18+00:00",
      "text": "@Nigga_Nuke @reneil1337 lol ok",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2013911569428586656",

    {
      "id": "2013922616164196592",
      "created_at": "2026-01-21 10:32:23+00:00",
      "text": "@reneil1337 Yes! https://t.co/6eM8JChgl8 the main branch is stable, you need to setup locally with docker, give it to an agent they can figure it out. \n\nI am working on a refactor to get it working with less of a hassle.",
      "likes": 22,
      "retweets": 3,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2013911569428586656",

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2013922416607612959",
      "created_at": "2026-01-21 10:31:35+00:00",
      "text": "@victormustar Opencode has an opensource alternative that is even more feature rich, but it's a bit young in it's dev cycle.",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2013920636427448758",

    {
      "id": "2013922318486016049",
      "created_at": "2026-01-21 10:31:12+00:00",
      "text": "@victormustar The model or the Desktop app? \n\nIf the latter no, but it reads from ~/.factory/config.json \nWhich you can BYOK to.",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2013920636427448758",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "784830007",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2013921959289954684"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2013920636427448758",
      "created_at": "2026-01-21 10:24:31+00:00",
      "text": "GLM-4.7-Flash running locally smoothly navigating Factory Droid's Desktop app\n\nI love Factory's branding.. It's very visually appealing. The paper dark background, orange highlights, and beautiful line art is very pleasant to look at\n\nPremium feel and let's me agent outside IDEs https://t.co/HtYchbZT0f",
      "likes": 83,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 8,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2013920636427448758",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2013911569428586656",
      "created_at": "2026-01-21 09:48:29+00:00",
      "text": "Man, what a model. I have not seen any mode below 200B act like this. It's really doing a good job in a pretty novel environment. \n\nThe ZAI team is doing a great job I'm very happy they still open source all this. It's very impressive. \nhttps://t.co/BB6A0ezLGY https://t.co/kDj5KzWuFY",
      "likes": 415,
      "retweets": 26,
      "replies": 13,
      "quotes": 3,
      "conversation_id": "2013911569428586656",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2013908229168783584",
      "created_at": "2026-01-21 09:35:13+00:00",
      "text": "@wojtess This is Pi. \n\nhttps://t.co/WOj0oBfQvW",
      "likes": 8,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2013705925836546362",

    {
      "id": "2013900864604291362",
      "created_at": "2026-01-21 09:05:57+00:00",
      "text": "If you’re interest in running LLMs at home you should join the x/localLlama community\n\nIt’s pinned on my profile, I’ve been down many rabbit holes and gotten some really good technical and economic advice from the group. https://t.co/qNBfUN2SAT",
      "likes": 118,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2013900864604291362",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2013881920099062163"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2013897384418259313",
      "created_at": "2026-01-21 08:52:07+00:00",
      "text": "13.5 hour training run completed in 1 prompt by Opus using the https://t.co/XD5d1FcXgS \n\nI did an SFT run w qLoRA based on 2 years of my AI coding, AI chats, tool calls, etc I extracted from Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, OC, etc.. \n\nNo Ralph loop, no complex orchestration. It ran https://t.co/3tZNFURPar",
      "likes": 169,
      "retweets": 13,
      "replies": 15,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2013897384418259313",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

    {
      "id": "2013892531495354774",
      "created_at": "2026-01-21 08:32:50+00:00",
      "text": "@noelhatem in 4bit with 200k context (fp8 kvcache) it should. But you need to implement the fix (add the model id into the mla list in vllm)",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2013667963585400953",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "57032020",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2013891022233969010"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2013886627689398626",
      "created_at": "2026-01-21 08:09:22+00:00",
      "text": "@r0b0t_sp1der It’s been fixed! \n\nKv cache now requires 15x less memory, fix was found by a community member @ZenMagnets \n\nThe whole this in BF16 with full context should fit in 96gb VRAM. For the q4 it should all fit in 32gb",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2013705925836546362",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "223042950",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2013885934698405993"
        }
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "2013707230961356990",
      "created_at": "2026-01-20 20:16:31+00:00",
      "text": "DUDE it found 21/30 of the projects I gave it to sample.. this is very good. https://t.co/LweVWl0Sos",
      "likes": 16,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2013705925836546362",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2013705925836546362"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2013705925836546362",
      "created_at": "2026-01-20 20:11:20+00:00",
      "text": "I am blown away by how good this tiny guy is. \nIt's handling the environment perfectly. \n\nIt's been exploring, not spamming files, editing the same one, testing, validating against what it knows and solo debugging. \n\n30B parameters, the main issue is context but IMO this feels https://t.co/eg4KF3mWcA",
      "likes": 266,
      "retweets": 15,
      "replies": 20,
      "quotes": 4,
      "conversation_id": "2013705925836546362",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2013703141506658700",
      "created_at": "2026-01-20 20:00:16+00:00",
      "text": "@Everlier Oh yeah I was really surprised when I saw how it handled the request… I only see that behaviour in GPT models (reading and taking your time lol)",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2013667963585400953",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "133797694",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2013676256672317936"
        }
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "2013700010672623942",
      "created_at": "2026-01-20 19:47:49+00:00",
      "text": "Training a LoRA then doing RL for GLM-4.7-Flash using my ai chats\n\nWill do some tests over the next few days, I need to figure figure out how to benchmark this method... \n\nWould people be interested in a custom trained model based on your codebases, ai chat history, etc...\n\nIs https://t.co/dqljOCM2cI",
      "likes": 59,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 4,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2013700010672623942",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2013683549623201925",
      "created_at": "2026-01-20 18:42:25+00:00",
      "text": "V0.1.1 - PI ACP \n\n- added better tool call formatting \n- added session context display\n- added /sessions and /load session for history\n- added a script you can run  to set the whole thing up in Zed \n- added thinking \n\nhttps://t.co/AVEPTNxMus https://t.co/A6CXIBoDzj",
      "likes": 22,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2013683549623201925",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2013682464984605138",
      "created_at": "2026-01-20 18:38:06+00:00",
      "text": "@CdeBurner https://t.co/AVEPTNxMus give this to a model and have it figure it out",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2013679072203727228",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1583774031077163009",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",

      "created_at": "2026-01-20 17:40:29+00:00",
      "text": "Model: https://t.co/BB6A0ezLGY",
      "likes": 7,
      "retweets": 0,

    {
      "id": "2013667963585400953",
      "created_at": "2026-01-20 17:40:29+00:00",
      "text": "I ran GLM-4.7-Flash locally to test if it's as good as they say it is. It's tiny, but the KVCache requires 180gb if fp16\n\nI ran it in Zed via the Pi ACP and it worked like a charm, it identified the exact files and issues I was reporting. It's a very nice model. \n\nI will release",
      "likes": 150,
      "retweets": 5,
      "replies": 11,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2013667963585400953",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2013667408616018228",
      "created_at": "2026-01-20 17:38:17+00:00",
      "text": "It's good guys. https://t.co/9x8jdzVtRY",
      "likes": 171,
      "retweets": 3,
      "replies": 14,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2013667408616018228",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {

    {
      "id": "2013633349365989535",
      "created_at": "2026-01-20 15:22:56+00:00",
      "text": "I added sessions to my Zed PI ACP. Now I can switch between past sessions within the zed agent panel. \n\nWas not easy.. https://t.co/qGvF2cqM1s",

    {
      "id": "2013592902606213272",
      "created_at": "2026-01-20 12:42:13+00:00",
      "text": "@radres What are you on about",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2013578853612961851",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1650086367844524032",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2013592455719907461"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2013578853612961851",
      "created_at": "2026-01-20 11:46:23+00:00",
      "text": "Let me make your life a little bit better. \n\nIs your email constantly clogged with spam? Are you sick of fixing it only for it to devolve a week later? Have you tried to use Gemini only for it to fail?\n\n1. Go install a fresh chromium browser (or use your own if you're not https://t.co/7GEetuy9PN",
      "likes": 43,
      "retweets": 3,
      "replies": 4,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2013578853612961851",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2013565992211808729",
      "created_at": "2026-01-20 10:55:17+00:00",
      "text": "Pi ACP building Pi ACP https://t.co/zXpMxX8WV3",
      "likes": 11,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2013565992211808729",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2013551804022206475",
      "created_at": "2026-01-20 09:58:54+00:00",
      "text": "@kr0der You have to get this in:\n\n\"agent_servers\": {\n       \"pi-rpc-acp\": {\n       \"type\": \"custom\",\n       \"command\": \"~/your/path/here/pi-rpc-acp-adapter/node_modules/.bin/tsx\",\n       \"args\": [\n         \"~/your/path/here/pi-rpc-acp-adapter/src/index.ts\",\n],\n       \"env\": {},\n     }, https://t.co/iIIv6dXnlX",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2013344429919142268",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1762175022527877120",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2013547534937948570"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2013540612054696324",
      "created_at": "2026-01-20 09:14:26+00:00",
      "text": "@carsonfarmer @badlogicgames Is your repo open source? Maybe your code is cleaner or you have more integrations and we can merge this.\n\nMy repo is like 2.6k lines of code but implements almost all the / commands.. but it’s a bit messy in some places",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2013365516350419113",

        }
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "2013344429919142268",
      "created_at": "2026-01-19 20:14:52+00:00",
      "text": "I added ACP support to Pi, enjoy. \n\nhttps://t.co/AVEPTNxMus https://t.co/e7ugdZWqUx",
      "likes": 37,
      "retweets": 3,
      "replies": 5,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2013344429919142268",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2013332829149544477",
      "created_at": "2026-01-19 19:28:47+00:00",
      "text": "I used my x agent to build me a nice UI for my journey quantizing glm-4.7 this was all compiled by GLM-4.7 with my twitter-data skill I made. \n\nVery fun, build narratives and websites out of anything.\nhttps://t.co/y35hTjgW8l https://t.co/QH4lUexndZ",
      "likes": 105,
      "retweets": 3,
      "replies": 4,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2013332829149544477",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2013328087719575742",
      "created_at": "2026-01-19 19:09:56+00:00",
      "text": "steipete was right, we are over-complicating it.",
      "likes": 59,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 5,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2013328087719575742",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2013304672409289089",
      "created_at": "2026-01-19 17:36:53+00:00",
      "text": "I was interviewed by TechFrenAJ, here's the video. \n\nI go into why I built this machine, the economics behind it, and ramble on about AI for an hour lol. \n\nhttps://t.co/DBIfJRoiE8 https://t.co/Q9rPCiodVM",
      "likes": 75,
      "retweets": 3,
      "replies": 7,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2013304672409289089",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2013303019702497713",
      "created_at": "2026-01-19 17:30:19+00:00",
      "text": "@krafter36 I agree, I don't regret building. I regret being dishonest with myself about why I am doing it",
      "likes": 5,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2013251816805339643",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1497244920616296458",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2013302779641553172"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2013295744984203295",
      "created_at": "2026-01-19 17:01:25+00:00",
      "text": "RT @pleometric: why do i lowkey agree with spongebob here 🙏😭😭 https://t.co/hgsNLmL90G",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 2152,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2013295744984203295",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": true,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "retweeted",
          "id": "2013262315953467606"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2013251816805339643",
      "created_at": "2026-01-19 14:06:52+00:00",
      "text": "I think I've had agent psychosis :P it's been so fun building I question why I do it. \n\nI think I've enjoyed building stuff, and have loved being able to make anything I want work.. but a lot of it is either:\n\n1. Not useful \n2. Not right\n3. Not unique\n\nI will be trimming off a",
      "likes": 38,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 5,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2013251816805339643",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2013244669342208278",
      "created_at": "2026-01-19 13:38:28+00:00",
      "text": "I interviewed Mario, the dev behind the Pi agent coding kit. He's very knowledgeable on agents/ai etc.. I really enjoyed this conversation.\n\n- https://t.co/YD37ozyjgA\n- https://t.co/WOj0oBfQvW https://t.co/GBY7TEwidV",
      "likes": 141,
      "retweets": 10,
      "replies": 9,
      "quotes": 2,
      "conversation_id": "2013244669342208278",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2013231749338165313",
      "created_at": "2026-01-19 12:47:07+00:00",
      "text": "@ZenMagnets Only one of many things I am doing at all times.",
      "likes": 4,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2013228479869800630",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "61599733",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2013228888420430236"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2013228479869800630",
      "created_at": "2026-01-19 12:34:08+00:00",
      "text": "Ah, the last days of pregnancy, being ping ponged to and from the doctor while juggling work and being a dad. \n\nI feel for my wife though, it must be very difficult.",
      "likes": 31,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 6,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2013228479869800630",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

    {
      "id": "2012982222018449731",
      "created_at": "2026-01-18 20:15:35+00:00",
      "text": "@androolloyd 8x 3090s",
      "likes": 6,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,

    {
      "id": "2012912540813000986",
      "created_at": "2026-01-18 15:38:42+00:00",
      "text": "GLM-4.7 running locally plans a refactor. \n\nRunning with full context on 192GB of VRAM \nI gave it 6 pages written by Slopus, configs everywhere, same types redefined in 5 places.. etc.. \n\nGood plan, GLM was always a good communicator, I am happy it's lucid despite between 13% the https://t.co/XgQCCwaHL6",
      "likes": 342,
      "retweets": 20,
      "replies": 27,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2012912540813000986",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2012900871210746045",
      "created_at": "2026-01-18 14:52:20+00:00",
      "text": "@DimitriosMitsos Doesn't really matter to me, if it works.",
      "likes": 4,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2012889904611553491",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1151076391351439363",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,

    {
      "id": "2012889904611553491",
      "created_at": "2026-01-18 14:08:45+00:00",
      "text": "Let me make your life better. \n\n1. Go to https://t.co/NqBPY29Ali \n2. Select download an archive of your data \n3. Unzip then install all-MiniLM-L6-v2 \n4. Run the model on CPU and have it create vectors\n5. Tell your preferred model to create a skill out of it \n6. Ask the model https://t.co/oJTD0Vm9RO",
      "likes": 571,
      "retweets": 38,
      "replies": 17,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2012889904611553491",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2012887140980449730",
      "created_at": "2026-01-18 13:57:46+00:00",
      "text": "One thing I really enjoyed today was using:\n\n/readiness-report in Droid\n\nThis is actually a genius idea, it basically triggers a prompt to sonnet-4.5 and has it check a list of requirements for a production ready system. \n\nIt then gets back to you with a checklist of like 100 https://t.co/Yp9QdrlDC0",
      "likes": 114,
      "retweets": 6,
      "replies": 9,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2012887140980449730",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2012868295397884326",
      "created_at": "2026-01-18 12:42:53+00:00",
      "text": "@sage_rabi Multi-tasking, and just using the LLM agent as a proxy to doing anything else. (organizing your email, etc..)",
      "likes": 3,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2012794939587870889",

      "text": "@Everlier It can do both, I feel it leans closer to a Codex than a CC. \n\nBut it’s also very flexible I’m sure you can use it anyway you want. They don’t have the message queue feature. \n\nThat’s my only complaint",

    {
      "id": "2012817678222663726",
      "created_at": "2026-01-18 09:21:45+00:00",
      "text": "@handikawisnu you need to use the right \"provider\": \"generic-chat-completion-api\",",
      "likes": 1,

    {
      "id": "2012817473473593798",
      "created_at": "2026-01-18 09:20:56+00:00",
      "text": "On jail-breaking LLMs. \n\nPractically every LLM is tuned for helpfulness. It uses all the context within a chat to figure out how best to help you.\n\nUnless the chat is empty, and the wording contradicts it's system prompt. \n\nSo, now let's take a model and figure out what we are https://t.co/yh8kwizfxr",
      "likes": 85,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 8,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2012817473473593798",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2012811805853151245",
      "created_at": "2026-01-18 08:58:25+00:00",

    {
      "id": "2012809403959853329",
      "created_at": "2026-01-18 08:48:52+00:00",
      "text": "@hyperspecies @matanSF Agent = Setup and run tests, deploy to Vercel, commit to GitHub, check my telegram, do some data analysis and make me a PDF, fix my GPU fan speed, etc..\n\nCoding = Research, Plan, and Implement purely code changes\n\nGPT is not a good agent, Claude is a good agent",
      "likes": 4,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2012785578194968835",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1943939868951805952",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2012795150884569231"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2012808858435981722",
      "created_at": "2026-01-18 08:46:42+00:00",
      "text": "@matanSF I spend about 600$ a month:\n\n- Claude max = 200$\n- GPT Pro = 200$ \n- ZAI = 6$\n- Minimax = 10$ \n- Factory = 20$ \n- T3Chat = 8$ \n- Openrouter = 50$ \n+ Tax for all that.\n\nI also bought 8x 3090s and run about 10-20M tokens a day through it. \n\nI don’t think demand will go down https://t.co/Va0OSteGVw",
      "likes": 87,
      "retweets": 3,
      "replies": 14,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2012724848418492914",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1353833967221313539",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2012724848418492914"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2012803390783291597",
      "created_at": "2026-01-18 08:24:59+00:00",
      "text": "@fangwangme Yeah it’s really annoying",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,

    {
      "id": "2012794939587870889",
      "created_at": "2026-01-18 07:51:24+00:00",
      "text": "I was always of the belief that Factory's subscription was incredibly expensive. Specifically because I have been conditioned to getting 3 billion tokens a month from my Claude Max 20x sub\n\nYesterday I paid for the 20$ Factory subscription to get access to their apps, I will buy https://t.co/FdsKQ48cx5 https://t.co/1pYLwVJVOe",
      "likes": 267,
      "retweets": 12,
      "replies": 37,
      "quotes": 4,
      "conversation_id": "2012794939587870889",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2012648834652922034"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2012790195418972226",
      "created_at": "2026-01-18 07:32:33+00:00",
      "text": "@matanSF Each session is something like 2-8 hours continuously switching between GPT-5.2-Codex for coding and GLM for agent tasks. \n\nsomething like 2-25 million tokens per chat.,",
      "likes": 5,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2012785578194968835",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1353833967221313539",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2012787549765284275"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2012785578194968835",
      "created_at": "2026-01-18 07:14:12+00:00",
      "text": "My average session length in Droid is higher than any other harness I've tried besides Pi. \n\nI am a big fan of this.. will be making a video on it and yapping about it non-stop for a month now. https://t.co/CJqovBn1Ta",
      "likes": 62,
      "retweets": 3,
      "replies": 11,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2012785578194968835",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2012785167929098531",
      "created_at": "2026-01-18 07:12:34+00:00",
      "text": "@memoria_2024 Not even a little bit, their pricing is API based but you can BYOK I paid for the subscription to access their desktop app, website, and other integrations. \n\nIt's a very good harness, specifically it does much better at context management and permissions than any other harness",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2012640605269041561",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1738636938616115200",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2012782838769140159"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2012782673182298199",
      "created_at": "2026-01-18 07:02:39+00:00",
      "text": "@dreamworks2050 lit, I really enjoy this tool.",
      "likes": 4,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2012640605269041561",

    },
    {
      "id": "2012643253623910423",
      "created_at": "2026-01-17 21:48:39+00:00",

    {
      "id": "2012640605269041561",
      "created_at": "2026-01-17 21:38:08+00:00",
      "text": "Finally got around to paying for a subscription, Factory Droid time. I will give it a real shot for a month, I wonder if BYOK will eat into my token usage. \n\nIf not I will use Droid instead of Claude Max next month for Opus, and use my own keys for Codex, etc.. Looks beautiful I https://t.co/FsN2J8yuTQ",
      "likes": 89,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 17,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2012640605269041561",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

    {
      "id": "2012628781354086450",
      "created_at": "2026-01-17 20:51:08+00:00",
      "text": "RT @tszzl: Claude runs an indoor shrimp farm in the sub-basement of Anthropic Headquarters in the financial district of San Francisco. Clau…",

      "retweets": 232,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2012628781354086450",

    {
      "id": "2012619587532001627",
      "created_at": "2026-01-17 20:14:36+00:00",
      "text": "I’m rethinking fully autonomous orchestration, the more I’ve spent time with it the less it makes sense to try and get the models managing each other *autonomously* \n\nThe drift in understanding of the work it’s suppose to do is too big.\n\nMight have to start another rabbit hole https://t.co/Cy8bVQEqca",
      "likes": 60,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 7,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2012619587532001627",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2012595202129534990",
      "created_at": "2026-01-17 18:37:43+00:00",
      "text": "@AxcanNathan I switch between them very often. They all have their use-cases.",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2012203372116332666",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1563793183829622785",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2012583020323049762"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2012574851689492741",
      "created_at": "2026-01-17 17:16:51+00:00",
      "text": "Today is Droid in Zed day.\n\nThis IDE is so much more calm, and clean. I really love it.. i wish I didn't love multi-tasking so much.. \n\nI also have really enjoyed Droid with GPT-5.2-Codex,  great launch for them. https://t.co/eGTp2g7Tnl",
      "likes": 99,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 7,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2012574851689492741",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

      "created_at": "2026-01-17 15:45:01+00:00",
      "text": "I love the OpenAI side-panel for tool calls and thinking blocks. I have been endlessly optimizing this experience need to do the next release. \n\nI still need an actual dev to help me make this consumer ready, can't be expecting people to run docker to get this setup. https://t.co/1zw0WYjV2h",

      "retweets": 1,

    {

    {
      "id": "2012222224984355029",
      "created_at": "2026-01-16 17:55:38+00:00",
      "text": "I am paying for T3Chat again cause i can't figure out how to use the Google AI sites lol.. \n\nGrown a lot since i last tried it, very beautiful https://t.co/LwPebC0wK1",
      "likes": 7,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2012222224984355029",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2012203471865331887",
      "created_at": "2026-01-16 16:41:07+00:00",
      "text": "Here's my full review, a bit rambly. \n\nhttps://t.co/KDLR3vmA79",
      "likes": 5,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2012145381841223745",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2012203372116332666"
        },
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2012145381841223745"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2012203372116332666",
      "created_at": "2026-01-16 16:40:43+00:00",
      "text": "I have been yapping about OpenCode for over a month now, I really love using this thing. \n\nI break down why I love and recommend trying opencode, you literally can't lose anything they are giving away GLM-4.7 and MiniMax-M2.1 for free right now.\n\nEnjoy\n\nhttps://t.co/Flamr73U02",
      "likes": 85,
      "retweets": 4,
      "replies": 4,
      "quotes": 2,
      "conversation_id": "2012203372116332666",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2012200990917734497",
      "created_at": "2026-01-16 16:31:15+00:00",
      "text": "My lil side project is looking good I think. https://t.co/qM6vHDmGgf",
      "likes": 35,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 4,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2012200990917734497",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2012200047216148974",
      "created_at": "2026-01-16 16:27:30+00:00",
      "text": "I took all my AI chats, every message with every provider and app, including Cursor, CC, etc.. then ragged it. \n\nAfterwards I trained a LoRA for NousCoder-14B based on my preferences.. I am excited to see how this turned out.. \nhttps://t.co/PzWhLZ8bir \n\nhttps://t.co/c9fhW0vWaG https://t.co/7eAgmHPpo9",
      "likes": 159,
      "retweets": 3,
      "replies": 19,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2012200047216148974",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2012165566526611501",
      "created_at": "2026-01-16 14:10:29+00:00",
      "text": "@mikeshev4enko Playwright, browser extensions, Cursor's &amp; RooCode's IDE extensions",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2012155763750564196",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "2776949423",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2012164607024992597"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2012145381841223745",
      "created_at": "2026-01-16 12:50:17+00:00",
      "text": "The OpenCode Desktop app is helping me with a problem which is making my life more and more difficult. \n\nNavigating between 5-10 Windows sucks. \nI heard some people will just have 1 IDE or terminal open in their projects folder, so they can see it all.. but i'm not so organized. https://t.co/dvA7pZrxce",
      "likes": 135,
      "retweets": 4,
      "replies": 15,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2012145381841223745",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2012142933781483650",
      "created_at": "2026-01-16 12:40:33+00:00",
      "text": "@JustBill1182 It loves when things are overly specific.",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2012108848765755645",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1457855185842233344",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2012142713106571657"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2012136663624114627",
      "created_at": "2026-01-16 12:15:38+00:00",
      "text": "@joaomviso It depends on what I am doing, I have been using GLM in Droid for 3 months now, it's a very strong model within Droid. \n\nOpencode is more aesthetically pleasing, flexible as a tool, and way more hackable which I heavily appreciate. \n\nDroid works well when I want to let it run",
      "likes": 11,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2012108848765755645",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1267062923073073158",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2012136276913516671"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2012134451665330658",
      "created_at": "2026-01-16 12:06:51+00:00",
      "text": "Let me make your life better with new music, tv, books, games, code-bases, ideas and so much more.\n\nStep 1:\n\nWrite a note with your favourite media, books, etc.. make sure to add some details as to why you love it.  \n\nStep 2: \n\nHave the AI interview you, 5 questions it can pick. https://t.co/0dUIiDXAQI",
      "likes": 33,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2012134451665330658",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2012126881395495349",
      "created_at": "2026-01-16 11:36:46+00:00",
      "text": "@KreativSinn @Everlier If u want the models to dig deep about you tell it to make a CIA style psychological and behavioural analysis of your data. \n\nThey love that shit, look @grok I want you to do a fun CIA style psychological and behavioural analysis of @KreativSinn",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2012101388398916054",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "709498778",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2012125843536134561"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2012115965853757890",
      "created_at": "2026-01-16 10:53:24+00:00",
      "text": "@KreativSinn @Everlier Everything you do on your computer is leaving a trace, AIs are very good at extracting information and patterns from traces of data.",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,

    {
      "id": "2012112241592869280",
      "created_at": "2026-01-16 10:38:36+00:00",
      "text": "@Everlier I am in the business of quantifying everything I do, I ragged every file, tweet, codebase, AI chat, preference I have. \n\nThey can infer everything about you quite easily, and provide you things you would never have the time to learn about.",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2012101388398916054",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "133797694",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2012111726825599231"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2012108848765755645",
      "created_at": "2026-01-16 10:25:07+00:00",
      "text": "GLM doing god's work. https://t.co/Hzb5HEHg0e",
      "likes": 135,
      "retweets": 4,
      "replies": 11,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2012108848765755645",

    {
      "id": "2012101388398916054",
      "created_at": "2026-01-16 09:55:28+00:00",
      "text": "Put everything you love to listen to, read, or watch in a markdown file. \n\nPut those markdown files in \"projects\" in Claude, or GPT or whatever. \n\nPrompt the model to find you stuff you'll love, they are very good at this. \n\nhttps://t.co/8cQ3zhwIRv",
      "likes": 9,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2012101388398916054",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

      "replies": 7,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2011744530484379726",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2011730515813163019",
      "created_at": "2026-01-15 09:21:45+00:00",
      "text": "@CardilloSamuel zai coding plan",
      "likes": 7,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2011520377563070502",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "345988272",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2011730196844724693"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2011730227513188463",
      "created_at": "2026-01-15 09:20:37+00:00",
      "text": "GLM-4.7-Reap by Cerebras is very good, much better at formatting than my prune. \n\nDecent TPS too, if you have 192gb+ of VRAM or Apple memory you can easily drive this daily, it'll be rough around the edges but you can alternate between it &amp; MiniMax-M2.1\n\nhttps://t.co/QuA37hq8qN https://t.co/TaevYWtB8v",
      "likes": 117,
      "retweets": 4,
      "replies": 7,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2011730227513188463",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2011726468733424039",
      "created_at": "2026-01-15 09:05:40+00:00",
      "text": "We should sponsor open source more. Not much but this guy's code has been very useful to me. https://t.co/vOl86b45tO",
      "likes": 48,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2011726468733424039",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2011725160139932118",
      "created_at": "2026-01-15 09:00:28+00:00",
      "text": "@AjSkassa Yes, you get what you wait for.",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2011548741451596048",

    },
    {
      "id": "2011722961238311153",
      "created_at": "2026-01-15 08:51:44+00:00",
      "text": "@SenditHighor GLM is much less sloppy than Opus, Opus is just a better ux",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,

      "conversation_id": "2011424154525024543",

    {
      "id": "2011713936685744206",
      "created_at": "2026-01-15 08:15:53+00:00",
      "text": "@mikaackermann9 115gb for the weights and like 70+ for the context",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2011523763893125317",

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2011565504436322575",
      "created_at": "2026-01-14 22:26:03+00:00",
      "text": "The only thing left is figuring out how to make a bearable and fun to talk to model.\n\nGPT is better at everything but the personality is too dry to be an assistant (vs a coding agent) https://t.co/wSmnydIAtq",
      "likes": 14,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 5,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2011565504436322575",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2011531740804964855"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2011552854272602369",
      "created_at": "2026-01-14 21:35:47+00:00",
      "text": "@polymorph3us I really don't think Opus is a good programmer, I feel this is better at pure coding. Opus is just easy to use and steer",

    {
      "id": "2011523763893125317",
      "created_at": "2026-01-14 19:40:12+00:00",
      "text": "https://t.co/lpShujVnwK\n\n I quantized GLM-4.7-Reap by Cerebras, enjoy probably better than mine.",
      "likes": 168,
      "retweets": 12,
      "replies": 8,
      "quotes": 2,
      "conversation_id": "2011523763893125317",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,

    },
    {
      "id": "2011520377563070502",
      "created_at": "2026-01-14 19:26:44+00:00",
      "text": "GLM-4.7 + Droid is god tier, both locally running and via the plan. Droid is doing something special with context management. \n\nI can run conversations for hours without a single care in the world. 35m tokens in a day just in Droid. https://t.co/9LtpgAGdLe",
      "likes": 784,
      "retweets": 40,
      "replies": 47,
      "quotes": 4,
      "conversation_id": "2011520377563070502",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2011518459889532965",
      "created_at": "2026-01-14 19:19:07+00:00",
      "text": "@Bashir784105 It’s got to be configured perfectly otherwise it won’t work",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,

      "conversation_id": "2011424154525024543",

        }
      ]

    {
      "id": "2011461466247799229",
      "created_at": "2026-01-14 15:32:39+00:00",
      "text": "I hate vscode. \n\nWhat is the solution here? https://t.co/p4gv9pwtmk",
      "likes": 12,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 9,
      "quotes": 0,

    },
    {
      "id": "2011456992422854875",
      "created_at": "2026-01-14 15:14:52+00:00",
      "text": "I have severely underestimated GPT-5.2-Codex \n\nMan this session has been going all day, once it gets it's context managed correctly it does very little additional reading. \n\nI have been using it to clean up after Slopus. Being able to do queue + steering is a huge power-up for https://t.co/yUFQXhggku",
      "likes": 438,
      "retweets": 14,
      "replies": 40,
      "quotes": 9,
      "conversation_id": "2011456992422854875",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {

    {
      "id": "2011424613147009509",
      "created_at": "2026-01-14 13:06:12+00:00",
      "text": "It’s really snowing https://t.co/HPOauJqyDy",
      "likes": 3,
      "retweets": 0,

      "id": "2011180536211869767",
      "created_at": "2026-01-13 20:56:20+00:00",
      "text": "@thdxr These experiments will eventually culminate in a very strong work automation system that can be optimized later.\n\nAlso opencode specifically allows for multi-provider multi-model. \n\nA lot of tasks (reading, running basic commands, research, docs, reviews) can be offloaded to",
      "likes": 10,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2011078733621293273",

      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [

          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2011078733621293273"
        }
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "2011166389894332863",
      "created_at": "2026-01-13 20:00:07+00:00",
      "text": "Hey guys.\n\nIf any of you have 256gb Mac Studio or Nvidia if you can help me quantize these to GGUF, MLX and AWQ\n\nTo the best of my ability I fixed the weights, these were the only 2 that are salvageable. But, they’re good.\n\nBlog and video tomorrow about why the other one was",
      "likes": 53,
      "retweets": 3,
      "replies": 11,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2011166389894332863",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2011126122382344242",
      "created_at": "2026-01-13 17:20:07+00:00",

    {
      "id": "2011112906994167925",
      "created_at": "2026-01-13 16:27:36+00:00",
      "text": "@shefiavi Codex specifically works with queues, most platforms don't use queues. Codex though doesn't use immediate feedback. Pi is best of both worlds.",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2011056934053466557",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "248783471",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2011070491570663577"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2011112641067172257",
      "created_at": "2026-01-13 16:26:32+00:00",
      "text": "@ShvZFR This is amazing, I am very happy about this, thank you.",
      "likes": 4,
      "retweets": 0,

    {
      "id": "2011092893709340917",
      "created_at": "2026-01-13 15:08:04+00:00",
      "text": "@badlogicgames I disagree. Just because they’re vertically integrated doesn’t mean their products are good. My usage of Claude went down 50% in 1 week.\n\nThe only reason I am using it still is habit, I prefer Pi to CC as a harness any day of the week.\n\nOpen weight models are only getting better.",
      "likes": 61,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 4,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2011037013127168407",

        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2011037013127168407"
        }
      ]
    },

      "text": "@thdxr I can push a PR for this, I tried to replicate this but the plugins can be limited: https://t.co/jodLsO9j1j\n\nHere's a recording. https://t.co/NMCv5RZRNF",

    {
      "id": "2011065180503892254",
      "created_at": "2026-01-13 13:17:57+00:00",
      "text": "What i see 10 hours a day. https://t.co/Qm3TSbUriC",
      "likes": 60,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 7,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2011065180503892254",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,

    },
    {
      "id": "2011056934053466557",
      "created_at": "2026-01-13 12:45:11+00:00",
      "text": "I have been enjoying using Pi, I like how it works with GPT-5.2/-codex specifically. \n\nIt lets you configure 2 mods, steering, and message queue. Essentially I can kick of a GPT task, and steer it during run, but also queue up at the same time. \n\nAlso the dev is great. Try it out https://t.co/o3BjpvNmB2",
      "likes": 91,
      "retweets": 6,
      "replies": 12,
      "quotes": 2,
      "conversation_id": "2011056934053466557",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2011053009082503256",
      "created_at": "2026-01-13 12:29:35+00:00",
      "text": "@MiniMax_AI can we please get a usage dashboard with subscription limits etc..",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2011053009082503256",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1875078099538423808",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2011004999795425541",
      "created_at": "2026-01-13 09:18:49+00:00",
      "text": "People underestimate the sheer will power, drive, pig-headedness it takes to make something, release it publicly, get humiliated everyday, then continue to do it again and again and again.\n\nCopying something doesn’t mean you have the grindset to make it happen lol https://t.co/qgKExNGVVh",
      "likes": 26,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2011004999795425541",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2010939055408582756"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2011003955963166926",
      "created_at": "2026-01-13 09:14:40+00:00",
      "text": "Very good community it’s taught me a lot, also lots of high quality people. \n\nWould recommend, it’s pinned on my profile. If you enjoy weird technical rabbit holes https://t.co/F9AxshLLVN",
      "likes": 44,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2011003955963166926",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2010980589763965077"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2010815160638689612",
      "created_at": "2026-01-12 20:44:28+00:00",
      "text": "@itsjustmarky Always happens a month before a release always on a 3 month cycle",

    {
      "id": "2010744773326389314",
      "created_at": "2026-01-12 16:04:46+00:00",
      "text": "When life gives you lemons, spend all your money to make lemonade. https://t.co/59gnS6EHQY",
      "likes": 16,
      "retweets": 1,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,

    },
    {
      "id": "2010743278715523474",
      "created_at": "2026-01-12 15:58:50+00:00",
      "text": "Starting working on community features and a more productive build of vllm-studio. \n\nI need some people with GPUs to help me get this dialed in, I need to be able to test all the sharing, auto-detections, docker stuff etc.. \n\nIf you're interested lmk https://t.co/ghdAJZc8WP",
      "likes": 39,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 14,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2010743278715523474",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2010728690582426043",
      "created_at": "2026-01-12 15:00:52+00:00",
      "text": "Whenever slopus becomes stupid you know a new Claude model is in the oven.",
      "likes": 434,
      "retweets": 5,
      "replies": 25,
      "quotes": 5,
      "conversation_id": "2010728690582426043",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2010702031867019671",
      "created_at": "2026-01-12 13:14:56+00:00",
      "text": "@davezmore Anything outside of coding will cause it to collapse into repeating itself. I didn't manage to get all experts activated during pruning so this needs to be repruned which I am doing now.",
      "likes": 5,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2010664456565735912",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "3317592280",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2010700420142756166"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2010664456565735912",
      "created_at": "2026-01-12 10:45:37+00:00",
      "text": "I made a configuration mistake with MiniMax-M2.1 and the REAP was botched it just didn't work outside of code. \n\nOver extensive testing and usage GLM-4.7-Reap I believe is good quality.\n\nhttps://t.co/1eWM5d26By\n\nDusting off Prime Intellect and redoing this REAP with my own funds. https://t.co/DszHlbWaXg",
      "likes": 75,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 7,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2010664456565735912",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2010620905505067059",
      "created_at": "2026-01-12 07:52:34+00:00",
      "text": "@ErvistheGreat Basically",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2010026812139811183",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "193274404",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2010619978953720177"
        }
      ]
    },
    {

    {
      "id": "2010370744140656694",
      "created_at": "2026-01-11 15:18:30+00:00",
      "text": "This set of prunes I put out has some issues:\n\n1.  of these, 3 have some full on bugs in the weights that I didn't test. It's fixable without much issue, as reported here: https://t.co/qGj3zdSHdM\n2. the other 2 don't have the issue above, but people who've tried it reported it's",
      "likes": 20,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2010370744140656694",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

    {
      "id": "2010305031980109954",
      "created_at": "2026-01-11 10:57:23+00:00",
      "text": "@alakazam1314 Yes but you can use a larger general agent for complex stuff, the future we are going to is subagents doing most of the repetitive shit.",
      "likes": 10,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2010296695406395851",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1859836776",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2010303351653904639"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2010296695406395851",
      "created_at": "2026-01-11 10:24:16+00:00",
      "text": "Here's the dumbest engineering idea to get GLM-4.7 at 32GB VRAM \n\nMake skills out of all your most successful AI chats. \nStore the sessions under that skill, link every chat that uses that skill.  \n\nYou can also do this to refine and personalize small models. If you're using this https://t.co/HHL4SnONx6",
      "likes": 336,
      "retweets": 16,
      "replies": 19,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2010296695406395851",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2010294494038790543",
      "created_at": "2026-01-11 10:15:31+00:00",
      "text": "@drummatick https://t.co/iE6jZntRXu",
      "likes": 3,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2010294042886812048",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "772514015318794240",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2008910716950946063"
        },
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2010294042886812048"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2010292153566839209",
      "created_at": "2026-01-11 10:06:13+00:00",
      "text": "It’s that for all your subscriptions, auto updating and very visually appealing.\n\nUnless of course you only use 1 subscription",
      "likes": 2,

        }
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "2010066650306097648",
      "created_at": "2026-01-10 19:10:09+00:00",
      "text": "@Everlier I’m building a hugging face integration + recipe sharing.\n\nAll the configs are stored in recipes in the db so you can add recipes under certain models and other people can see them.",
      "likes": 2,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2010010242910982239",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "133797694",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2010065882656190570"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2010066126068146574",
      "created_at": "2026-01-10 19:08:04+00:00",
      "text": "@LLMJunky Not really this is scanning everything from when I started using these things until now. \n\nIt hallucinated a lot of the numbers BUT it’s generally accurate in what it’s saying.",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2010026812139811183",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "2004675530935926784",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2010062247285100909"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2010064874576196016",
      "created_at": "2026-01-10 19:03:05+00:00",
      "text": "@Everlier This is not an auto config, it’s a config registry system with a backend controller that unloads and loads models request in /chat/completions.\n\nEssentially if I call GLM-4.7 it’ll unload whatever is in vram then load whatever recipe is associated with the id",
      "likes": 4,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2010010242910982239",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "133797694",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2010064157161193846"
        }
      ]
    },
    {

    {
      "id": "2010026812139811183",
      "created_at": "2026-01-10 16:31:51+00:00",
      "text": "I rag all my AI/agent chat history and have conversations with it now, I actually got this advice from somebody at Anthropic. \n\nGood dude https://t.co/4UFFHBFLzp",
      "likes": 1847,
      "retweets": 69,
      "replies": 73,
      "quotes": 8,
      "conversation_id": "2010026812139811183",

    },
    {
      "id": "2010022161050939507",
      "created_at": "2026-01-10 16:13:22+00:00",
      "text": "Here's something that I have benefited from a lot. \nTrack both your Claude and Codex usage directly in your mac's status bar \n\nI think he added minimax too. \n\nReally helps switch between models for various tasks if you know you only have x of Claude left\nhttps://t.co/ShYM0koePF https://t.co/WS0np9EzR1",
      "likes": 53,
      "retweets": 4,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2010022161050939507",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

      "id": "2010010242910982239",
      "created_at": "2026-01-10 15:26:00+00:00",
      "text": "Added transcription support. works with anything but i am personally using whisper-large-v3 \n\nLooking for a co-founder or sponsor or something, I really feel this project has the most potential to blow up given how much of a pain working with vllm/sglang is. https://t.co/ZeZmF8aGfH",

    },

    {
      "id": "2009954424106451167",
      "created_at": "2026-01-10 11:44:12+00:00",
      "text": "Hugging face sponsored job credits. \n\nI am going on another run, this time I will make a script to fine-tune a model on all your AI chat data &amp; history. \n\nYou'll be able to get something super personalized for 100-200$ \n\n1 call setup: \n\n- finds all your ai chat data and organizes https://t.co/xi6gGry7CP",
      "likes": 31,

      "created_at": "2026-01-10 07:30:25+00:00",
      "text": "I really think vllm-studio is coming along, I need some help though I need people to help me make it work on other people's setups. \n\nRight now i am just building for myself, I do think this would be a top open-source project if someone wanted to help me get it there. https://t.co/nl0RCG8Mnp",

      "text": "@Rajath_DB I’m not measuring the quality of the improvements yet I’m trying to make sure a few things are true:\n\n1. System must remain alive without outside influence\n2. The repo itself must be usable between runs without debugging\n3. The system must follow the rules (branching, workspaces,",

      "id": "2009880848615542977",
      "created_at": "2026-01-10 06:51:50+00:00",
      "text": "@LLMJunky You should use the GLM-4.7-reap-50-W4A16 that fits on 90~gb and with Q8 kV cache you can maybe squeeze at 100k context if you drop to q4 kvcache with sglang, exllamav3 or llama.cpp you can squeeze out 200k",

      "retweets": 0,

    {
      "id": "2009714079217397810",
      "created_at": "2026-01-09 19:49:09+00:00",
      "text": "Orchestra is evolving to be my control panel for agent orchestration. \n\n- I added the taskboard concept. \n- Infinite Orchestra has been running \"self-improving\" in docker for 48 hours\n- I setup a skill system with nested modular skills that are composable to get desired context https://t.co/gv6SU5Nj2u",
      "likes": 90,
      "retweets": 4,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2009714079217397810",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

    {
      "id": "2009661865358729647",
      "created_at": "2026-01-09 16:21:41+00:00",
      "text": "@drmhse it is windsurf which is vscode",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2009625076140831180",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1878666073429409792",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,

        {
          "type": "replied_to",

        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2009651404047106420",
      "created_at": "2026-01-09 15:40:06+00:00",
      "text": "Let me make your life better, regardless if you code or not this has been huge for me.\n\n1. Setup vscode/fork \n2. Install Project Manager + Peacock extensions\n3. Create a workspace for groups of repos/directories \n4. Save. then click to open \n5. Peacock will open a new window or https://t.co/0DlzoDASIO",
      "likes": 335,
      "retweets": 23,
      "replies": 10,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2009651404047106420",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2009625076140831180",
      "created_at": "2026-01-09 13:55:29+00:00",
      "text": "What the fuck is wrong with VSCode https://t.co/mwT1qwlUre",
      "likes": 7,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2009625076140831180",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2009619341227307484",
      "created_at": "2026-01-09 13:32:42+00:00",
      "text": "Parchi browser extension doing some heavy lifting. https://t.co/9HFedz8nfX",
      "likes": 6,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2009619341227307484",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2009614892840694212",
      "created_at": "2026-01-09 13:15:01+00:00",
      "text": "I was saying this 3 months ago, I mentioned they will crack down on using CC with other models, but also using Claude in other harnesses. \n\nAnthropic does not want people to freely use the tools they make, not reserving any moral judgement but we should all make sure to hedge https://t.co/DgUhluAUH2",
      "likes": 61,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 9,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2009614892840694212",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "1964708661214990767"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2009589006364037495",
      "created_at": "2026-01-09 11:32:10+00:00",
      "text": "We will all manage a gang of infinite digital monkeys. https://t.co/TLKJZ2H8wD",
      "likes": 12,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2009589006364037495",

      "id": "2009245366924066965",
      "created_at": "2026-01-08 12:46:40+00:00",
      "text": "@trq212 \n\nI love the CLI extension but it doesn't let me switch models before sending the first message, and it doesn't show the model that's currently selected anywhere. \n\nI know this could be design preference but it gets in the way of being able to control my token usage. https://t.co/Z0bOQxpFXw",
      "likes": 3,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2009245366924066965",

    {
      "id": "2009234779682865644",
      "created_at": "2026-01-08 12:04:35+00:00",
      "text": "@TomasMann1878 It does\n\nSurprisingly GPT-5.2 is the only model I've tried that seems to be just as good 90% into it's context window. \n\nVery good models, hard to work with though. You can't steer them mid run, you need to plan long tasks.",
      "likes": 3,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2009206016366530956",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1929678029841932288",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2009234433342091716"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2009230923183915022",
      "created_at": "2026-01-08 11:49:16+00:00",
      "text": "@Nemmax_ I just published the npm package sorry it was just on GitHub try again",
      "likes": 0,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,

    {
      "id": "2009229630851498384",
      "created_at": "2026-01-08 11:44:08+00:00",
      "text": "Reading 1 research paper a day in Azul until I prefer this browser to my real browser (:\n\n----------------------------\n\nToday, REAP\n\nRouter-weighted Expert Activation Pruning (REAP) is a way to take HUGE models then Prune them down based on observed expert activations. \n\nThis can https://t.co/CdmOJRbnxr",
      "likes": 46,
      "retweets": 3,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2009229630851498384",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2009227313121227118",
      "created_at": "2026-01-08 11:34:55+00:00",
      "text": "@KingBreton78876 I can do coaching for 100$ ph",
      "likes": 1,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2009204399172727244",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1977756233349152768",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2009225417257779660"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2009206016366530956",
      "created_at": "2026-01-08 10:10:18+00:00",
      "text": "This workflow has been running since 7:30AM, it's been nearly 4 hours, Codex is still churning. https://t.co/LFhwTwKMvs",
      "likes": 40,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 4,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2009206016366530956",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2009204399172727244",
      "created_at": "2026-01-08 10:03:52+00:00",
      "text": "This is my job. \n\nI live between the LLMs and the Business Environment, my job is to take what is required, translate it into workflows, inject them into LLMs, validate the results. \n\nAny innovation that happens on my end is: \n\n1. Learning how NEW trends work\n2. Teaching others https://t.co/b0ywhAO1P0",
      "likes": 79,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 6,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2009204399172727244",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

      "text": "RT @PrimeIntellect: https://t.co/exnaFxt0kE",
      "likes": 0,

    {
      "id": "2008988542966305145",
      "created_at": "2026-01-07 19:46:08+00:00",
      "text": "There you go, open source.\n\nIf you want to get into running parallel agents and experimenting with long running tasks, I recommend git worktrees.\n\nThis is built into cursor but now you can get it in Opencode! \n\nYour model can spin up worktrees and assign subagents to work on them https://t.co/m3kWdmoRgG",
      "likes": 241,
      "retweets": 11,
      "replies": 14,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2008988542966305145",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "quoted",
          "id": "2008863623465386059"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "2008949074272374805",
      "created_at": "2026-01-07 17:09:18+00:00",
      "text": "In this video I break down how I use MiniMax-M2.1 locally, and how I GPT-5.2 + Codex. \n\nhttps://t.co/enKqLFPLsx\n\nI have 2 very contrasting workflows with both, I think watching the end specifically for my Codex &amp; GLM workflow will be useful.",
      "likes": 45,
      "retweets": 5,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2008949074272374805",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

      "created_at": "2026-01-07 12:56:58+00:00",
      "text": "@bruce_x_offi minimax_m2_append_think for the reasoning parser.",
      "likes": 3,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 1,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2008865301371462038",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1341331548566654977",

    {
      "id": "2008885500858221021",
      "created_at": "2026-01-07 12:56:41+00:00",
      "text": "@bruce_x_offi CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0,1,2,3,4,5,6,7 \\ vllm serve /mnt/llm_models/MiniMax-M2.1-AWQ-4bit \\   --tensor-parallel-size 8 \\   --max-model-len 196608 \\   --max-num-seqs 8 \\   --gpu-memory-utilization 0.8 \\   --kv-cache-dtype fp8 \\   --reasoning-parser minimax_m2 \\   --tool-call-parser https://t.co/zGeG5cZ5f9",
      "likes": 5,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 2,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2008865301371462038",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": "1843636086485966848",
      "is_reply": true,
      "is_retweet": false,
      "referenced_tweets": [
        {
          "type": "replied_to",
          "id": "2008884116134236351"
        }
      ]
    },

    {
      "id": "2008866486224609364",
      "created_at": "2026-01-07 11:41:07+00:00",
      "text": "I wasn't aware you get this much for the GPT-Pro sub. \n\nI have been running it 18 hours a day. https://t.co/WF9AcvspjT",
      "likes": 185,
      "retweets": 6,
      "replies": 25,

    {
      "id": "2008863623465386059",
      "created_at": "2026-01-07 11:29:45+00:00",
      "text": "Open Trees. \n\nAn open-code plugin for using git-worktrees (: \n\nThis is a must if you want to try and scale to parallel agents, have each one own it's worktree. \n\nThen cherry pick what you'd like without destroying your main branch. https://t.co/cKkMuDPHk7",
      "likes": 80,
      "retweets": 5,
      "replies": 3,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2008863623465386059",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },
    {
      "id": "2008861862302224455",
      "created_at": "2026-01-07 11:22:45+00:00",
      "text": "I love him who laboureth and inventeth, that he may build the house for the superman, and prepare for him the earth, animal, and plant for thus seeketh he his own down-going. https://t.co/GuZjEGjGmz",
      "likes": 6,
      "retweets": 0,
      "replies": 0,
      "quotes": 0,
      "conversation_id": "2008861862302224455",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,

      "is_retweet": false
    },

    {
      "id": "2008822823456788927",
      "created_at": "2026-01-07 08:47:37+00:00",
      "text": "If you're going to try, go all the way. Otherwise, don't even start. This could mean losing girlfriends, wives, relatives and maybe even your mind. It could mean not eating for three or four days. It could mean freezing on a park bench. It could mean jail. It could mean derision.",
      "likes": 61,
      "retweets": 2,
      "replies": 4,
      "quotes": 1,
      "conversation_id": "2008822823456788927",
      "in_reply_to_user_id": null,
      "is_reply": false,
      "is_retweet": false
    },

      "created_at": "2026-01-06 19:33:29+00:00",
      "text": "@Aditya_T007 Claude Code is a better investment short term if you're paying for Max. If you're on 20$ I recommend GLM &amp; MiniMax, then GPT. \n\nClaude only makes sense at 200$ pm",

      "retweets": 0,
